title: Zeekr one-piece hot-stamped B-pillar + rocker sill tailored blank
description: Replace 4-piece welded B-pillar (3× 590MPa stampings + 1 reinforcement, 14 spot welds) with a single laser-welded tailored blank of 22MnB5 boron steel hot-stamped at 1500MPa, varying 1.2mm in the deformation zone to 1.8mm at the roof node. Eliminates 14 weld joints, 2 stamping dies (NRC −£0.94M) and 0.9kg/vehicle. Part cost drops from £19.9 to £14.5 (£5.4/veh), labour −1.4 min. Mirrors Zeekr 001 BIW.

ideaData.title: Zeekr one-piece hot-stamped B-pillar + rocker sill tailored blank
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline B-pillar is a 4-part assembly: three CR340LA/590MPa cold stampings plus a separate reinforcement, joined by 14 resistance spot welds across two flange rows. The proposal replaces it with one laser-welded tailored blank of 22MnB5 boron steel, austenitised at ~900C and press-hardened to 1500MPa UTS in a cooled die. Blank thickness steps from 1.2mm in the lower deformation zone to 1.8mm at the roof node, tuning the intrusion vs energy-absorption split. Hot stamping locks martensitic strength and near-zero springback in a single press stroke, deleting 14 welds, two cold-stamp dies and the reinforcement, and shedding 0.9kg per vehicle.
ideaData.materialGrade: 22MnB5 / 1500 MPa press-hardened boron steel (tailor-welded 1.2-1.8mm)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates a multi-station cold line plus a spot-weld cell into one hot-stamp press with integrated laser blank-welding. Deletes 14 spot welds, two stamping dies and a fixture; labour -1.4 min/veh. Requires a press-hardening furnace and laser blank seam; trims a robot weld station from the bodyside framing line.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Part consolidation plus hot-stamp down-gauging cuts joining labour, tooling and mass simultaneously.

ideaData.riskNotes: Press-hardened 22MnB5 needs hydrogen-embrittlement control and post-hot-stamp shot-blast descaling; tailor-weld line position must be CAE-validated against Euro NCAP side-pole load. Blank-weld undercut and soft-zone scatter require seam inspection. Coating choice (AlSi vs uncoated+blast) affects e-coat adhesion.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Zeekr 001 BIW one-piece hot-stamped B-pillar/rocker, 2022-2023; confirmed in 2023 teardown

evidenceSources.title: Zeekr 001 BIW teardown - hot-stamped tailored-blank B-pillar
Press-hardened steel tailor-welded blanks in EV body structures

title: Audi roll-formed 1500MPa rocker reinforcement
description: Swap a deep-drawn 780MPa DP rocker reinforcement (1.6mm, 38% material scrap on blanking) for a roll-formed closed section in 1500MPa martensitic steel at 1.2mm. Roll forming yields 97% material utilisation vs 62%, saving 1.1kg and £4.1/veh in raw coil. Tooling NRC for roll set is £204k vs £1.19M draw die. Section modulus rises 18% for side-impact. Benchmarks Audi Q6 e-tron sill.

ideaData.title: Audi roll-formed 1500MPa rocker reinforcement
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline rocker reinforcement is deep-drawn from 1.6mm DP780 dual-phase steel, with severe blanking loss (~38% scrap) and a costly draw die. The change roll-forms a closed-section rocker reinforcement from 1.2mm martensitic MS1500 (1500MPa) coil through a progressive roll-set with in-line closure welding. Roll forming is a continuous, near-net process: material utilisation rises to ~97% versus 62% for deep draw, cutting raw coil consumption and 1.1kg. The higher-strength martensitic grade at thinner gauge raises section modulus 18% for the side-impact load path while the roll set NRC (£204k) is a fraction of a £1.19M draw die.
ideaData.materialGrade: MS1500 / 1500 MPa martensitic steel (1.2mm roll-formed)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces a deep-draw press station with a continuous roll-forming line plus in-line laser/HF closure weld and sweep-bend. Material utilisation 97% vs 62%; tooling NRC £204k vs £1.19M draw die. Fixed-section profile suits high-volume single-section parts; reduced press tonnage and scrap handling.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Near-net roll forming slashes coil scrap and tooling cost while up-gauging strength.

ideaData.riskNotes: Martensitic MS1500 has limited bendability; roll-forming radii and closure-weld HAZ softening must be validated for pole-impact. Springback control across the roll set needs tuning; section is constant so any local reinforcement features must be added as separate parts. Coil camber affects sweep-bend tolerance.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Audi Q6 e-tron (PPE) rocker/sill reinforcement, 2024

evidenceSources.title: Audi PPE body structure material strategy
Roll-formed UHSS rocker reinforcements for EV side impact

ideaData.regulatoryContext: Euro NCAP side pole / UN R135

title: Nio aluminium-extrusion front crash box with crush initiators
description: Replace a 3-part welded steel crash box (590MPa, 9 spot welds) with a 6063-T6 extruded octagonal tube, 2.5mm wall, with laser-cut crush beads tuned for 180kN peak. Extrusion + sawing replaces stamping and welding, removing 9 joints and a fixture (NRC −£153k). Bolted MIG-free attach to bumper beam saves 0.7 min/veh. Net −0.8kg, piece cost £16.2 vs £19 (£2.8/veh). Mirrors Nio ET5 longitudinal.

ideaData.title: Nio aluminium-extrusion front crash box with crush initiators
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline front crash box is a 3-part welded steel assembly in CR340LA/590MPa with 9 spot welds and a dedicated weld fixture. The redesign uses a 6063-T6 aluminium extruded octagonal tube, 2.5mm wall, with laser-cut crush beads (crush initiators) tuned to fold progressively at a 180kN peak crush force. Extrusion gives a constant closed section ideal for stable axial collapse, and sawing-to-length plus laser bead-cutting replaces stamping and spot welding, removing 9 joints. A bolted attachment to the bumper beam eliminates MIG/spot joining, saving 0.7 min/veh, while the lighter alloy section nets -0.8kg and a lower piece cost.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6063-T6 aluminium extrusion (2.5mm wall, octagonal)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Swaps stamping + spot-weld cell for extrusion, saw-to-length and laser bead-cutting, deleting 9 welds and one weld fixture (NRC -£153k). Bolted attach removes joining station and -0.7 min/veh. Crush-bead geometry set by laser, enabling fast tuning without die changes.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Extruded one-piece crush tube removes welds, fixtures and mass with bolt-on serviceability.

ideaData.riskNotes: 6063-T6 has moderate crush ductility; bead pitch must be validated to avoid global buckling and to hold 180kN peak across temperature. Bolted interface needs anti-loosening and galvanic isolation from steel beam. Aluminium repair after low-speed crash differs from steel; RCAR rating must be checked.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Nio ET5 front longitudinal/crash-box, 2022

evidenceSources.title: Nio ET5 front structure teardown - extruded crash box

ideaData.regulatoryContext: RCAR low-speed crash / IIHS bumper; UN R42

title: Mercedes door-in-white aluminium-steel hybrid architecture
description: Re-architect the front door-in-white: AA6014-T4 outer panel roll-hemmed to a 590MPa steel inner ring, replacing an all-steel 6-panel assembly (22 spot welds + 2 reinforcements). Self-pierce riveting and structural adhesive cut 22 welds to 16 SPR points, removing a reinforcement bracket. Mass −2.3kg, piece cost −£9.5/veh, one stamping die deleted (NRC −£765k). Benchmarks Mercedes EQE door build.

ideaData.title: Mercedes door-in-white aluminium-steel hybrid architecture
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline front door-in-white is an all-steel 6-panel assembly with 22 spot welds plus two reinforcements. The redesign uses an AA6014-T4 (AlMgSi) aluminium outer roll-hemmed to a 590MPa (CR340LA) steel inner ring, a mixed-material door. Self-pierce riveting (SPR) combined with a continuous structural epoxy adhesive bead replaces resistance spot welding on the dissimilar Al-steel stack, cutting 22 welds to 16 SPR points and deleting one reinforcement bracket carried by the bonded ring stiffness. The aluminium outer sheds 2.3kg, one stamping die is removed, and adhesive plus SPR provide corrosion isolation between the dissimilar metals.
ideaData.materialGrade: AA6014-T4 Al outer + CR340LA 590MPa steel inner ring
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces the spot-weld door line with an SPR + adhesive-dispense cell and roll-hemming; 22 welds to 16 SPR points, one reinforcement and one stamping die deleted (NRC -£765k). Requires riveter C-frames, adhesive metering and oven cure; dissimilar-metal handling adds sealer but removes weld guns.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Mixed-material door with SPR/adhesive consolidates panels and reinforcements for mass and labour savings.

ideaData.riskNotes: Al-steel galvanic corrosion demands robust adhesive sealing and rivet coating; SPR joint strength and fatigue must be validated against side-intrusion and sag durability. Thermal-expansion mismatch can distort hem flush over paint bake. Adhesive cure window and rivet die wear need process control.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes EQE/EQS door-in-white aluminium-steel hybrid build, 2022

evidenceSources.title: SPR and adhesive joining for mixed-material doors

ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS 214 / Euro NCAP side impact; UN R95

title: Porsche hot-stamped A-pillar with tailor-rolled blank
description: Convert a 2-piece A-pillar (980MPa AHSS + insert, 11 welds) to a single tailor-rolled 22MnB5 blank hot-stamped 1500MPa, continuously varying 1.0–2.1mm to delete the local insert. Removes 11 welds and one stamping. Mass −1.5kg, piece cost £22.8 vs £26.8 (£4/veh), labour −1.2 min. Improves roof-crush FMVSS216 margin 14%. Benchmarks Porsche Macan EV upper A-pillar.

ideaData.title: Porsche hot-stamped A-pillar with tailor-rolled blank
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline A-pillar is a 2-piece part: a 980MPa AHSS stamping plus a local insert, joined by 11 welds. The proposal converts it to a single tailor-rolled blank (TRB) of 22MnB5 boron steel, press-hardened to 1500MPa, with thickness continuously rolled from 1.0mm to 2.1mm so the insert function is built into the gauge profile. Hot stamping delivers martensitic strength with minimal springback and the variable gauge places mass only where roof-crush load demands it, deleting the insert and 11 welds and one stamping. Net -1.5kg, with a 14% improvement in FMVSS 216 roof-crush margin.
ideaData.materialGrade: 22MnB5 / 1500 MPa press-hardened boron, tailor-rolled 1.0-2.1mm
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Tailor-rolling mill plus hot-stamp press replaces a cold stamping and a weld cell; deletes the local insert, 11 welds and one stamping. Labour -1.2 min/veh. Needs TRB coil supply and press-hardening furnace; continuous gauge transition removes the need for patchwork reinforcement tooling.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Continuously variable-gauge hot-stamped pillar removes inserts and welds while gaining crush margin.

ideaData.riskNotes: Tailor-rolled gauge transitions create local strain/thinning zones needing forming-limit validation; hydrogen embrittlement and quench uniformity must be controlled. TRB coil supply base is narrow (IP/sourcing risk). Roof-crush certification must re-verify the deleted insert region.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Porsche Macan EV upper A-pillar press-hardened structure, 2024

evidenceSources.title: Tailor-rolled blanks for A/B-pillar mass reduction

ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS 216 roof crush; Euro NCAP

title: Xpeng front gigacast shock-tower integration
description: Integrate front shock towers, strut housings and dash-toe nodes into a single AlSi10MnMg gigacast (3.5mm wall) replacing 38 stamped/welded parts and 210 joints. Two robot weld cells removed; BIW front-end takt −19%. Piece cost £112 vs £160 assembly (£48/veh), NRC amortised on 7000T die. Vacuum casting limits porosity for fatigue. Benchmarks Xpeng G6 front structure.

ideaData.title: Xpeng front gigacast shock-tower integration
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline front structure is 38 stamped/welded steel parts joined by ~210 spot/MIG joints, including shock towers, strut housings and dash-toe nodes. The redesign integrates them into a single high-pressure vacuum die-cast (HPDC) gigacasting in AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500-class) with a 3.5mm nominal wall, shot on a ~7000-tonne press. Vacuum-assisted casting suppresses gas porosity so the part meets fatigue requirements at the strut mounts. Consolidation removes two robot weld cells, cutting front-end body takt by 19%, and replaces a £160 welded assembly with a £112 casting (-£48/veh), the large die NRC amortised over volume.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) vacuum HPDC, 3.5mm wall
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces 38 stampings and 210 joints with one casting; deletes two robot weld cells and cuts front-end takt 19%. Requires a ~7000T gigacasting press, vacuum tooling, melt handling and CNC datum machining of bolt interfaces. Massive die NRC; reduced floor space and weld consumables.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Mega-casting collapses dozens of parts and weld cells into one shot for large per-vehicle saving.

ideaData.riskNotes: HPDC porosity/fatigue at strut mounts requires vacuum control and X-ray/CT inspection; casting is repair-limited so crash-box bolt-on zones must isolate damage. Alloy ductility and die thermal fatigue affect scrap; single-source die is a supply/IP risk and a launch single-point-of-failure.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Xpeng G6 front gigacasting (SEPA architecture), 2023
ideaData.regulatoryContext: Euro NCAP/C-NCAP frontal; UN R94

title: Volvo PA66-GF30 windscreen encapsulation injection-molded frame
description: Replace a separately extruded PVC windscreen surround plus 6 clips with an in-mould PA66-GF30 encapsulation injected directly onto the glass edge (2.5mm rib). Eliminates 6 clips, a primer step and 0.9 min manual fitment. Dimensional tolerance tightens to ±0.3mm improving flush/gap. Piece cost £6 vs £8 (£2/veh), tooling NRC £264k. Reduces wind-noise leak path. Benchmarks Volvo EX90 windscreen.

ideaData.title: Volvo PA66-GF30 windscreen encapsulation injection-molded frame
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline windscreen surround is a separately extruded PVC profile retained by six clips, needing a primer step and manual fitment. The redesign injection-moulds a PA66-GF30 (30% glass-filled polyamide) encapsulation directly onto the glass edge in-mould, forming a 2.5mm rib bonded to the glass, eliminating the six clips, the primer operation and 0.9 min of manual fitting. In-mould encapsulation holds dimensional tolerance to +/-0.3mm, improving flush/gap and tightening the wind-noise leak path. The glass-reinforced nylon gives rigidity and weather resistance while consolidating the surround into the glazing module.
ideaData.materialGrade: PA66-GF30 (30% glass-filled polyamide) in-mould encapsulation
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces an extrusion line plus clip-and-prime assembly with a glass-encapsulation injection cell; deletes six clips, the primer station and -0.9 min fitment. Tool NRC £264k. Tightens dimensional control to +/-0.3mm and integrates surround into the supplied glazing module (fewer plant parts).

costSavingPotential.qualitative: In-mould encapsulation consolidates surround, clips and primer into one glazing module.

ideaData.riskNotes: Glass-to-PA66 adhesion needs primer/plasma and validation against thermal cycling and UV; in-mould glass breakage risk and cycle time affect cost. CTE mismatch can stress the bond at temperature extremes; water-management and wind-noise must be re-verified on a flush surround.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo EX90 windscreen encapsulated glazing, 2024

evidenceSources.title: Glass encapsulation PA66-GF30 windscreen module data
Volvo EX90 glazing and NVH overview

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN R43 safety glazing; wind-noise/leak internal targets

title: Li Auto multi-material BIW side frame with laser-welded blanks
description: Re-engineer the full bodyside outer as 3 laser-welded tailored blanks (180BH skin, 340LA quarter, 1180MPa hinge zone) instead of 7 discrete stampings with 31 welds. Consolidation deletes 3 dies (NRC −£1.36M) and 18 welds, with 24% less edge scrap. Mass −3.1kg, piece cost −£11.4/veh. Class-A retained via laser-seam grinding. Benchmarks Li Auto L9 bodyside outer.

ideaData.title: Li Auto multi-material BIW side frame with laser-welded blanks
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline bodyside outer is 7 discrete cold stampings joined by 31 spot welds. The redesign forms the full bodyside outer from three laser-welded tailored blanks: a 180BH bake-hardening skin for the Class-A surface, CR340LA quarter section, and a 1180MPa CP zone at the hinge/load region, then drawn as integrated panels. Consolidation deletes three stamping dies and 18 welds while cutting edge scrap 24% by nesting the blanks before welding. The strength is placed only where the load path needs it, shedding 3.1kg, and the laser seam is ground flush to retain the Class-A appearance.
ideaData.materialGrade: Laser-welded blank: 180BH skin / CR340LA / CP1180 (1180 MPa)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: A laser blank-welding line feeds a single draw die replacing seven stampings and a 31-weld bodyside framing sequence; deletes three dies (NRC -£1.36M) and 18 welds, -24% edge scrap. Adds laser-seam grinding for Class-A; reduces panel logistics and framing-station count.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Tailor-welded bodyside places strength by zone, cutting dies, welds, scrap and mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: Laser-weld seam visible on Class-A requires grinding quality control and may telegraph after paint; mixed-grade formability (180BH vs CP1180) risks splitting at transitions. Large single-panel draw raises die-tryout and springback complexity; a defective blank-weld scraps a high-value panel.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Li Auto L9 bodyside outer tailor-welded panel, 2022

evidenceSources.title: Li Auto L9 bodyside construction teardown
Laser-welded blanks for one-piece bodyside outers

ideaData.regulatoryContext: Euro NCAP side impact; Class-A surface standards

title: BMW hydroformed roof bow cross-member
description: Replace a 2-shell welded roof bow (270MPa, 16 welds + 2 flanges) with a single hydroformed closed tube in 590MPa steel, 1.4mm wall. Hydroforming removes 16 welds, 2 stampings and the flange overlap, saving 0.8kg and improving torsional stiffness 11%. Piece cost £12.1 vs £14.7 (£2.6/veh), tool NRC £357k vs £833k for twin draw dies. Benchmarks BMW Neue Klasse roof structure.

ideaData.title: BMW hydroformed roof bow cross-member
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline roof bow is a 2-shell welded section in 270MPa mild steel with 16 spot welds and two overlapping flanges. The redesign hydroforms a single closed tube in 590MPa (CR340LA) steel at 1.4mm wall: a tube is placed in a die and internal hydraulic pressure expands it to a closed cross-member, removing 16 welds, two stampings and the flange overlap. The closed hydroformed section raises torsional stiffness 11% versus the open spot-welded twin shell, sheds 0.8kg, and the hydroform tool NRC (£357k) undercuts twin draw dies (£833k).
ideaData.materialGrade: CR340LA / 590 MPa steel, 1.4mm hydroformed tube
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces twin draw dies and a 16-weld station with a hydroforming cell (tube mill feed, bend, hydroform, end-cut). Deletes 16 welds, two stampings and flange overlap; tool NRC £357k vs £833k. Cycle time governed by hydroform pressurise/dump; reduces framing welds and improves dimensional repeatability.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Closed hydroformed tube removes welds and flanges while raising stiffness at lower tooling cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Hydroforming 590MPa needs controlled pre-bend to avoid wrinkling/bursting; end-of-tube attachment to bodyside requires accessible weld/SPR flanges. Cycle time and seal wear affect throughput; tube-mill weld-seam quality is critical to burst resistance. Closed-section spring needs CAE check.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BMW Neue Klasse roof structure cross-member, 2025

evidenceSources.title: BMW Neue Klasse body structure overview
Hydroformed closed sections in automotive roof/cross-members

ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS 216 roof crush (indirect); torsional NVH targets

title: Hongqi tailgate plastic-composite SMC outer panel
description: Substitute a steel tailgate outer (2 stampings, 12 welds) with a single compression-moulded SMC (low-density 1.3 g/cc, glass-fibre) panel bonded to a steel inner. Removes 1 stamping die (NRC −£663k) and 12 welds, dropping 2.7kg. SMC tolerates online paint at 180°C. Piece cost £18.3 vs £22 (£3.7/veh), no e-coat corrosion risk. Benchmarks Hongqi E-HS9 liftgate.

ideaData.title: Hongqi tailgate plastic-composite SMC outer panel
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline tailgate outer is two steel stampings joined by 12 spot welds. The redesign substitutes a single compression-moulded SMC (sheet moulding compound) panel - low-density 1.3 g/cc glass-fibre/polyester - bonded with structural adhesive to a steel inner. SMC compression moulding forms complex outer geometry in one shot, deleting one stamping die and 12 welds and dropping 2.7kg from the lighter composite. The low-density SMC formulation tolerates the online e-coat/paint bake at 180C without distortion and is inherently corrosion-free, removing e-coat corrosion risk at the outer panel.
ideaData.materialGrade: Low-density SMC (1.3 g/cc glass-fibre/polyester composite)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces two stampings and a 12-weld cell with an SMC compression press plus adhesive-bond fixture to the steel inner; deletes one stamping die (NRC -£663k). SMC must survive 180C e-coat bake; adds a bonding/cure station. Lower tooling cost than steel draw dies; reduced framing welds.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Lightweight SMC outer bonded to steel inner removes a die, welds and corrosion risk.

ideaData.riskNotes: SMC-to-steel CTE mismatch can cause read-through/flatness after bake; adhesive bond durability and Class-A surface (waviness, sink) need validation. Recyclability and repair of thermoset SMC are limited; paint adhesion and dimensional stability across temperature must be qualified.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Hongqi E-HS9 composite liftgate outer, 2021

evidenceSources.title: Low-density SMC for online-paint tailgate panels

ideaData.regulatoryContext: Rear-impact/closure durability; paint-shop compatibility

title: Denza co-extruded TPV glass-run channel with flocked lip
description: Replace EPDM dense-rubber glass-run channel with a co-extruded TPV (thermoplastic vulcanizate) profile, dense carrier + foamed sealing lip, flocked contact face. TPV is fully recyclable and extrudes 30% faster with no sulfur curing oven, cutting energy and scrap. Mass −0.4kg/door set, piece cost £3.7 vs £4.8 (£1.1/veh across 4 doors). Lower glass-drop friction. Benchmarks Denza N7 door glass run.

ideaData.title: Denza co-extruded TPV glass-run channel with flocked lip
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline glass-run channel is dense EPDM rubber, sulfur-vulcanised in a curing oven. The redesign co-extrudes a TPV (thermoplastic vulcanizate, Santoprene-class) profile with a dense carrier and a foamed sealing lip, with a flocked contact face for low-friction glass travel. TPV is melt-processable and fully recyclable, extruding ~30% faster with no sulfur curing oven, cutting energy and scrap. The foamed lip lowers glass-drop friction and the profile sheds 0.4kg per door set. Piece cost falls from £4.8 to £3.7 across four doors.
ideaData.materialGrade: TPV (thermoplastic vulcanizate) co-extrusion with flocked lip
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces sulfur-cured EPDM extrusion + vulcanising oven with a co-extrusion line (dense carrier + foamed lip) and flocking; ~30% faster line speed, no curing oven (energy/scrap down). Recyclable scrap reground. Same install clips; reduces seal-shop footprint and cure-time WIP.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: TPV co-extrusion removes the vulcanising oven and enables recyclable, faster, lighter glass runs.

ideaData.riskNotes: TPV compression-set and high-temperature creep are weaker than EPDM; long-term sealing and glass-guidance retention need durability/heat-aging validation. Flock adhesion and abrasion over cycle life must be qualified. Low-temp flexibility and UV resistance require grade selection.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Denza N7 door glass-run TPV sealing, 2023

evidenceSources.title: TPV glass-run channel material and process data

title: AITO rear floor-pan single-shot stamped deep-draw consolidation
description: Combine a 4-piece rear floor pan (270MPa, 26 welds, 2 spare-well inserts) into one deep-drawn 340LA panel with integrated spare well and seat-mount embossments. Deletes 3 stampings and 18 welds, saving 1.9kg of flange overlap. Piece cost £15.9 vs £19.6 (£3.7/veh), tool NRC £0.94M consolidates 4 dies into 1 progressive line. Benchmarks AITO M7 rear floor.

ideaData.title: AITO rear floor-pan single-shot stamped deep-draw consolidation
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline rear floor pan is a 4-piece assembly in 270MPa mild steel with 26 spot welds and two spare-well inserts. The redesign deep-draws a single CR340LA panel with the spare well and seat-mount embossments formed in, deleting three stampings and 18 welds and removing 1.9kg of flange overlap. A larger progressive deep-draw die consolidates four dies into one line (NRC £0.94M), and the embossed features replace welded inserts. The 340LA grade gives the formability for the deep spare-well draw while supporting seat-mount loads.
ideaData.materialGrade: CR340LA / 340 MPa HSLA steel, deep-drawn single pan
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates four stampings and a 26-weld cell into one deep-draw die line with integrated spare well and seat embossments; deletes three dies (NRC £0.94M net consolidation), 18 welds and 1.9kg overlap. Larger press tonnage/blank; fewer framing welds and panel logistics.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One deep-drawn pan absorbs inserts and sub-panels, cutting welds, mass and die count.

ideaData.riskNotes: Deep draw of an integrated spare well risks thinning/splitting in 340LA; forming-limit and springback need die tryout. Large single panel raises scrap value if rejected and demands higher-tonnage press availability. Seat-anchor pull strength on embossed (non-reinforced) mounts must be validated.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: AITO M7 rear floor pan single-shot stamping, 2022

evidenceSources.title: Deep-draw consolidation of rear floor pans

ideaData.regulatoryContext: ECE R14 seat-belt anchorage; rear-impact

title: AVATR cast-aluminium rear gigacast underbody
description: Single AlSi10MnMg rear underbody gigacast (vacuum HPDC, 3.2mm wall) replacing 79 parts and 280 spot/MIG joints including rear rails and damper mounts. Removes 2 weld cells, cutting takt 21% and 16kg flange mass. Piece cost £122 vs £175 assembly (£53/veh); 8000T press die NRC amortised over 6 yr. Localised heat-treat-free alloy avoids quench distortion. Benchmarks AVATR 12 rear body.

ideaData.title: AVATR cast-aluminium rear gigacast underbody
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline rear underbody is 79 parts joined by ~280 spot/MIG joints, including rear rails and damper mounts. The redesign casts it as a single AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) vacuum HPDC gigacasting at 3.2mm wall on an ~8000-tonne press. Vacuum-assisted casting controls porosity for fatigue-critical damper mounts, and the heat-treat-free alloy variant avoids quench distortion, allowing tight as-cast tolerances. Consolidation removes two weld cells, cutting body takt 21% and shedding 16kg of flange overlap. A £175 welded assembly becomes a £122 casting (-£53/veh), the press die amortised over six years.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) heat-treat-free vacuum HPDC, 3.2mm
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces 79 parts and ~280 joints with one casting; deletes two weld cells, cuts takt 21% and 16kg flange mass. Requires ~8000T press, vacuum die, melt/dosing and CNC datum machining. Heat-treat-free alloy skips a furnace step and quench distortion; very high die NRC amortised over 6 yr.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Rear mega-casting collapses ~79 parts and two weld cells into one shot.

ideaData.riskNotes: Fatigue at damper mounts demands vacuum-porosity control and CT inspection; casting is repair-limited so collision damage may total the underbody. Single die is an IP/launch single-point-of-failure; alloy ductility for crash and scrap rate drive economics.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: AVATR 12 rear-underbody gigacasting, 2023
ideaData.regulatoryContext: C-NCAP/Euro NCAP rear impact; UN R34

title: Maextro 1180MPa cold-stamped dash cross-member
description: Upgrade the dash (cowl) cross-member from 590MPa DP at 1.8mm to 1180MPa CP steel at 1.2mm cold-stamped. Down-gauging saves 1.3kg and £4.3/veh raw steel while holding bending stiffness for steering-column mount. Existing dies reused with new draw beads (NRC £136k). Removes one reinforcement patch (−4 welds). Benchmarks Maextro S800 cowl cross-car beam.

ideaData.title: Maextro 1180MPa cold-stamped dash cross-member
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline dash (cowl) cross-member is DP590 dual-phase steel at 1.8mm with a separate reinforcement patch. The redesign up-grades to CP1180 (1180MPa complex-phase) cold-stamped at 1.2mm, down-gauging while holding bending stiffness for the steering-column mount. The higher-strength CP grade lets thickness drop 0.6mm, saving 1.3kg and £4.3/veh of raw steel, and the reinforcement patch is deleted (-4 welds) because the base material now carries the load. Existing dies are reused with new draw beads to manage the higher springback of CP1180 (NRC £136k).
ideaData.materialGrade: CP1180 / 1180 MPa complex-phase steel, 1.2mm cold-stamped
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Re-uses existing draw dies with new draw beads (NRC £136k) to manage CP1180 springback; deletes one reinforcement patch and 4 welds. Higher press tonnage for the stronger grade; same line and station count, so impact is mostly material and minor tooling rework.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Grade up / gauge down deletes a reinforcement and steel mass on the cowl beam.

ideaData.riskNotes: CP1180 springback and reduced formability at 1.2mm risk dimensional scatter at the column mount; bending-stiffness and modal targets must be CAE/test-confirmed without the patch. Edge-stretch and hole-expansion limits constrain features; die-bead rework must be validated for the harder grade.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Maextro S800 cowl/cross-car beam structure, 2024
ideaData.regulatoryContext: Steering-column/NVH targets; UN R12 (indirect)

title: Luxeed two-piece-to-one roll-formed roof rail
description: Convert a stamped 2-shell roof side rail (440MPa, 20 welds) to a roll-formed closed profile in 980MPa AHSS at 1.4mm. Roll forming reaches 95% utilisation vs 58%, saving £4.8/veh coil and 1.4kg. Two draw dies deleted (NRC −£1.1M), 20 welds cut to 8 closure welds. Improves roof-crush 16%. Benchmarks Luxeed S7 roof rail.

ideaData.title: Luxeed two-piece-to-one roll-formed roof rail
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline roof side rail is a stamped 2-shell section in 440MPa steel with 20 spot welds. The redesign roll-forms a closed profile from 980MPa AHSS at 1.4mm through a roll set with in-line closure welding. Roll forming reaches 95% material utilisation versus 58% for the twin-shell stampings, saving £4.8/veh of coil and 1.4kg, and deletes two draw dies (NRC -£1.1M). The 20 spot welds drop to 8 closure welds, and the high-strength closed section improves roof-crush performance 16%.
ideaData.materialGrade: 980 MPa AHSS (DP980-class), 1.4mm roll-formed closed rail
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces two draw dies and a 20-weld station with a continuous roll-forming line + closure weld + sweep-bend; utilisation 95% vs 58%, two dies deleted (NRC -£1.1M), welds 20 to 8. Constant section limits local features; lower press tonnage and scrap, fewer framing welds.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Roll-formed closed roof rail boosts utilisation and crush while cutting dies and welds.

ideaData.riskNotes: DP980 bend radii and closure-weld HAZ softening must be validated for roof-crush; constant section forces separate brackets for attachments. Sweep-bend springback and coil camber affect roof-line fit; closure-weld integrity is crash-critical.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Luxeed S7 roof side rail roll-formed structure, 2023
ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS 216 roof crush; Euro NCAP

title: Yangwang adhesive-bonded panoramic glass roof module
description: Replace a steel roof panel + bolted glass sunroof cassette with a structural-bonded fixed panoramic laminated glass (4.0mm) directly on the roof ring via PU bead, deleting the cassette frame, 14 bolts and a seal cartridge. Saves 5.2kg and one stamping die (NRC −£697k). Piece cost £63 vs £82 (£19/veh) net of glass premium. Tightens flush ±0.4mm. Benchmarks Yangwang U8 glass roof.

ideaData.title: Yangwang adhesive-bonded panoramic glass roof module
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline roof is a steel roof panel plus a bolted glass sunroof cassette with its own frame, 14 bolts and a seal cartridge. The redesign structurally bonds a fixed panoramic laminated glass (4.0mm, PVB-interlayer laminate) directly to the roof ring with a polyurethane (PU) adhesive bead, deleting the cassette frame, 14 bolts and the seal cartridge. The bonded glass becomes a stressed roof member, saving 5.2kg and one stamping die (NRC -£697k). Direct bonding tightens flush to +/-0.4mm; net piece cost £63 vs £82 (-£19/veh) after the glass premium.
ideaData.materialGrade: Laminated PVB safety glass, 4.0mm, PU structural bond
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces a bolted cassette + roof stamping with a PU direct-glaze cell bonding glass to the roof ring; deletes the cassette frame, 14 bolts, a seal cartridge and one stamping die (NRC -£697k). Adds primer/robot-bead glaze and cure; reduces fastening labour and sunroof sub-assembly logistics.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Structural-bonded fixed glass roof deletes the cassette, fasteners and a stamping.

ideaData.riskNotes: Bonded glass becomes structural - PU bond durability, peel and rollover (FMVSS 216a) load transfer must be validated; glass breakage repair means full re-glaze. Thermal load/solar gain and acoustic comfort need management; primer/surface-prep control is critical to bond reliability.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Yangwang U8 fixed panoramic glass roof, 2023

title: BMW SPR-joined aluminium bonnet inner with reduced ribs
description: Re-design the bonnet inner from a deep-drawn AA5182 single panel (heavy rib pattern) to an optimised AA6016-T4 hexagonal-rib inner self-pierce-riveted to outer with structural adhesive, replacing 18 hem-bonded mastic pads. Topology cut removes 1.1kg while meeting pedestrian head-impact GTR9. Piece cost −£5.9/veh, 18 mastic stations deleted (−1.6 min). Benchmarks BMW i5 bonnet.

ideaData.title: BMW SPR-joined aluminium bonnet inner with reduced ribs
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline bonnet inner is a deep-drawn AA5182-O aluminium single panel with a heavy rib pattern, hem-bonded to the outer with 18 mastic pads. The redesign uses an optimised AA6016-T4 inner with a topology-derived hexagonal rib pattern, self-pierce-riveted to the outer with a continuous structural adhesive bead, replacing the 18 mastic pads. The hexagonal ribbing meets pedestrian head-impact (GTR9) energy management while removing 1.1kg, and SPR + adhesive replaces the mastic stations, saving 1.6 min and £5.9/veh. The 6016-T4 grade improves dent resistance and bake-hardening response.
ideaData.materialGrade: AA6016-T4 inner / AA5182-O baseline; SPR + structural adhesive
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Topology-optimised inner deletes 1.1kg; SPR + adhesive bead replaces 18 mastic-pad stations (-1.6 min/veh). Requires riveter and adhesive-dispense in the hood-hemming cell; hexagonal rib die replaces the heavy-rib draw die. Fewer manual mastic operations and improved hem-line repeatability.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Topology rib + SPR/adhesive sheds mass and removes mastic-pad labour on the bonnet.

ideaData.riskNotes: Hexagonal-rib stiffness must meet pedestrian head-impact (GTR9) HIC while preserving dent/oil-canning resistance; SPR into thin aluminium needs joint-strength and corrosion validation. Adhesive cure and read-through onto the Class-A outer must be controlled; 6016-T4 forming limits constrain rib depth.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BMW i5 aluminium bonnet construction, 2023
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN GTR9 / UN R127 pedestrian protection

title: Zeekr integrated front-rail-to-shock-tower gigacast
description: Cast front longitudinal rails, shock towers and plenum lower into one AlSi10MnMg gigacast (3.4mm wall) replacing 36 stamped parts and 190 joints. Deletes 2 weld stations, dropping front-end takt 18% and 14kg. Piece cost £109 vs £155 (£46/veh); 7200T die NRC over 6 yr. Local annealing zones aid bolt-on crash-box repairability. Benchmarks Zeekr 007 front casting.

ideaData.title: Zeekr integrated front-rail-to-shock-tower gigacast
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline front end is 36 stamped parts joined by ~190 spot welds, including front longitudinal rails, shock towers and plenum lower. The redesign casts them as one AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) gigacasting at 3.4mm wall on a ~7200-tonne press. Consolidation deletes two weld stations, dropping front-end takt 18% and 14kg. Local annealing zones (soft zones) are induced in the casting to keep the bolt-on crash-box interface repairable after low-speed impact. A £155 welded assembly becomes a £109 casting (-£46/veh), the die NRC amortised over six years.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) HPDC, 3.4mm, local soft zones
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces 36 stampings and ~190 joints with one casting; deletes two weld stations, takt -18%, -14kg. Requires ~7200T press, vacuum die, melt handling and datum machining. Local annealing of bolt-on zones adds a heat step but preserves crash-box repairability; major die NRC over 6 yr.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Front mega-casting consolidates rails, towers and plenum with repairable bolt-on crash boxes.

ideaData.riskNotes: Porosity/fatigue at strut and rail nodes need vacuum control and CT; localised annealing process window must be validated for both repairability and crash load path. Single die is a launch/IP single-point risk; casting damage in moderate crashes may force large-part replacement.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Zeekr 007 front gigacasting, 2023

title: Mercedes laser-brazed roof-to-bodyside zero-gap joint
description: Replace a spot-welded roof ditch with applied moulding by a continuous CuSi3 laser-brazed roof-to-bodyside seam, eliminating the roof-ditch moulding, 2 clips and a sealant bead. Removes a 0.6m moulding part (£3.2) and 0.9 min fitment. Brazing speed 4 m/min reduces flange width 6mm, saving 0.5kg. Net −£3.9/veh. Improves Class-A appearance. Benchmarks Mercedes C-Class roof braze.

ideaData.title: Mercedes laser-brazed roof-to-bodyside zero-gap joint
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline roof-to-bodyside joint is a spot-welded ditch covered by an applied roof-ditch moulding, with two clips and a sealant bead. The redesign uses a continuous CuSi3 (copper-silicon) laser-brazed seam joining roof to bodyside, eliminating the moulding, two clips and the sealant. Laser brazing at ~4 m/min lays a smooth filler fillet that also seals the joint, allowing the flange width to shrink 6mm and saving 0.5kg. Deleting the 0.6m moulding (£3.2) and 0.9 min of fitment nets -£3.9/veh while improving the Class-A roof-line appearance with a visible brazed seam.
ideaData.materialGrade: CuSi3 (copper-silicon) braze filler; steel roof/bodyside
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces spot-weld + applied moulding with a continuous laser-braze cell (CuSi3 wire, ~4 m/min); deletes the roof-ditch moulding, two clips, a sealant bead and -0.9 min fitment, narrowing flange 6mm. Adds laser-braze head and seam quality control; removes moulding logistics and a Class-A trim part.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Laser brazing seals and finishes the roof joint, deleting the moulding and clips.

ideaData.riskNotes: Brazed seam is visible Class-A: porosity, spatter and colour after paint must be controlled; gap fit-up tolerance (zero-gap) demands tight roof/bodyside dimensional management. CuSi3 wire feed stability and seam corrosion sealing need validation; rework of a defective braze is difficult.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes C-Class laser-brazed roof joint, 2021

evidenceSources.title: Mercedes C-Class roof-to-bodyside laser braze teardown
CuSi3 laser brazing for visible roof seams

title: BYD CTB integrated floor-as-battery-lid structure
description: Adopt cell-to-body where the battery upper cover IS the vehicle floor pan, deleting a separate stamped floor (4 parts, 40 welds) and a pack lid. Honeycomb-sandwich blade-cell top sheet bonds to sills via structural adhesive. Removes 1 stamping line (NRC −£1.19M), 12kg and 3.5 min sealing labour. Net −£26/veh. Raises torsional stiffness 30%. Benchmarks BYD Seal CTB floor.

ideaData.title: BYD CTB integrated floor-as-battery-lid structure
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline uses a separate stamped floor pan (4 parts, 40 welds) plus a separate battery pack lid. The redesign adopts cell-to-body (CTB), where the battery upper cover IS the vehicle floor pan: the honeycomb-sandwich top sheet over BYD Blade LFP cells bonds to the rockers/sills with structural adhesive, deleting a stamped floor and a pack lid. This removes one stamping line (NRC -£1.19M), 12kg and 3.5 min of sealing labour, and raises torsional stiffness 30% by turning the pack into a stressed structural member. Net -£26/veh from the deleted floor and lid.
ideaData.materialGrade: Aluminium honeycomb-sandwich CTB top sheet; structural adhesive bond
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Eliminates a stamped floor line (NRC -£1.19M) and the pack lid; the pack bonds directly to sills, removing 40 floor welds and 3.5 min of sealing. Battery and body assembly merge, requiring adhesive-bond and leak-test stations; raises stiffness but couples pack and body serviceability.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Cell-to-body deletes the floor pan and pack lid, sharing one structural surface.

ideaData.riskNotes: CTB couples pack and body - adhesive bond must seal/insulate and survive thermal events; serviceability and end-of-life pack removal are harder. Crash load path through the bonded pack needs thorough validation; sealing/leak integrity is safety-critical and repair after damage is costly.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD Seal CTB (cell-to-body) floor, 2022
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN R100 battery safety; C-NCAP/Euro NCAP side-pole

title: Audi co-extruded EPDM-foam body main weatherstrip on carrier-less clip
description: Replace a steel-carrier EPDM door aperture weatherstrip with a carrier-less co-extruded EPDM-sponge profile using moulded push-in fir-tree clips, deleting the embedded metal carrier and 100% of clip-on labour on the robot apply cell. Mass −0.6kg/car, raw cost −£1.8/veh across full aperture. Sealing force tuned to 8N/100mm for door-close effort. Benchmarks Audi A6 e-tron primary seal.

ideaData.title: Audi co-extruded EPDM-foam body main weatherstrip on carrier-less clip
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline door-aperture weatherstrip is a co-extruded EPDM seal on an embedded steel carrier, clipped onto the flange. The redesign uses a carrier-less co-extruded EPDM-sponge profile retained by moulded push-in fir-tree clips, deleting the embedded metal carrier and 100% of the clip-on labour on the robotic apply cell (the fir-trees self-retain). Removing the steel carrier sheds 0.6kg and £1.8/veh of raw material, and the sealing force is tuned to 8N/100mm to control door-close effort. The EPDM sponge gives compression-set resistance for long-term sealing.
ideaData.materialGrade: EPDM sponge co-extrusion, carrier-less with moulded fir-tree clips
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes the embedded steel carrier and removes carrier-insertion from the extrusion line; moulded fir-tree clips self-retain so robotic clip-on labour is eliminated. Lower extrusion complexity and scrap; same robot apply cell, faster cycle. Mass -0.6kg/car.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Carrier-less seal with self-retaining clips removes steel and clip-on labour.

ideaData.riskNotes: Without a steel carrier, retention force and dimensional stability rely on fir-tree fit and flange tolerance - risk of seal walk-off or whistle; door-close effort (8N/100mm) and water-leak must be validated. EPDM sponge compression-set over life and clip pull-out strength need durability testing.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Audi A6 e-tron primary door seal, 2024

evidenceSources.title: Carrier-less EPDM aperture seal with fir-tree retention

title: Porsche cast-magnesium front strut shock-tower
description: Replace a 5-piece welded steel shock tower (590MPa, 22 welds) with a single AM60B magnesium die-casting, 4.0mm wall. Consolidation removes 22 welds, 4 stampings and a fixture (NRC −£646k), shedding 2.8kg. Mg HPDC piece cost £29 vs £24 steel assembly but deletes £9.3/veh labour+joining, net −£4.3/veh. Local steel insert for strut-bolt fatigue. Benchmarks Porsche Panamera tower.

ideaData.title: Porsche cast-magnesium front strut shock-tower
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline shock tower is a 5-piece welded steel assembly in 590MPa (CR340LA) with 22 spot welds. The redesign casts it as a single AM60B magnesium high-pressure die-casting at 4.0mm wall, consolidating five stampings into one part and removing 22 welds and a weld fixture (NRC -£646k). Magnesium HPDC sheds 2.8kg; although the Mg casting piece cost (£29) exceeds the steel assembly (£24), deleting £9.3/veh of labour and joining nets -£4.3/veh. A local steel insert is cast-in at the strut-bolt boss to handle bolt-preload fatigue where magnesium creep would otherwise be a concern.
ideaData.materialGrade: AM60B magnesium HPDC, 4.0mm wall, cast-in steel strut insert
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces five stampings and a 22-weld cell with one Mg die-casting and a bolt-on/weld interface; deletes the weld fixture (NRC -£646k) and 22 welds. Requires Mg HPDC press with protective cover-gas melt handling and insert placement; lower part count and faster assembly offset higher Mg piece cost.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Single magnesium casting consolidates the tower, trading material cost for labour and mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: Magnesium creep/relaxation at bolted joints requires the cast-in steel insert and torque-retention validation; Mg corrosion (galvanic with steel/fasteners) needs coating/isolation. Mg HPDC fire-safety in melt handling and porosity at the boss must be controlled; supply base for AM60B is limited.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Porsche Panamera magnesium shock tower, 2023

evidenceSources.title: Porsche Panamera front strut tower teardown

title: Nio bonded full door-in-white aluminium ring frame
description: Build the rear door-in-white as an AA6014 inner ring SPR-bonded to AA6014-T4 outer with a 1500MPa hot-stamped anti-intrusion beam, replacing an all-steel 7-panel door (26 welds, 2 reinforcements). 26 welds become 20 SPR + adhesive; one die deleted (NRC −£748k). Mass −3.4kg, piece cost −£10.9/veh. Benchmarks Nio ET7 rear door build.

ideaData.title: Nio bonded full door-in-white aluminium ring frame
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline rear door-in-white is an all-steel 7-panel door with 26 spot welds and two reinforcements. The redesign builds an AA6014 (AlMgSi) inner ring SPR-bonded to an AA6014-T4 outer with a 1500MPa 22MnB5 hot-stamped anti-intrusion beam, replacing the steel panels. The 26 spot welds become 20 SPR points plus structural adhesive, and one stamping die is deleted (NRC -£748k) as the ring consolidates panels. The aluminium ring and adhesive shed 3.4kg while the press-hardened boron beam meets side-intrusion, netting -£10.9/veh of door piece cost.
ideaData.materialGrade: AA6014-T4 Al ring/outer + 22MnB5 1500 MPa hot-stamped beam
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces a steel spot-weld door line with an SPR + adhesive aluminium-ring cell plus a hot-stamped beam insert; 26 welds to 20 SPR, one die deleted (NRC -£748k). Adds riveting, adhesive dispense and oven cure; dissimilar-metal sealing required. Lower mass, fewer panels and framing welds.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Aluminium SPR-bonded door ring with boron beam cuts welds, mass and a die.

ideaData.riskNotes: Al ring with steel beam mixes metals - galvanic isolation, SPR joint fatigue and adhesive durability need validation; side-intrusion (FMVSS 214) load transfer through the bonded ring must be proven. Hem flush stability over bake and rivet-die wear require process control.

title: Li Auto roll-formed 1500MPa front bumper beam
description: Convert a hot-stamped B-section bumper beam (1500MPa, 2-piece, 10 welds) to a roll-formed closed octagon in 1500MPa martensitic at 1.6mm, sweep-bent to bumper radius. Roll forming removes the hot-stamp furnace energy and 10 welds, with 96% utilisation. Mass −1.0kg, piece cost £18.1 vs £22.2 (£4.1/veh), tool NRC £323k. Benchmarks Li Auto L7 front beam.

ideaData.title: Li Auto roll-formed 1500MPa front bumper beam
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline front bumper beam is a hot-stamped B-section in 1500MPa boron, 2-piece with 10 welds. The redesign roll-forms a closed octagonal section from 1500MPa martensitic (MS1500) coil at 1.6mm, then sweep-bends it to the bumper radius. Roll forming removes the hot-stamp furnace energy and the 10 welds at ~96% material utilisation, shedding 1.0kg. The closed octagon gives high bending resistance for the beam load case at lower process cost: piece cost £18.1 vs £22.2 (-£4.1/veh), with a roll-set tool NRC of £323k versus a hot-stamp die.
ideaData.materialGrade: MS1500 / 1500 MPa martensitic steel, 1.6mm roll-formed octagon
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces a hot-stamp furnace + draw die + 10-weld assembly with a roll-forming line and sweep-bender; ~96% utilisation, 10 welds removed, tool NRC £323k. Eliminates furnace energy and quench die; constant section bent to radius. Lower energy and consumables, fewer welds.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Roll-formed martensitic beam removes the hot-stamp furnace and welds at high utilisation.

ideaData.riskNotes: MS1500 bend-radius and sweep-bend springback risk cracking; closure-weld integrity is crash-critical for the bumper load case. RCAR/IIHS low-speed and barrier performance must be re-validated versus the hot-stamped baseline; constant section limits crush-can integration.

title: Hongqi gigacast rear floor with integrated rear rails

ideaData.title: Hongqi gigacast rear floor with integrated rear rails
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline rear floor module is 68 stamped/welded parts joined by ~240 joints, including rear floor, rear rails and tower mounts. The redesign casts them as one AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) gigacasting at 3.5mm wall on an ~8000-tonne press. Crash zones are tuned through wall-thickness grading so energy absorption is controlled within the casting. Consolidation removes two weld cells and 15kg of flanges and cuts takt 20%. A £168 welded assembly becomes a £119 casting (-£49/veh), the die NRC amortised over seven years.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) gigacast, 3.5mm, graded walls
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces 68 parts and ~240 joints with one casting; deletes two weld cells, -15kg flanges and takt -20%. Requires ~8000T press, vacuum die, melt handling and datum machining. Wall-thickness grading tunes crush; high die NRC over 7 yr; far fewer framing welds and panel logistics.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Rear-floor mega-casting collapses ~68 parts and two weld cells into one shot.

ideaData.riskNotes: Graded-wall crush tuning and porosity control at tower mounts need CAE plus CT inspection; casting is repair-limited, raising collision repair cost. Single die is a launch/IP single-point risk; alloy ductility for rear-impact and scrap rate drive the business case.

title: Volvo down-gauged 1180MPa CP A-pillar lower
description: Re-grade the A-pillar lower from 780MPa DP at 1.9mm to 1180MPa CP at 1.3mm, down-gauging while holding small-overlap crash load path. Saves 1.2kg and £4/veh raw steel. Existing draw die reworked for new springback (NRC £128k). Pairs with a deleted local reinforcement (−6 welds). Benchmarks Volvo EX30 A-pillar lower.

ideaData.title: Volvo down-gauged 1180MPa CP A-pillar lower
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline A-pillar lower is DP780 dual-phase steel at 1.9mm. The redesign re-grades to CP1180 (1180MPa complex-phase) at 1.3mm, down-gauging while preserving the small-overlap (IIHS) crash load path. The stronger CP grade lets thickness drop 0.6mm, saving 1.2kg and £4/veh of raw steel, and a local reinforcement is deleted (-6 welds) because the base material now carries the load. The existing draw die is reworked for the higher CP1180 springback (NRC £128k), keeping the line and station count unchanged.
ideaData.materialGrade: CP1180 / 1180 MPa complex-phase steel, 1.3mm (from DP780 1.9mm)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Re-works the existing draw die for CP1180 springback (NRC £128k); deletes a local reinforcement and 6 welds. Higher press tonnage for the stronger grade; same line/stations. Impact is material-led with minor tooling rework and a small weld-count reduction.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Grade-up/gauge-down on the A-pillar lower deletes a reinforcement and steel mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: CP1180 limited bendability and springback risk dimensional scatter on a crash-critical part; small-overlap load path must be CAE/test-confirmed without the deleted reinforcement. Edge-stretch/hole-expansion limits and die rework validation are required.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo EX30 A-pillar lower material strategy, 2024
ideaData.regulatoryContext: IIHS small-overlap; Euro NCAP frontal

title: AITO TPE bonded fixed-glass body sealing system
description: Consolidate quarter-glass, C-pillar trim seal and drip rail into one robot-applied TPE encapsulation bonded to flush fixed glass, deleting 2 EPDM strips, 8 clips and a butyl bead. Removes 1.1 min manual fitment and 0.7kg. Flush glazing cuts Cd by 0.004. Piece cost £8.3 vs £11.2 (£2.9/veh). Benchmarks AITO M9 rear quarter glazing.

ideaData.title: AITO TPE bonded fixed-glass body sealing system
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline rear quarter sealing is multiple parts: quarter glass, a C-pillar trim seal, a drip rail, two EPDM strips, eight clips and a butyl bead. The redesign consolidates them into one robot-applied TPE (thermoplastic elastomer) encapsulation bonded to a flush fixed quarter glass, deleting the two EPDM strips, eight clips and the butyl bead. Removing the discrete seals and the manual fitment saves 1.1 min and 0.7kg, and the flush glazing reduces drag coefficient by 0.004. Piece cost drops £8.3 vs £11.2 (-£2.9/veh) with the TPE giving recyclable, weatherable sealing.
ideaData.materialGrade: TPE encapsulation on flush fixed quarter glass
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces multi-part seal/trim assembly with a robot TPE-encapsulation glazing module; deletes two EPDM strips, eight clips, a butyl bead and -1.1 min manual fitment. Module supplied flush-bonded to glass, cutting plant part count; adds encapsulation tooling and bond/prime control.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Flush TPE-encapsulated quarter glass consolidates seals, clips and trim into one module.

ideaData.riskNotes: TPE-to-glass adhesion and weather/UV durability need validation; flush bonded glass complicates service replacement. Water-management/drip function moved into the module must be leak-tested; aero benefit assumes consistent flushness within glaze tolerance.

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN R43 glazing; water-leak targets

title: AVATR 980MPa laser-welded blank floor cross-member
description: Replace a 3-piece seat cross-member (440MPa, 16 welds) with a laser-welded tailored blank (980MPa Docol at seat anchors, 340LA mid-span) stamped as one part. Deletes 2 stampings, 10 welds and a fixture (NRC −£357k). Mass −0.9kg, piece cost £11.6 vs £14.4 (£2.8/veh). Meets seat-belt-anchor pull. Benchmarks AVATR 11 floor cross-member.

ideaData.title: AVATR 980MPa laser-welded blank floor cross-member
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline seat cross-member is a 3-piece assembly in 440MPa steel with 16 spot welds. The redesign forms one laser-welded tailored blank - 980MPa Docol at the seat-anchor zones and CR340LA at mid-span - stamped as a single part. The tailor-welded blank places high strength only at the belt-anchor regions, deleting two stampings, 10 welds and a fixture (NRC -£357k) and shedding 0.9kg of overlap. The 980MPa anchor zones meet seat-belt-anchorage pull (ECE R14) while the mid-span 340LA keeps formability for the deep section.
ideaData.materialGrade: Laser-welded blank: Docol 980 (980 MPa) anchors / CR340LA mid-span
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: A laser blank-welding line feeds one draw die replacing three stampings and a 16-weld cell; deletes two stampings, 10 welds and a fixture (NRC -£357k). Strength placed by zone; fewer framing welds and panel parts. Adds blank-weld inspection.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Tailor-welded cross-member places anchor-grade steel by zone, cutting welds and parts.

ideaData.riskNotes: Weld-line position relative to belt-anchor pull (ECE R14) is critical - formability mismatch (980 vs 340LA) can split at the transition; seam quality must be inspected. A single high-value blank scraps if mis-welded; springback of the mixed-grade draw needs tryout.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: AVATR 11 floor/seat cross-member, 2022
ideaData.regulatoryContext: ECE R14 seat-belt anchorage; ECE R17 seat strength

title: Xpeng aluminium-extrusion sill with internal crush ribs
description: Replace a multi-cell steel rocker (3× 1180MPa stampings, 28 welds) with a 6082-T6 multi-void extruded sill, 3.0mm webs, protecting the battery side in pole impact. Extrusion deletes 28 welds and 3 dies (NRC −£1.27M), saving 2.6kg. Bolted/bonded to floor cuts 2.1 min weld time. Piece cost £32 vs £28 steel but −£12/veh labour+joining, net −£7.6/veh. Benchmarks Xpeng G9 sill.

ideaData.title: Xpeng aluminium-extrusion sill with internal crush ribs
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline rocker is a multi-cell steel assembly: three 1180MPa stampings joined by 28 spot welds. The redesign uses a 6082-T6 aluminium multi-void extruded sill with 3.0mm internal webs, providing progressive crush to protect the battery side in pole impact. Extrusion delivers the multi-cell section in one profile, deleting 28 welds and three dies (NRC -£1.27M) and shedding 2.6kg. Bolting/bonding the extrusion to the floor cuts 2.1 min of weld time; although the extrusion piece cost (£32) exceeds steel (£28), deleting £12/veh of labour and joining nets -£7.6/veh.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 aluminium multi-void extrusion, 3.0mm webs
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces three stampings and a 28-weld cell with one extrusion, saw-to-length and bolt/bond to floor; deletes three dies (NRC -£1.27M) and 28 welds (-2.1 min). Adds extrusion machining and adhesive/fastener install; lower framing welds. Higher piece cost offset by labour and mass.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Multi-void extruded sill consolidates the multi-cell rocker, trading material for labour and mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: 6082-T6 crush stability in pole impact must protect the battery - web buckling and weld/bond joint strength need validation; galvanic isolation from steel floor required. Extrusion straightness/tolerance and energy-absorption repeatability across temperature must be qualified; RCAR repair differs from steel.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Xpeng G9 aluminium sill/rocker, 2022

evidenceSources.title: Extruded multi-cell sills for EV battery protection

ideaData.regulatoryContext: Euro NCAP/C-NCAP side pole; UN R100 battery; FMVSS 214

title: Maextro PA66-GF35 active grille shutter glazing surround
description: Replace an aluminium windscreen lower cowl seal retainer with an injection-moulded PA66-GF35 retainer integrating drain channels and the cowl-to-glass seal lip in one shot. Deletes a metal bracket, 4 screws and a foam dam. Mass −0.5kg, piece cost £4.6 vs £6.2 (£1.6/veh), tool NRC £238k. Improves water management. Benchmarks Maextro S800 cowl seal.

ideaData.title: Maextro PA66-GF35 active grille shutter glazing surround
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline windscreen lower cowl uses an aluminium seal retainer plus a separate metal bracket, four screws and a foam dam. The redesign injection-moulds a PA66-GF35 (35% glass-filled polyamide) retainer that integrates drain channels and the cowl-to-glass seal lip in one shot, deleting the metal bracket, four screws and the foam dam. The glass-reinforced nylon gives the stiffness and heat resistance for the cowl while integrating water-management drains, improving water handling. Mass -0.5kg, piece cost £4.6 vs £6.2 (-£1.6/veh), tool NRC £238k.
ideaData.materialGrade: PA66-GF35 (35% glass-filled polyamide) injection-moulded cowl retainer
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces an aluminium retainer + bracket + screws + foam dam with a single injection-moulded part integrating drains and seal lip; deletes the bracket, four screws and the foam dam. Tool NRC £238k. Fewer parts to handle and fasten; integrated drains simplify cowl water-management assembly.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Glass-filled nylon cowl retainer integrates drains and seal lip, deleting metal parts and fasteners.

ideaData.riskNotes: PA66-GF35 moisture absorption and CTE can affect dimensional fit to glass over climate; integrated seal lip durability and drain-channel clogging need validation. UV/heat aging under the windscreen and warpage of the glass-filled part must be controlled.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Maextro S800 windscreen cowl/seal, 2024
ideaData.regulatoryContext: Water-management/leak targets

title: Luxeed full BIW floor multi-material laser-welded architecture
description: Re-architect the floor assembly using 5 laser-welded tailored blanks (boron tunnel, 590MPa pans, 1180MPa anchors) replacing 19 stampings and 64 welds. Consolidation deletes 4 dies (NRC −£1.61M), 22 welds and 4.0kg flange overlap. Continuous laser seams seal better, cutting sealer 1.8 min. Net −£14/veh. Benchmarks Luxeed R7 floor.

ideaData.title: Luxeed full BIW floor multi-material laser-welded architecture
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline floor assembly is 19 stampings joined by 64 spot welds. The redesign re-architects it as five laser-welded tailored blanks - a 22MnB5 boron tunnel, CR340LA/590MPa pans, and 1180MPa anchor zones - placing strength by region. Consolidation deletes four stamping dies (NRC -£1.61M), 22 welds and 4.0kg of flange overlap. Continuous laser seams seal better than spot-weld flanges, cutting sealer application 1.8 min. Net -£14/veh from reduced dies, welds, mass and sealing labour, with strength tuned to each load path.
ideaData.materialGrade: Laser-welded blanks: 22MnB5 boron tunnel / 590MPa pans / 1180MPa anchors
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Five tailor-welded blanks feed consolidated draw dies replacing 19 stampings and 64 welds; deletes four dies (NRC -£1.61M), 22 welds and 4.0kg overlap, with continuous laser seams cutting sealer 1.8 min. Adds blank-welding line and seam inspection; far fewer panels and framing welds.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Tailor-welded floor places strength by zone, cutting dies, welds, mass and sealer.

ideaData.riskNotes: Mixed-grade blanks (boron/590/1180) risk weld-line cracking and formability mismatch; seam position vs crash load paths needs CAE validation. Large consolidated panels raise scrap value and press-tonnage demand; continuous-seam sealing must be leak-verified.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Luxeed R7 floor multi-material architecture, 2024
ideaData.regulatoryContext: Euro NCAP/C-NCAP frontal & side; UN R100

title: Denza hot-stamped one-piece B-pillar outer + reinforcement
description: Integrate B-pillar outer and reinforcement into a single tailor-rolled 22MnB5 hot-stamped part (1500MPa, 1.0–1.9mm) with a soft-zone at the lower base for controlled folding, replacing 3 parts and 18 welds. Deletes 2 dies (NRC −£1.02M), 1.6kg and 14 welds. Piece cost £21.2 vs £25.8 (£4.7/veh). Benchmarks Denza D9 B-pillar.

ideaData.title: Denza hot-stamped one-piece B-pillar outer + reinforcement
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline B-pillar is three parts (outer + reinforcement) joined by 18 welds. The redesign integrates outer and reinforcement into a single tailor-rolled 22MnB5 blank, press-hardened to 1500MPa with gauge varying 1.0-1.9mm, and a deliberate soft-zone at the lower base for controlled folding in side impact. Hot stamping with tailored tempering places strength and ductility by location, replacing three parts and deleting two dies (NRC -£1.02M), 1.6kg and 14 welds. The soft-zone manages the lower hinge of the pillar so occupant-cell intrusion is controlled. Piece cost £21.2 vs £25.8 (-£4.7/veh).
ideaData.materialGrade: 22MnB5 / 1500 MPa press-hardened, tailor-rolled 1.0-1.9mm, soft-zone base
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Tailor-rolling + hot-stamp with in-die tailored tempering replaces three cold parts and an 18-weld cell; deletes two dies (NRC -£1.02M), 14 welds and 1.6kg. Soft-zone created by controlled die-cooling; needs press-hardening furnace and TRB supply. Fewer panels and framing welds.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One tailor-rolled hot-stamped B-pillar with a soft zone integrates the reinforcement.

ideaData.riskNotes: Soft-zone process window (cooling-rate control) must reliably hit the folding target without weakening intrusion resistance; tailor-rolled transitions risk thinning. Hydrogen embrittlement and side-pole certification (Euro NCAP) must be re-validated; TRB/PHS supply base is narrow.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Denza D9 B-pillar one-piece hot-stamped, 2022

evidenceSources.title: Tailored-tempering soft zones in press-hardened B-pillars

ideaData.regulatoryContext: Euro NCAP side pole / UN R135; FMVSS 214

title: BMW aluminium gigacast rear floor (Neue Klasse)
description: Replace a stamped-welded rear floor module (66 parts, 230 joints) with one AlSi10MnMg gigacast, 3.3mm wall, integrating rear rails and damper towers. Removes 2 weld cells, 15kg and cuts takt 20%. Piece cost £117 vs £167 (£49/veh); 8500T die NRC over 7 yr. Self-piercing repair patches preserve serviceability. Benchmarks BMW Neue Klasse rear casting.

ideaData.title: BMW aluminium gigacast rear floor (Neue Klasse)
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline rear floor module is 66 stamped-welded parts joined by ~230 joints. The redesign casts it as one AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) gigacasting at 3.3mm wall, integrating rear rails and damper towers, shot on an ~8500-tonne press. Self-piercing repair patches are designed into the casting so collision damage can be locally re-skinned, preserving serviceability. Consolidation removes two weld cells, 15kg and cuts takt 20%. A £167 welded assembly becomes a £117 casting (-£49/veh), the large die NRC amortised over seven years.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) gigacast, 3.3mm wall
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces 66 parts and ~230 joints with one casting; deletes two weld cells, -15kg, takt -20%. Requires ~8500T press, vacuum die, melt handling and datum machining. SPR repair-patch interfaces designed in for serviceability; very high die NRC over 7 yr; minimal framing welds.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Neue-Klasse rear mega-casting collapses ~66 parts and two weld cells with designed-in repairability.

ideaData.riskNotes: Porosity/fatigue at damper towers need vacuum control and CT; the SPR repair-patch concept must be validated for crash and corrosion. Single die is a launch/IP single-point risk; alloy ductility for rear impact and scrap rate drive the case; collision repair cost vs steel must be assessed.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BMW Neue Klasse rear gigacasting, 2025
ideaData.regulatoryContext: Euro NCAP rear impact; UN R34; RCAR repairability

title: Mercedes 6016-T4 aluminium fender with integrated bracket
description: Replace a steel fender (270BH) plus 3 bolted steel mounting brackets with a single AA6016-T4 fender with stamped-in mounting flanges, deleting 3 brackets, 9 bolts and 0.8 min assembly. Mass −1.7kg, piece cost £13.9 vs £16.3 incl. brackets (£2.4/veh). Aluminium avoids stone-chip corrosion. Benchmarks Mercedes EQS fender.

ideaData.title: Mercedes 6016-T4 aluminium fender with integrated bracket
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline fender is a 270BH bake-hardening steel panel plus three bolted steel mounting brackets. The redesign uses a single AA6016-T4 aluminium fender with the mounting flanges stamped in, deleting the three brackets, nine bolts and 0.8 min of assembly. The 6016-T4 grade gives the formability and dent resistance for the outer skin while the integrated flanges replace the discrete brackets, shedding 1.7kg. Aluminium also eliminates stone-chip corrosion at the wheel-arch edge. Piece cost £13.9 vs £16.3 including brackets (-£2.4/veh).
ideaData.materialGrade: AA6016-T4 aluminium fender (from 270BH steel + brackets)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Integrated stamped-in mounting flanges delete three brackets and nine bolts (-0.8 min); an aluminium draw die replaces the steel die and bracket sub-assembly. Lighter handling, no bracket logistics; aluminium needs separate hemming/handling but removes corrosion-protection steps at the arch.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Aluminium fender with integrated flanges deletes brackets, bolts and corrosion risk.

ideaData.riskNotes: 6016-T4 dent/oil-canning and flange formability must meet stiffness at the integrated mounts; the aluminium-to-steel body interface needs galvanic isolation. Thermal expansion affects flush/gap over temperature; repair and paint match differ from steel.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes EQS aluminium fender, 2021
ideaData.regulatoryContext: Pedestrian-impact fender deformation; corrosion standards

title: BYD cold-formed 1500MPa door anti-intrusion beam
description: Switch the door side-impact beam from a hot-stamped tube (1500MPa) to a roll-formed 1500MPa martensitic tube at 1.4mm, removing the hot-stamp furnace energy and quench die. Roll forming cuts piece cost £7.6 vs £10.3 (£2.7/veh across 4 doors) and 0.6kg. Meets FMVSS214 intrusion. Tool NRC £204k. Benchmarks BYD Han door beam.

ideaData.title: BYD cold-formed 1500MPa door anti-intrusion beam
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline door side-impact beam is a hot-stamped tube at 1500MPa. The redesign roll-forms a 1500MPa martensitic (MS1500) tube at 1.4mm, removing the hot-stamp furnace energy and quench die. Roll forming produces the closed beam continuously and is cut to length, cutting piece cost £7.6 vs £10.3 (-£2.7/veh across four doors) and shedding 0.6kg. The closed martensitic tube meets FMVSS 214 side-intrusion at a lower process cost, with a roll-set tool NRC of £204k versus a hot-stamp die.
ideaData.materialGrade: MS1500 / 1500 MPa martensitic steel, 1.4mm roll-formed tube
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces a hot-stamp furnace + quench die with a roll-forming line and cut-off; removes furnace energy, tool NRC £204k. Continuous tube production, lower energy. Beam attaches to door inner via brackets/welds as before; main impact is process energy and piece cost.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Roll-formed martensitic door beam removes the hot-stamp furnace at lower piece cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: MS1500 bend/forming limits and closure-weld integrity are intrusion-critical; FMVSS 214 and side-pole must be re-validated versus the hot-stamped baseline. Springback and tube-mill seam quality affect performance; bracket-attach fatigue needs checking.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD Han door anti-intrusion beam, 2021
ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS 214 side impact; Euro NCAP side pole

title: Audi structural-foam-filled A-pillar node
description: Replace a welded-in steel reinforcement bracket at the A-pillar/hinge node with an expandable epoxy structural-foam insert baked during e-coat, restoring stiffness and NVH without the stamping. Deletes 1 bracket (£2.2), 6 welds and 0.5 min. Foam insert £0.8, net −£1.8/veh and −0.4kg. Improves body-bending mode 6Hz. Benchmarks Audi Q4 e-tron pillar node.

ideaData.title: Audi structural-foam-filled A-pillar node
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline A-pillar/hinge node uses a welded-in steel reinforcement bracket. The redesign replaces it with an expandable epoxy structural-foam insert that is placed in the cavity and expands/cures during the e-coat bake, restoring section stiffness and NVH without a stamping. This deletes one bracket (£2.2), six welds and 0.5 min of labour; the foam insert costs £0.8, netting -£1.8/veh and -0.4kg. The cured structural foam couples the cavity walls, raising the body-bending mode by 6Hz.
ideaData.materialGrade: Expandable epoxy structural foam (e-coat-cured cavity insert)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes a welded-in steel reinforcement and six welds; an expandable foam insert is clipped into the cavity pre-paint and cures in the e-coat oven (no added thermal step). Removes a stamping and a weld operation (-0.5 min); adds an insert-placement station.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: E-coat-cured structural foam replaces a welded reinforcement, cutting parts and welds.

ideaData.riskNotes: Foam expansion fill and adhesion must be consistent to hit stiffness/NVH; cavity geometry and bake temperature window govern cure. Long-term durability, water intrusion and foam aging need validation; crash load contribution of the node must be confirmed if structurally relevant.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Audi Q4 e-tron A-pillar node foam reinforcement, 2021

evidenceSources.title: Audi Q4 e-tron body cavity inserts teardown

ideaData.regulatoryContext: NVH targets; structural-node stiffness

title: Porsche full aluminium spaceframe side-frame with cast nodes
description: Build the bodyside as extruded 6082-T6 rails joined by AlSi10MnMg cast nodes (A-pillar, B-pillar, C-pillar) via MIG + SPR, replacing 9 steel stampings and 34 welds. Cast nodes consolidate junctions, deleting 5 dies (NRC −£1.87M) and 4.6kg. Net piece cost −£9.3/veh after labour. Benchmarks Porsche Taycan side spaceframe.

ideaData.title: Porsche full aluminium spaceframe side-frame with cast nodes
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline bodyside is nine steel stampings joined by 34 welds. The redesign builds it as a 6082-T6 aluminium spaceframe - extruded rails joined by AlSi10MnMg cast nodes at the A-, B- and C-pillar junctions via MIG welding plus self-pierce riveting. The cast nodes consolidate the complex junctions that previously needed stamped reinforcements, deleting five dies (NRC -£1.87M) and 4.6kg. The aluminium spaceframe trades higher material cost for far lower joining and mass, netting -£9.3/veh after labour, with extrusions and castings tailored to each load path.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6082-T6 extrusions + AlSi10MnMg cast nodes (MIG + SPR)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces nine stampings and a 34-weld steel framing with extrusion cut/bend, cast nodes and a MIG + SPR cell; deletes five dies (NRC -£1.87M) and 4.6kg. Adds aluminium welding, riveting and node casting; fewer panel parts but new joining processes and dimensional control of the frame.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Aluminium spaceframe with cast nodes consolidates the bodyside, trading material for tooling and mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: Aluminium MIG-weld distortion and node-casting porosity affect fit/fatigue; SPR/MIG joint validation and corrosion isolation are required. Dimensional control of a multi-extrusion frame is challenging; crash performance of cast nodes must be proven and repair differs from monocoque steel.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Porsche Taycan aluminium side spaceframe, 2020
ideaData.regulatoryContext: Euro NCAP side impact; UN R95

title: Nio flocked TPV inner-belt waist seal
description: Replace an EPDM inner-belt (waist) seal with stainless insert by a co-extruded TPV belt seal with a stamped-clip-less push-on carrier and flocked lip. TPV recyclable, no vulcanising oven, extrudes 25% faster. Mass −0.3kg/car, piece cost £2.6 vs £3.7 (£1.1/veh). Lower glass scrub noise. Benchmarks Nio ES6 belt seal.

ideaData.title: Nio flocked TPV inner-belt waist seal
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline inner-belt (waist) seal is EPDM with a stainless-steel insert/carrier. The redesign co-extrudes a TPV belt seal with a stamped-clip-less push-on carrier and a flocked lip. TPV is recyclable and needs no vulcanising oven, extruding ~25% faster, and the carrier-less push-on retention deletes the stainless insert and clips. Mass drops 0.3kg/car and piece cost £2.6 vs £3.7 (-£1.1/veh). The flocked lip lowers glass-scrub noise as the window travels. The TPV grade provides weatherability and recyclable scrap.
ideaData.materialGrade: TPV co-extruded belt seal, flocked lip, carrier-less
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces EPDM + stainless insert + vulcanising oven with a TPV co-extrusion line and push-on retention; ~25% faster, no cure oven, recyclable regrind. Deletes the stainless carrier and clips; same install location. Lower seal-shop energy and WIP.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: TPV belt seal removes the stainless carrier and cure oven while lowering scrub noise.

ideaData.riskNotes: Without a stainless insert, belt-line retention/stiffness depends on the push-on profile and flange tolerance - risk of seal sag or glass-rattle; TPV compression-set/heat creep weaker than EPDM. Flock abrasion and low-temp flexibility need durability validation.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Nio ES6 belt-line waist seal, 2021

title: Zeekr aluminium-casting integrated rear shock-tower + wheelhouse
description: Integrate rear shock tower, wheelhouse inner and rail node into one AlSi10MnMg casting (3.6mm wall) replacing 12 stampings and 58 welds. Deletes 3 dies (NRC −£1.1M) and 4.1kg. Casting bolts to extruded rails, cutting 2.4 min weld time. Net piece cost −£11/veh. Benchmarks Zeekr 009 rear tower casting.

ideaData.title: Zeekr aluminium-casting integrated rear shock-tower + wheelhouse
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline rear corner is 12 stampings (rear shock tower, wheelhouse inner, rail node) joined by 58 welds. The redesign casts them as one AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) casting at 3.6mm wall, consolidating the tower, wheelhouse and rail node into a single part. This deletes three dies (NRC -£1.1M) and 4.1kg, and the casting bolts to extruded rails, cutting 2.4 min of weld time. Unlike a full gigacasting this is a node-scale casting that integrates the local junction, netting -£11/veh after labour, with the cast geometry tuned for damper-mount fatigue.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) HPDC node casting, 3.6mm wall
