ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces 12 stampings and a 58-weld cell with one node casting bolted to extruded rails; deletes three dies (NRC -£1.1M), 4.1kg and -2.4 min weld time. Requires HPDC press and datum machining of bolt interfaces; far fewer framing welds and panels. Node-scale die NRC lower than full gigacast.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Rear-corner node casting integrates tower, wheelhouse and rail junction, cutting welds and dies.

ideaData.riskNotes: Damper-mount fatigue requires porosity control (vacuum HPDC) and CT inspection; bolted rail interface needs joint fatigue and sealing validation. Casting repair is limited; alloy ductility for rear impact and dimensional control of the bolt pattern must be qualified.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Zeekr 009 rear shock-tower casting, 2022

evidenceSources.title: Node-scale structural castings vs full gigacasting

ideaData.regulatoryContext: Rear-impact; damper-mount durability

title: Hongqi 1180MPa roll-formed dash cross-car beam
description: Replace a 2-shell stamped cowl beam (440MPa, 18 welds) with a roll-formed 1180MPa closed tube at 1.3mm. Roll forming reaches 95% utilisation, deletes 18 welds and 2 dies (NRC −£0.94M), saving 1.5kg. Higher torsional stiffness aids steering-column NVH. Piece cost £12.8 vs £15.6 (£2.8/veh). Benchmarks Hongqi H9 cowl beam.

ideaData.title: Hongqi 1180MPa roll-formed dash cross-car beam
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline cowl beam is a 2-shell stamped section in 440MPa steel with 18 spot welds. The redesign roll-forms a closed tube in 1180MPa CP steel at 1.3mm. Roll forming reaches ~95% material utilisation, deletes 18 welds and two dies (NRC -£0.94M), and sheds 1.5kg. The closed high-strength section raises torsional stiffness, aiding steering-column NVH, and the continuous process undercuts twin draw dies. Piece cost £12.8 vs £15.6 (-£2.8/veh), with strength placed in the closed cross-section rather than overlapping flanges.
ideaData.materialGrade: CP1180 / 1180 MPa complex-phase steel, 1.3mm roll-formed tube
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces two draw dies and an 18-weld station with a roll-forming line + closure weld; ~95% utilisation, two dies deleted (NRC -£0.94M), 18 welds removed. Constant section needs separate brackets; lower press tonnage and scrap, fewer framing welds and higher dimensional repeatability.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Roll-formed CP1180 cowl tube removes dies and welds while raising NVH stiffness.

ideaData.riskNotes: CP1180 bend radii and closure-weld HAZ softening must be validated; constant section forces bracketry for column/HVAC mounts. Sweep/straightness tolerance to bodyside and steering-column modal targets must be confirmed; closure-weld integrity needed for stiffness.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Hongqi H9 cowl cross-car beam, 2021
ideaData.regulatoryContext: Steering-column NVH; UN R12 (indirect)

title: Li Auto adhesive-bonded panoramic windscreen-to-roof glazing
description: Adopt a continuous bonded windscreen-into-roof panoramic laminate (4.2mm) deleting the front roof bow header, header trim seal and 2 mouldings, replaced by a PU structural bead to the cowl/cant rail. Removes 1 stamping (NRC −£527k), 2.9kg and 1.3 min sealing. Net −£12/veh after glass premium. Benchmarks Li Auto MEGA front glazing.

ideaData.title: Li Auto adhesive-bonded panoramic windscreen-to-roof glazing
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline front roof uses a front roof bow header, a header trim seal and two mouldings. The redesign adopts a continuous bonded windscreen-into-roof panoramic laminate (4.2mm, PVB-interlayer safety glass) glazed with a PU structural bead to the cowl and cant rail, deleting the header bow, the header seal and the two mouldings. The glass becomes a structural roof member, removing one stamping (NRC -£527k), 2.9kg and 1.3 min of sealing. Net -£12/veh after the glass premium, with the continuous bond improving sealing and the panoramic feel.
ideaData.materialGrade: Laminated PVB panoramic glass, 4.2mm, PU structural bond
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces the roof-bow header + seals + mouldings with a PU direct-glaze cell bonding a large panoramic laminate to cowl/cant rail; deletes one stamping (NRC -£527k), 2.9kg and -1.3 min sealing. Adds primer/robot-bead glaze and cure; large-glass handling fixtures; fewer trim parts.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Bonded windscreen-into-roof glass deletes the header bow, seals and mouldings.

ideaData.riskNotes: Removing the header bow shifts roof-crush/rollover load into the bonded glass and cant rails - FMVSS 216a and bond durability must be validated; large laminate raises breakage and solar/thermal load. Wind-noise, water-leak and stone-impact at the front edge need qualification.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Li Auto MEGA front panoramic glazing, 2024

evidenceSources.title: Li Auto MEGA front glazing teardown

ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS 216a roof crush; UN R43 glazing; UN R29-adjacent

title: AITO 22MnB5 hot-stamped front rail with tailored soft-zone
description: Replace a 3-piece stamped front longitudinal (590MPa, 24 welds) with a hot-stamped 22MnB5 rail (1500MPa) with laser-quench-tuned soft zones for axial crush, integrating the front bulkhead flange. Deletes 2 dies (NRC −£1.02M), 18 welds and 2.0kg. Piece cost £23 vs £27 (£4.3/veh). Benchmarks AITO M5 front rail.

ideaData.title: AITO 22MnB5 hot-stamped front rail with tailored soft-zone
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline front longitudinal rail is a 3-piece stamped assembly in 590MPa (CR340LA) with 24 welds. The redesign hot-stamps a single 22MnB5 boron rail at 1500MPa with laser-quench-tuned soft zones for progressive axial crush, integrating the front bulkhead flange. Tailored tempering places hard zones for intrusion resistance and soft zones for controlled folding, replacing three parts and deleting two dies (NRC -£1.02M), 18 welds and 2.0kg. The integrated bulkhead flange removes a joint. Piece cost £23 vs £27 (-£4.3/veh), with crush behaviour tuned by laser-induced soft zones.
ideaData.materialGrade: 22MnB5 / 1500 MPa press-hardened boron, laser-quench soft zones
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Hot-stamp press with laser/in-die tailored tempering replaces three cold stampings and a 24-weld cell; deletes two dies (NRC -£1.02M), 18 welds and 2.0kg, integrating the bulkhead flange. Needs press-hardening furnace and soft-zone control; fewer panels and framing welds.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One hot-stamped front rail with tailored soft zones manages axial crush and consolidates parts.

ideaData.riskNotes: Axial-crush stability of a press-hardened rail with soft zones must be CAE/test-proven - hard PHS can fracture rather than fold if soft zones are mis-placed; the process window for tailored tempering is critical. Hydrogen embrittlement and frontal certification (UN R94) must be validated.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: AITO M5 front longitudinal rail hot-stamped, 2022

evidenceSources.title: Soft-zone press-hardened front rails for axial crush

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN R94 frontal; Euro NCAP/C-NCAP

title: AVATR full BIW underbody central gigacast (front+mid)
description: Combine front and mid underbody into a single large AlSi10MnMg gigacast (3.4mm wall) replacing 90 parts and 300 joints across rails, tunnel and seat mounts. Removes 3 weld cells, 18kg and cuts BIW takt 24%. Piece cost £133 vs £185 (£53/veh); 9000T die NRC over 7 yr. Crush-grading via local wall control. Benchmarks AVATR 12 central casting.

ideaData.title: AVATR full BIW underbody central gigacast (front+mid)
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline front and mid underbody is 90 parts joined by ~300 spot/MIG joints across rails, tunnel and seat mounts. The redesign combines them into a single large AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) gigacasting at 3.4mm wall on a ~9000-tonne press (Idra-class). Crush behaviour is graded via local wall control. Consolidation removes three weld cells, 18kg and cuts BIW takt 24%. A £185 welded assembly becomes a £133 casting (-£53/veh), the very large die NRC amortised over seven years. This is among the largest single-casting underbody concepts.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) large gigacast, 3.4mm, graded walls
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces 90 parts and ~300 joints with one large casting; deletes three weld cells, -18kg and BIW takt -24%. Requires a ~9000T (Idra-class) press, very large vacuum die, melt dosing and extensive datum machining. Highest die NRC over 7 yr; dramatic framing-weld and logistics reduction.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Front+mid mega-casting collapses ~90 parts and three weld cells into a single large shot.

ideaData.riskNotes: A very large casting raises distortion, fill and porosity risk - requires vacuum HPDC, die thermal management and CT; crash grading via wall control needs extensive CAE. A single huge die is a major launch/IP single-point-of-failure; collision repair and scrap economics are critical.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: AVATR 12 central front+mid gigacasting, 2024

evidenceSources.title: Idra 9000T-class presses for large underbody castings

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN R94 frontal; Euro NCAP/C-NCAP; UN R100

title: Maextro cold-stamped 980MPa floor longitudinal reinforcement
description: Re-grade a floor longitudinal reinforcement from 590MPa DP at 1.8mm to 980MPa DP Docol at 1.3mm, down-gauging while holding crash load path. Saves 1.1kg and £3.6/veh raw steel. Existing die reused with new beads (NRC £119k). Pairs with deleted overlap patch (−4 welds). Benchmarks Maextro S800 floor reinforcement.

ideaData.title: Maextro cold-stamped 980MPa floor longitudinal reinforcement
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline floor longitudinal reinforcement is DP590 dual-phase steel at 1.8mm. The redesign re-grades to DP980 (Docol 980) at 1.3mm, down-gauging while holding the crash load path. The stronger dual-phase grade lets thickness drop 0.5mm, saving 1.1kg and £3.6/veh of raw steel, and a separate overlap patch is deleted (-4 welds) because the base material carries the load. The existing draw die is reused with new draw beads (NRC £119k) to manage DP980 springback, keeping the line and station count unchanged.
ideaData.materialGrade: DP980 / 980 MPa dual-phase (Docol 980), 1.3mm (from DP590 1.8mm)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Re-uses the existing draw die with new beads (NRC £119k) for DP980 springback; deletes an overlap patch and 4 welds. Higher press tonnage for the stronger grade; same line/stations. Mostly material-led saving with minor tooling rework.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Grade-up/gauge-down on the floor reinforcement deletes a patch and steel mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: DP980 springback and reduced formability at 1.3mm risk dimensional scatter; the crash load path must be CAE/test-confirmed without the deleted patch. Edge-stretch/hole-expansion limits constrain features; die-bead rework validation required for the harder grade.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Maextro S800 floor longitudinal reinforcement, 2024
ideaData.regulatoryContext: Frontal/side crash load path; UN R94/R95 (indirect)

title: Luxeed injection-moulded door inner module carrier
description: Replace a steel door inner sub-panel plus 4 brackets with a PP-LGF40 injection-moulded inner module carrier integrating window-reg rails, speaker and wiring clips, bonded to the door ring, deleting 4 brackets, 11 fasteners and 1.4 min assembly. Mass −2.1kg, piece cost £15.6 vs £19.2 (£3.7/veh). Benchmarks Luxeed S7 door module.

ideaData.title: Luxeed injection-moulded door inner module carrier
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline door inner is a steel sub-panel plus four brackets carrying window-regulator rails, speaker and wiring. The redesign uses a PP-LGF40 (40% long-glass-fibre polypropylene) injection-moulded inner module carrier that integrates the window-reg rails, speaker mount and wiring clips in one part, bonded to the door ring. This deletes four brackets, 11 fasteners and 1.4 min of assembly and sheds 2.1kg. The long-glass-fibre PP gives the stiffness and creep resistance for the regulator load while consolidating hardware mounting into a single moulded carrier. Piece cost £15.6 vs £19.2 (-£3.7/veh).
ideaData.materialGrade: PP-LGF40 (40% long-glass-fibre polypropylene) module carrier
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One injection-moulded carrier integrates reg-rails, speaker and clips, replacing a steel sub-panel + four brackets + 11 fasteners (-1.4 min), bonded to the door ring. Tooling is a large injection mould vs steel die + brackets; fewer fasteners and sub-parts, simplified door-line assembly and wire routing.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Long-glass-fibre PP carrier integrates door hardware mounts, deleting brackets and fasteners.

ideaData.riskNotes: PP-LGF40 creep and stiffness under regulator/load and crash (intrusion) must be validated; bond to the door ring and watertight wet/dry separation are critical. Long-fibre weld lines and warpage need mould control; acoustic and rattle performance of the integrated carrier require testing.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Luxeed S7 door module carrier, 2023
ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS 214 intrusion (carrier non-structural); water-management

title: Denza forged-aluminium door hinge with integrated check
description: Replace a 2-piece stamped-steel door hinge plus a separate bolted door-check link with one forged 6082 aluminium hinge integrating the detent check ramp, deleting the check bracket, 3 bolts and 0.6 min. Mass −0.5kg/hinge (−1.0kg/door pair). Forging piece cost £5.3 vs £7.1 incl. check (£1.9/veh per hinge). Benchmarks Denza N8 door hinge.

ideaData.title: Denza forged-aluminium door hinge with integrated check
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline door hinge is a 2-piece stamped-steel hinge plus a separate bolted door-check link. The redesign forges a single 6082 aluminium hinge that integrates the detent check ramp into the hinge body, deleting the check bracket, three bolts and 0.6 min of assembly. Forging gives the strength and fatigue life for the door-swing and sag load while consolidating the check function. Mass drops 0.5kg per hinge (1.0kg per door pair), and forging piece cost £5.3 vs £7.1 including the check (-£1.9/veh per hinge), with the integrated ramp tuned for detent feel.
ideaData.materialGrade: Forged 6082 aluminium hinge with integrated check ramp
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces a stamped-steel hinge + separate bolted check with one forged aluminium hinge; deletes the check bracket, three bolts and -0.6 min. Forging die vs stamping die; integrated ramp removes a sub-assembly and fasteners. Lighter handling; check-feel set by forged geometry rather than a separate link.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Forged aluminium hinge integrates the door check, deleting a link, bracket and bolts.

ideaData.riskNotes: Aluminium hinge fatigue and door-sag (FMVSS 206 retention) must meet steel-equivalent durability; integrated detent-ramp wear and consistent check feel over life need validation. Galvanic isolation at steel fasteners and forging porosity at the pivot bore must be controlled.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Denza N8 forged aluminium door hinge, 2023

evidenceSources.title: Forged aluminium hinge with integrated check

ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS 206 door retention; UN R11

title: Yangwang gigacast front+shock-tower mega-casting
description: Cast the entire front structure (rails, shock towers, plenum, A-pillar lower nodes) as one AlSi10MnMg gigacast, 3.5mm wall, replacing 42 parts and 200 joints. Deletes 2 weld cells, 16kg and cuts takt 19%. Piece cost £124 vs £173 (£49/veh); 8000T die NRC over 7 yr. Bolt-on crash boxes for repair. Benchmarks Yangwang U9 front casting.

ideaData.title: Yangwang gigacast front+shock-tower mega-casting
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline front structure is 42 parts (rails, shock towers, plenum, A-pillar lower nodes) joined by ~200 joints. The redesign casts the entire front as one AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) gigacasting at 3.5mm wall on an ~8000-tonne press. Bolt-on crash boxes preserve low-speed repairability at the front extremities. Consolidation removes two weld cells, 16kg and cuts takt 19%. A £173 welded assembly becomes a £124 casting (-£49/veh), the die NRC amortised over seven years, with wall geometry tuned for frontal load management.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg (EN AC-43500) front gigacast, 3.5mm wall
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces 42 parts and ~200 joints with one casting plus bolt-on crash boxes; deletes two weld cells, -16kg, takt -19%. Requires ~8000T press, vacuum die, melt handling and datum machining. Bolt-on crash boxes localise repair; high die NRC over 7 yr; minimal framing welds.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Front mega-casting consolidates rails, towers and plenum with repairable bolt-on crash boxes.

ideaData.riskNotes: Porosity/fatigue at strut/rail nodes require vacuum control and CT; the bolt-on crash-box interface must isolate damage and transfer crash load reliably. Single die is a launch/IP single-point risk; alloy ductility for frontal crash and scrap rate drive economics; repair cost vs steel must be assessed.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Yangwang U9 front gigacasting, 2024

evidenceSources.title: Repairable front gigacastings with bolt-on crash boxes

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN R94 frontal; Euro NCAP/C-NCAP; RCAR repairability

title: BMW co-extruded EPDM-TPV hybrid trunk aperture seal
description: Replace a moulded-corner EPDM trunk seal (4 vulcanised joints) with a continuous co-extruded EPDM-TPV profile using injection-moulded corners in-line, deleting 4 hand-glued joints and the corner-mould fixture (NRC −£153k). Mass −0.4kg, piece cost £4.3 vs £5.8 (£1.4/veh), 0.7 min saved. Better water-leak seal. Benchmarks BMW iX trunk seal.

ideaData.title: BMW co-extruded EPDM-TPV hybrid trunk aperture seal
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline trunk seal is a moulded-corner EPDM profile with four vulcanised (hand-glued) corner joints. The redesign uses a continuous co-extruded EPDM-TPV hybrid profile with injection-moulded corners formed in-line, deleting the four hand-glued joints and the corner-mould fixture (NRC -£153k). The hybrid combines EPDM sealing performance with TPV processability for in-line corner moulding, removing manual jointing labour (-0.7 min) and improving the water-leak seal by eliminating glued-joint leak paths. Mass -0.4kg, piece cost £4.3 vs £5.8 (-£1.4/veh).
ideaData.materialGrade: EPDM-TPV hybrid co-extrusion, in-line injection-moulded corners
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces hand-glued EPDM corner joints with in-line injection-moulded corners on a co-extruded EPDM-TPV profile; deletes four manual joints, the corner-mould fixture (NRC -£153k) and -0.7 min. Continuous closed-loop seal; lower labour and scrap, fewer leak-path joints.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: In-line moulded-corner hybrid seal removes hand-glued joints and a fixture.

ideaData.riskNotes: EPDM-TPV co-extrusion bond and in-line corner-mould adhesion must seal reliably; compression-set at corners and water-leak performance need validation. Material-transition integrity along the profile and corner-mould cycle consistency require process control.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BMW iX trunk aperture seal, 2021

evidenceSources.title: EPDM-TPV hybrid aperture seal with moulded corners

ideaData.regulatoryContext: Water-leak/dust-seal targets

title: Mercedes one-piece hot-stamped tunnel + tailored floor pan
description: Integrate the centre tunnel and front floor pans into one laser-welded tailored blank (1500MPa boron tunnel + 340LA pans) hot-stamped as a single part, replacing 5 stampings and 30 welds. Deletes 3 dies (NRC −£1.27M), 22 welds and 2.4kg overlap. Continuous seam improves NVH and sealing (−1.2 min sealer). Net −£9.3/veh. Benchmarks Mercedes EQE tunnel/floor.

ideaData.title: Mercedes one-piece hot-stamped tunnel + tailored floor pan
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline centre tunnel and front floor pans are five separate stampings joined by 30 spot welds. The redesign integrates them into one laser-welded tailored blank - a 1500MPa 22MnB5 boron tunnel zone with CR340LA pans - hot-stamped as a single part. The tailor-welded blank places high strength in the tunnel backbone and formable steel in the pans, deleting three dies (NRC -£1.27M), 22 welds and 2.4kg of overlap. The continuous laser seam improves NVH and sealing, cutting sealer application 1.2 min, netting -£9.3/veh, with strength tuned to the central load path.
ideaData.materialGrade: Laser-welded blank: 22MnB5 1500 MPa tunnel / CR340LA 340 MPa pans
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: A laser blank-welding line feeds a consolidated hot-stamp/draw die replacing five stampings and a 30-weld cell; deletes three dies (NRC -£1.27M), 22 welds and 2.4kg, with the continuous seam cutting sealer 1.2 min. Needs press-hardening for the boron tunnel; fewer panels and framing welds.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One tailor-welded tunnel+floor pan places strength by zone, cutting dies, welds and sealer.

ideaData.riskNotes: The mixed boron/340LA blank risks weld-line cracking and formability mismatch in a deep tunnel draw; seam position vs crash/NVH load path needs CAE validation. The large consolidated panel raises scrap value and press-tonnage demand; continuous-seam sealing must be leak-verified.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes EQE tunnel/floor tailor-welded structure, 2022
ideaData.regulatoryContext: Euro NCAP frontal/side; UN R94/R95; UN R100

title: Xpeng PA66-GF30 panoramic glass retention frame
description: Replace an aluminium panoramic-roof glass retention frame plus EPDM seal with an injection-moulded PA66-GF30 frame with a co-moulded TPE seal lip in one shot, bonded to the laminate. Deletes the metal frame, 8 clips and a separate seal (£3.5), saving 1.3kg. Piece cost £9.5 vs £13.3 (£3.7/veh), tool NRC £289k. Improves flush and acoustic seal. Benchmarks Xpeng G6 panoramic roof frame.

ideaData.title: Xpeng PA66-GF30 panoramic glass retention frame
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline panoramic-roof glass retention frame is aluminium plus a separate EPDM seal. The redesign injection-moulds a PA66-GF30 (30% glass-filled polyamide) frame with a co-moulded TPE seal lip in one shot, bonded to the laminate. This deletes the metal frame, eight clips and the separate seal (£3.5) and sheds 1.3kg. The two-shot (hard PA66-GF30 + soft TPE lip) part integrates structure and sealing, improving flush appearance and acoustic sealing of the panoramic glass. Piece cost £9.5 vs £13.3 (-£3.7/veh), tool NRC £289k.
ideaData.materialGrade: PA66-GF30 frame + co-moulded TPE seal lip (2K), on glass laminate
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: A 2K (hard PA66-GF30 + soft TPE lip) injection mould replaces an aluminium frame + EPDM seal + eight clips, bonded to the laminate; deletes the metal frame, clips and separate seal. Tool NRC £289k. Module supplied with glass, cutting plant parts; adds 2K moulding and glass-bond control.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: 2K glass-filled-nylon frame with co-moulded seal consolidates frame, clips and seal.

ideaData.riskNotes: PA66-GF30-to-glass and PA66-to-TPE 2K bond durability under UV/thermal cycling must be validated; glass-filled-nylon CTE and moisture uptake affect flush over climate. Acoustic/water seal of the co-moulded lip and warpage of the large 2K part need mould and bond-process control.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Xpeng G6 panoramic roof glass frame, 2023

evidenceSources.title: Xpeng G6 panoramic roof frame teardown

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN R43 glazing; acoustic/water-leak targets

title: LFP blade cell migration from NMC622 prismatic
description: Replace NMC622 prismatic 120Ah cells with LFP blade cells (960mm long, 13.5mm thick). LFP eliminates cobalt and nickel, cutting cell cost from ~£89/kWh to ~£61/kWh. The blade form factor enables direct cell-to-pack stacking, removing module hardware. Energy density loss (160 vs 220 Wh/kg) is offset by 18% volumetric packaging gain via CTP integration. At 90kWh fleet this saves ~£28/kWh. Benchmarks BYD Han EV blade architecture.

ideaData.title: LFP blade cell migration from NMC622 prismatic
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline is a 120Ah NMC622 prismatic cell (polycrystalline LiNi0.6Mn0.2Co0.2O2, graphite anode, ~220 Wh/kg) in welded modules. The change adopts LFP (LiFePO4) Blade cells, 960mm x 13.5mm x ~90mm, with a long-thin aluminium can welded and structurally bonded directly into a cell-to-pack tray. The olivine LFP lattice is cobalt- and nickel-free, cutting cathode commodity cost from ~£89 to ~£61/kWh. The elongated can lets the cell act as a structural cross-member, deleting module endplates and side rails. Lower 160 Wh/kg energy is recovered by an 18% volumetric packaging gain from CTP integration. Cells laser-welded and PU-adhesive bonded into the blade array.
ideaData.materialGrade: LiFePO4 (LFP) blade cell vs LiNi0.6Mn0.2Co0.2O2 (NMC622)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes module shell assembly: endplates, side rails, module busbars and hundreds of fasteners removed via direct cell-to-pack stacking. Blade cells indexed and adhesive-bonded into the tray on one line, cutting module sub-assembly stations. Long-format laser welding replaces per-module wire bonding, lowering takt and weld count; handling fixtures redesigned for 960mm cells.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Cobalt/nickel-free LFP plus module-hardware deletion via CTP

ideaData.riskNotes: LFP cold-temperature power fade and flat OCV curve complicate SOC estimation; mitigate with improved BMS algorithms and bottom-cooling pre-heat. Lower energy density risks range targets, offset by CTP volumetric gain. Long blade dimensional control and adhesive bond-line integrity need fixturing and pull-test validation.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD Han EV Blade CTP architecture, 2020; teardown-confirmed 2021

evidenceSources.title: BYD Blade pack cell-to-pack teardown analysis
BYD Blade Battery launch technical briefing

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN ECE R100.03 and GB 38031-2020 thermal-runaway no-propagation

title: Single-crystal NMC811 cathode for cycle-life uprate
description: Switch polycrystalline NMC811 to single-crystal cathode morphology. Single-crystal grains resist micro-cracking, extending cycle life from 1500 to 2800 cycles and removing the need for 8% oversizing that compensated for capacity fade. Eliminating the oversize buffer reduces active material per pack by ~6kg, saving ~£15/kWh. Lower gassing also permits thinner cell can walls. Benchmarks Nio 100kWh cell upgrade using single-crystal high-nickel cathode.

ideaData.title: Single-crystal NMC811 cathode for cycle-life uprate
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline NMC811 (LiNi0.8Mn0.1Co0.1O2) uses polycrystalline secondary particles (agglomerated ~10µm spheres) that micro-crack along grain boundaries during cycling, accelerating fade and gassing and forcing ~8% capacity oversizing. The change calcines the cathode to single-crystal morphology (1-3µm monolithic grains) that resists intergranular cracking, extending life from ~1500 to ~2800 cycles. Removing the oversize buffer cuts ~6kg active material per pack (~£15/kWh). Reduced gas evolution lowers can-wall stress, permitting thinner prismatic can stock. Achieved via modified lithiation ratio and higher single-step sintering, with an Al2O3/borate surface coating stabilizing the high-nickel surface.
ideaData.materialGrade: Single-crystal LiNi0.8Mn0.1Co0.1O2 (sc-NMC811)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Cathode calcination temperature and dwell increase on the existing coating/calendering line. Pack bill shrinks ~6kg active material with marginally shorter cell stack. Thinner can wall enables lighter deep-draw stamping. No new assembly stations; formation cycling validated longer initially. Cell-can deep-draw tooling adjusts to the thinner wall gauge.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Delete capacity oversizing buffer via longer cycle life

ideaData.riskNotes: Single-crystal cathodes have lower rate capability and harder calendering (particle-fracture risk under high pressure); mitigate with optimized porosity and binder. High-nickel surface remains moisture-sensitive — dry-room control retained. Longer cycle-life claim needs accelerated-aging validation before deleting oversize buffer.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Nio 100kWh pack single-crystal high-nickel cell upgrade, 2021

title: Silicon-carbon anode to shrink cell count
description: Introduce 6% silicon-carbon composite anode to lift cell capacity from 4.2Ah to 4.9Ah in 4680 cells. Higher gravimetric capacity (470 vs 360 mAh/g blend) cuts cell count per pack by 14%, removing ~120 cells, busbars, and welds per vehicle. Net BOM saving ~£47/vehicle on interconnects plus assembly time. Pre-lithiation manages first-cycle loss. Benchmarks Porsche/Group silicon anode cell development with Cellforce.

ideaData.title: Silicon-carbon anode to shrink cell count
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline 4680 cells use a graphite anode (~360 mAh/g blend) at 4.2Ah. The change blends 6% silicon-carbon composite (Si embedded in carbon matrix) into the anode, raising blended capacity to ~470 mAh/g and cell capacity to 4.9Ah. The ~17% capacity uplift cuts cell count per pack ~14% (~120 fewer cells), removing their busbars, laser welds and holders — ~£47/vehicle of interconnect and assembly. Silicon's ~300% volumetric swelling is managed by the carbon scaffold, an engineered void structure, an FEC/VC electrolyte for a stable SEI, and pre-lithiation to offset the high first-cycle irreversible loss. Anode coating uses the existing slurry line with revised calendering.
ideaData.materialGrade: 6% silicon-carbon composite anode (Si/C, ~470 mAh/g) in 4680
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Adds a pre-lithiation step and tighter calendering control for the swelling-prone anode; same coating line. Pack assembly simplified by ~120 fewer cells: fewer laser-weld cycles, busbars and holders, shortening module/CTP takt. Formation protocol extended for SEI stabilization. Dry-room humidity control and slurry mixing parameters re-tuned for the silicon fraction.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Higher cell capacity shrinks cell count and interconnect content

ideaData.riskNotes: Silicon swelling drives mechanical fatigue and SEI regrowth, risking cycle-life and gassing; mitigate with void-engineered Si/C, pre-lithiation, and stack pre-load. Pre-lithiation adds process safety/handling risk. Calendering window narrow. Requires extensive abuse and cycle validation.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Porsche/Cellforce silicon-anode high-performance cell development, 2023

title: Sodium-ion entry cells for base-trim pack
description: Deploy sodium-ion cylindrical cells on the entry-trim 45kWh pack. Sodium-ion uses abundant Na and aluminium current collectors on both electrodes, removing copper foil (~£3.4/kWh) and cobalt entirely, targeting ~£55/kWh. Excellent low-temperature retention (90% at -20°C) reduces heater duty. Lower energy density (145 Wh/kg) is acceptable for short-range trim. Benchmarks BYD/CATL sodium-ion pack programs entering city EVs in 2024–2025.

ideaData.title: Sodium-ion entry cells for base-trim pack
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline entry 45kWh pack uses LFP cylindrical cells with copper anode foil. The change deploys sodium-ion cylindrical cells: a layered-oxide or Prussian-white cathode with a hard-carbon anode and aluminium current collectors on BOTH electrodes (Na does not alloy with Al), eliminating copper foil (~£3.4/kWh) and all cobalt, targeting ~£55/kWh. Na-ion retains ~90% capacity at -20°C, cutting heater duty and improving winter charge. Energy density is ~145 Wh/kg, acceptable for a short-range base trim. Cells can be discharged to 0V for safe transport. Manufactured on existing cylindrical lines with minimal retooling versus Li-ion.
ideaData.materialGrade: Sodium-ion (layered-oxide / Prussian-white cathode, hard-carbon anode, Al collectors)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Drops the copper foil supply stream; both electrodes coat onto aluminium, simplifying procurement. Runs on existing cylindrical winding/assembly lines with adjusted electrolyte and formation. Pack thermal/heater hardware downsized due to superior low-temperature performance. Zero-volt transport eases logistics handling. BMS calibration and formation recipes updated for the sodium-ion voltage profile.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Abundant sodium and copper-free, cobalt-free chemistry

ideaData.riskNotes: Lower energy density constrains to base trim only; cathode cycle-life and air-sensitivity (layered oxides) still maturing. Supply base nascent — dual-source risk. Validate calendar aging and fast-charge. BMS recalibrated for Na-ion OCV curve. Cross-trim commonisation limited.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD/CATL sodium-ion city-EV pack programs, 2024-2025
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN 38.3 transport; GB 38031 pack safety

title: Composite copper-PET current collector foil
description: Replace 6µm rolled copper anode foil with composite collector: 4.5µm PET film sputtered with 1µm copper on each side. Copper mass drops ~60%, saving ~£3/kWh in commodity content, and the polymer core acts as a fusible safety layer that interrupts internal shorts. Lower foil weight adds ~5 Wh/kg. Roll-to-roll plating scales to GWh. Benchmarks Zeekr cells trialling composite current collectors for energy density and safety.

ideaData.title: Composite copper-PET current collector foil
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline anode current collector is 6µm rolled copper foil. The change is a composite collector: a 4.5µm PET (or PP) polymer film magnetron-sputtered then electroplated with ~1µm copper on each face. Copper mass drops ~60% (~£3/kWh commodity), and the polymer core acts as a fusible barrier — under an internal short it melts and retracts the thin metal layer, interrupting current and improving safety. Reduced foil mass adds ~5 Wh/kg. Manufactured roll-to-roll (sputter seed + plate) scalable to GWh. Tab welding requires ultrasonic/laser parameters tuned for the thin metal-on-polymer stack.
ideaData.materialGrade: Composite Cu-PET current collector (4.5µm PET + 1µm Cu/side)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Introduces roll-to-roll sputter-seed plus electroplating for foil production. Cell winding largely unchanged but tab-welding parameters retuned for the metal/polymer/metal sandwich; transfer-welding or pre-tabbing may be needed. Improves yield safety margins via fusing behavior. Foil tension and web-handling controls added for the delicate composite stock, with inline thickness gauging.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: 60% copper reduction plus inherent short-circuit fusing
costSavingPotential.calculationBasis: ~£3/kWh x pack kWh x program volume

ideaData.riskNotes: Thin metal-on-polymer is delicate: handling tears, higher sheet resistance, and tab-weld reliability are key risks; mitigate with optimized plating thickness and welding process. Long-term interfacial adhesion and high-rate ohmic loss need validation. Supply maturity limited.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Zeekr composite current-collector cell trials, 2023

evidenceSources.title: Composite polymer-metal current collector for Li-ion cells
Composite current collector safety and cost review

title: Ceramic-coated polyolefin separator downgauge
description: Move from 16µm PE separator with 4µm double-side alumina coat to a 9µm PE base with 2µm boehmite single-side coat. Thinner, lighter separator reclaims ~3% jelly-roll volume for active material and cuts separator cost ~£1.5/kWh. Boehmite is cheaper than alumina and maintains 150°C shutdown integrity. Validated for thermal-runaway propagation resistance. Benchmarks Zeekr golden-brick LFP cell separator specification.

ideaData.title: Ceramic-coated polyolefin separator downgauge
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline separator is a 16µm polyethylene (PE) membrane with 4µm double-sided alumina (Al2O3) ceramic coating. The change moves to a 9µm PE base with a 2µm single-sided boehmite (AlOOH) coat. The thinner, lighter separator reclaims ~3% jelly-roll volume for active material and cuts separator cost ~£1.5/kWh. Boehmite is cheaper than alpha-alumina yet maintains dimensional stability and ~150°C shutdown integrity, preserving thermal-runaway propagation resistance. Single-side coating simplifies the coating pass. Wettability and puncture strength validated; thinner base raises ionic conductivity slightly, aiding fast charge.
ideaData.materialGrade: 9µm PE separator + 2µm boehmite (AlOOH) coat vs 16µm PE + alumina
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Single-side boehmite coating replaces double-side alumina, halving a coating pass and ceramic consumption. Thinner film increases winding-tension sensitivity, needing converting-line control. Reclaimed volume allows marginally more active material per cell at equal can size. Slitting and inspection parameters re-set for the thinner web, with pinhole detection added.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Downgauge separator and cheaper boehmite coating
costSavingPotential.calculationBasis: ~£1.5/kWh x pack kWh x program volume

ideaData.riskNotes: Thinner separator lowers puncture/short margin and dendrite tolerance; mitigate with boehmite mechanical reinforcement and validated shutdown testing. Winding tension and pin-hole control critical. Single-side coat orientation must face the correct electrode. Propagation testing mandatory.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Zeekr Golden Brick LFP cell separator specification, 2023
ideaData.regulatoryContext: GB 38031 / UN R100 thermal propagation

title: Dry-electrode coating to remove NMP solvent
description: Adopt solvent-free dry-electrode calendering (PTFE-fibrillated binder) for cathode coating, eliminating NMP slurry, the 80m drying ovens, and solvent-recovery plant. Energy per cell falls ~30% and capex for coating lines drops ~£12.75M amortised to ~£6/kWh. Thicker electrodes (>4 mAh/cm²) also raise energy density. Yields stabilising in 2024 pilots. Benchmarks Tesla 4680 dry-cathode rollout informing Group cell strategy.

ideaData.title: Dry-electrode coating to remove NMP solvent
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline cathode coating is a wet process: active material slurried in N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP) solvent, coated, then dried in ~80m ovens with solvent recovery. The change is solvent-free dry-electrode processing: cathode powder dry-mixed with PTFE binder that is sheared into fibrils, then calendered into a self-supporting film laminated to the foil. Eliminating NMP removes the long drying ovens, solvent-recovery plant and ~30% process energy, amortising to ~£6/kWh. Dry films also support thicker electrodes (>4 mAh/cm²), raising energy density and reducing inactive mass. PTFE fibrillation and lamination tension are the critical control parameters.
ideaData.materialGrade: PTFE-fibrillated dry cathode (NMC/LFP) vs NMP-slurry electrode
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes the 80m drying ovens and solvent-recovery/abatement plant — major capex and footprint cut. Replaces coating head with dry-mixing, fibrillation and calender-lamination stations. Cuts process energy ~30% and line length sharply; yield ramp is the gating risk versus mature wet lines.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Eliminate NMP, drying ovens and solvent recovery
costSavingPotential.calculationBasis: ~£6/kWh x pack kWh x program volume

ideaData.riskNotes: Dry-electrode yield, film uniformity and thickness control are immature; defects cause capacity scatter. PTFE can reduce at low anode potentials (anode adoption harder). Mitigate by starting with cathode, tight fibrillation control, and pilot-line qualification before high-volume cutover.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Tesla 4680 dry-cathode rollout informing VW Group cell strategy, 2023

evidenceSources.title: Solvent-free electrode fabrication via PTFE fibrillation

title: LMFP cathode blend for range without nickel
description: Upgrade LFP to LMFP (lithium manganese iron phosphate, ~Mn0.6Fe0.4) lifting cell voltage from 3.2V to 3.7V and energy density ~15% to 200 Wh/kg. Keeps the cobalt/nickel-free cost base (~£63/kWh) while closing range gap, avoiding a switch to costly NMC. Fewer cells needed for equal kWh trims interconnect content. Manganese dissolution mitigated by surface coating. Benchmarks Zeekr/CATL M3P-style manganese-phosphate cells.

ideaData.title: LMFP cathode blend for range without nickel
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline is LFP (LiFePO4) at 3.2V nominal, ~170 Wh/kg. The change is LMFP (LiMn0.6Fe0.4PO4), where partial manganese substitution raises the redox plateau to ~3.7V/4.1V and lifts energy density ~15% to ~200 Wh/kg, while keeping the cobalt/nickel-free phosphate cost base (~£63/kWh). The range gain avoids switching to costly NMC. Higher cell energy reduces cell count for an equal-kWh trim, trimming interconnect content. Manganese dissolution at the cathode (and Mn deposition on the anode) is mitigated by a carbon/oxide surface coating and tuned electrolyte additives. Drop-in compatible with existing LFP coating lines.
ideaData.materialGrade: LiMn0.6Fe0.4PO4 (LMFP) vs LiFePO4 (LFP)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Drop-in on existing LFP electrode lines with revised precursor (Mn-Fe phosphate) and surface coating; mixing and calcination retuned. Cell format unchanged. Slightly higher voltage allows fewer cells in series for a given pack voltage, marginally simplifying interconnects. Surface-coating and electrolyte-additive dosing stations added to mitigate manganese dissolution.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: +15% energy at LFP cost base, avoiding NMC switch
costSavingPotential.calculationBasis: NMC-avoidance + interconnect saving x program volume

ideaData.riskNotes: Manganese dissolution degrades cycle life and can poison the anode SEI; mitigate with surface coating and additive electrolyte. Lower intrinsic conductivity than LFP needs nano-sizing/carbon coating. Validate calendar and high-temperature cycling before fleet rollout.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Zeekr/CATL M3P manganese-phosphate cells, 2023

evidenceSources.title: CATL M3P / Zeekr LMFP cell briefing

title: Tabless 4680 electrode for fast-charge yield
description: Convert 21700 cells to tabless 4680 format. The full-height continuous current path cuts internal resistance ~5x, slashing I²R heat and allowing the thermal system to shed one chiller stage. Larger format halves cell count per pack, removing ~50% of welds, wire bonds, and holder parts, saving ~£34/vehicle in interconnects. Cell-level energy density rises via reduced inactive mass. Benchmarks BMW Gen6 large cylindrical cell program.

ideaData.title: Tabless 4680 electrode for fast-charge yield
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline is 21700 cylindrical cells with discrete welded tabs, where current travels the full electrode length to a single tab, raising internal resistance and I²R heat. The change is the tabless 4680 format (46mm dia x 80mm): the electrode foil is laser-patterned and folded so the entire edge forms a continuous current collector, cutting internal resistance ~5x. Lower resistance slashes heat generation, allowing deletion of one chiller stage. The larger format roughly halves cell count per pack, removing ~50% of welds, wire bonds and holders (~£34/vehicle). Reduced inactive mass and tabless design also raise cell-level energy density.
ideaData.materialGrade: Tabless 4680 cylindrical cell vs tabbed 21700
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Requires new large-format winding/jelly-roll tooling and continuous-edge (tabless) current-collector welding. Pack assembly simplified: ~50% fewer cells, welds, wire bonds and holders, cutting takt. Thermal system sheds one chiller stage. Cell-can deep-draw stamping retooled to 46mm. Larger format also changes cell-handling, tray-indexing and bottom-cooling interface fixtures.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Tabless low-resistance large cell cuts cell count and thermal hardware

ideaData.riskNotes: Tabless current-collector forming and full-edge welding are process-intensive with yield risk; large-format thermal gradients must be managed despite lower resistance. Mitigate with bottom/side cooling and robust weld inspection. Validate fast-charge thermal and mechanical abuse.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BMW Gen6 (Neue Klasse) large-cylindrical 4695 cell program, 2025

evidenceSources.title: BMW Gen6 large round cell announcement

title: Cell vent valve laser-scored membrane
description: Replace the riveted burst-disc vent on prismatic cells with a laser-scored aluminium membrane integrated into the cell lid stamping. Eliminates a separate disc, gasket, and crimp operation (3 parts, 1 station) at ~£0.2/cell, scaling to meaningful savings at 8M cells/year. Scoring depth tuned for 1.0MPa burst keeps directional venting away from neighbours. Benchmarks BYD blade cell integrated top-vent design.

ideaData.title: Cell vent valve laser-scored membrane
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline prismatic cells use a riveted burst-disc vent: a separate aluminium disc, gasket and crimp/rivet operation assembled into the cell lid. The change laser-scores a thin aluminium membrane directly into the cell-lid stamping, removing 3 parts (disc, gasket, rivet) and one assembly station at ~£0.2/cell — meaningful at ~8M cells/year. The score depth is tuned for a 1.0MPa burst pressure with a directional rupture pattern that vents ejecta away from neighbouring cells. Scoring is done in-line on the lid stamping with a controlled-depth laser, with vision inspection of residual wall thickness for repeatability.
ideaData.materialGrade: Laser-scored aluminium cell-lid membrane (e.g. Al 3003) vs riveted burst disc
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Integrates the vent into the existing lid-stamping flow with an added laser-scoring and vision-inspection step, deleting a separate disc/gasket/crimp station and its parts feeding. Lowers part count, BOM and assembly faults. Score-depth process control is the key new parameter. Parts feeding and the rivet/crimp station are removed from the lid line layout.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Integrate vent into lid stamping, delete disc/gasket/rivet

ideaData.riskNotes: Score-depth tolerance directly sets burst pressure — too deep risks premature venting/leak, too shallow risks over-pressure; mitigate with closed-loop laser depth control and 100% vision check. Validate directional venting and long-term fatigue/corrosion of the thinned membrane.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD Blade cell integrated top-vent design, 2020

evidenceSources.title: BYD Blade cell lid and vent teardown

ideaData.regulatoryContext: GB 38031 / UN R100 cell venting

title: Wire-bond to laser-weld busbar interconnect
description: Replace aluminium wire-bonding of cylindrical cells with laser-welded stamped busbars and a fusible nickel link. Wire bonding runs ~1.2s per bond across thousands of bonds; stamped busbar welding processes a full row in one shot, cutting takt ~40% and removing the bonder consumables. Busbar also lowers interconnect resistance. Saves ~£7.6/module in cycle time and scrap. Benchmarks Tesla structural pack busbar welding adopted by Volvo cell lines.

ideaData.title: Wire-bond to laser-weld busbar interconnect
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline interconnect uses aluminium wire-bonding of cylindrical cells, ~1.2s per bond across thousands of bonds, with the bond wire doubling as a fuse. The change uses laser-welded stamped busbars with a fusible nickel link spot. A formed busbar is laid over a cell row and laser-welded in a single multi-spot shot, processing the whole row at once and cutting interconnect takt ~40% while removing bond-wire consumables. The thicker busbar lowers interconnect resistance and I²R loss; the engineered nickel neck provides per-cell overcurrent protection. Saves ~£7.6/module in cycle time and reduced scrap. Requires busbar stamping tooling and weld fixturing.
ideaData.materialGrade: Stamped Cu/Ni busbar laser-weld vs Al wire bond
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces wire-bonders with stamped-busbar feeding and a laser-weld cell. Row-at-once welding cuts interconnect takt ~40% and removes bond-wire consumables and bonder maintenance. Adds busbar stamping tooling and weld-quality (penetration) inspection. Lower interconnect resistance. Row fixturing and busbar flatness control are added to ensure consistent weld penetration.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Row-at-once busbar welding replaces per-bond wire bonding

ideaData.riskNotes: Laser weld-through to the cell can risks venting if penetration is mis-set; mitigate with calibrated power, anti-reflective treatment and weld-pull testing. Fusible-link calibration must clear a shorted cell reliably. Busbar flatness/contact pressure affects weld quality.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Tesla structural-pack busbar welding adopted on Volvo cell lines, 2022

title: Module endplate from glass-filled PA to HPDC
description: Switch the two module compression endplates from machined 6082 aluminium to high-pressure die-cast ADC12. Casting nets net-shape features (locating bosses, tie-rod seats) in one shot, eliminating 6 machining operations and 30% material scrap. Per-endplate cost falls from ~£6.3 to ~£3.5. Stiffness maintained via ribbing for cell swelling preload of 4kN. Benchmarks Mercedes EQ module endplate casting conversion.

ideaData.title: Module endplate from glass-filled PA to HPDC
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline module compression endplates are machined from 6082 aluminium billet, requiring multiple milling operations and generating ~30% scrap. The change die-casts them from ADC12 (AlSi11Cu, ~228 MPa UTS) high-pressure die-cast alloy, netting locating bosses, tie-rod seats and ribbing in one shot. Per-endplate cost falls from ~£6.3 to ~£3.5. The cast geometry maintains stiffness to hold the ~4kN cell-swelling preload via optimized ribbing rather than solid section. Six machining operations are eliminated; only critical sealing/datum faces are finish-machined. HPDC tooling amortised over module volume.
ideaData.materialGrade: ADC12 (AlSi11Cu) HPDC vs machined 6082-T6 aluminium
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Moves from billet machining to net-shape HPDC, eliminating ~6 milling operations and ~30% material scrap per endplate; only datum/seal faces finish-machined. Adds die-cast tooling. Integrated bosses and tie-rod seats reduce secondary features. Casting porosity control needed at preload load paths.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Net-shape casting replaces billet machining

ideaData.riskNotes: HPDC porosity can weaken tie-rod seats under 4kN preload and cyclic swelling fatigue; mitigate with gating/vacuum-assist casting, X-ray inspection and ribbing. Datum-face flatness from casting needs finish machining. Validate creep under sustained preload.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes EQ module endplate casting conversion, 2022

title: Foam-in-place cell spacer for swelling control
description: Replace die-cut PU compression pads between prismatic cells with dispensed foam-in-place 2K polyurethane. FIP eliminates pad tooling, manual pick-and-place, and 12% positional scrap, while delivering tuned compression curves for 350µm of charge swelling. Robotic dispense cuts ~£2.6/module versus pre-cut pads. Foam doubles as a minor thermal break. Benchmarks Li Auto module assembly using dispensed compression foam.

ideaData.title: Foam-in-place cell spacer for swelling control
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline uses die-cut polyurethane (PU) compression pads placed by hand between prismatic cells to absorb ~350µm of charge-swelling and maintain preload. The change dispenses foam-in-place (FIP) 2K polyurethane robotically onto the cell face, which expands and cures to a tuned compression curve. FIP eliminates pad die-cut tooling, manual pick-and-place, and ~12% positional/edge scrap, saving ~£2.6/module. The dispensed foam conforms to cell-surface tolerance, gives a programmable spring rate for swelling management, and provides a minor thermal break between cells. Robotic dispense integrates into the stacking station with mix-ratio and bead-path control.
ideaData.materialGrade: 2K dispensed polyurethane (FIP) vs die-cut PU compression pad
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces pad die-cut tooling and manual placement with a robotic 2K dispense station inline at cell stacking, removing ~12% pad scrap and a manual operation. Adds mix-meter dispense and cure dwell; bead geometry programmable per cell, easing changeover. Cure time gates stack throughput.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Dispensed foam removes pad tooling and manual placement

ideaData.riskNotes: Foam density/compression curve depends on mix ratio, temperature and cure — drift risks wrong preload and cell stress; mitigate with closed-loop dispense metering and durometer sampling. Outgassing and adhesion to cell film validated. Rework (foam removal) harder than swapping a pad.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Li Auto module assembly dispensed compression foam, 2022

title: Module-to-CTP conversion eliminating module shell
description: Adopt cell-to-pack (CTP) architecture, deleting the intermediate module layer: endplates, side rails, module busbars, wiring harnesses and fasteners. Cells bond directly into the tray with structural adhesive. Volumetric efficiency rises from 55% to 72%, and part count drops ~40% (~360 fewer parts/pack), saving ~£47/vehicle in hardware and assembly. Benchmarks BYD Blade CTP and CATL Qilin pack architecture.

ideaData.title: Module-to-CTP conversion eliminating module shell
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline is a modular pack: cells grouped into modules with endplates, side rails, module busbars, intra-module harnesses and fasteners, then modules bolted into the tray. The change adopts cell-to-pack (CTP): the module layer is deleted and cells are bonded directly into the tray with structural adhesive, with long busbars spanning the array. Volumetric efficiency rises from ~55% to ~72% and part count drops ~40% (~360 fewer parts/pack), saving ~£47/vehicle in hardware and assembly. The tray and cross-beams provide the mechanical constraint formerly given by module shells; bottom cooling contacts cells directly. Requires CTP line tooling and adhesive dispense/cure.
ideaData.materialGrade: Structural-adhesive cell-to-pack (LFP/NMC cells) vs modular pack
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Removes the entire module sub-assembly line: endplate/side-rail welding, module busbar and harness stations. Cells indexed and adhesive-bonded straight into the tray, with long-busbar laser welding. ~360 fewer parts/pack cut assembly takt ~40%; adhesive dispense/cure and cell-placement accuracy become critical stations.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Delete module layer, +17pt volumetric efficiency

ideaData.riskNotes: Adhesive bonding makes cell-level service/recycling harder and bond-line integrity safety-critical; mitigate with validated structural adhesive, pull-testing and serviceable design zones. Thermal-runaway propagation without module walls needs inter-cell barriers. Cell placement tolerance stack-up validated.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD Blade CTP and CATL Qilin CTP pack architecture, 2022

evidenceSources.title: CATL Qilin / BYD Blade CTP pack teardown

ideaData.regulatoryContext: GB 38031 no-propagation; UN ECE R100.03

title: Cell-to-body integration of pack into floor
description: Move from cell-to-pack to cell-to-body (CTB), where cells bond to a sealed floor that doubles as the pack upper cover and structural body member. Removes the separate pack lid and a redundant sealing interface, and the body floor replaces the pack top plate, saving ~£41/vehicle in panels and 14kg. Torsional stiffness rises ~30%. FIPG silicone seals the cell array to the body. Benchmarks BYD CTB Seal platform.

ideaData.title: Cell-to-body integration of pack into floor
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline is cell-to-pack with a separate pack upper cover plus the vehicle floor above it — a redundant panel and sealing interface. The change is cell-to-body (CTB): cells bond to a sealed tray whose top surface IS the vehicle floor, so the body floor doubles as the pack upper cover and a structural body member. This deletes the separate pack lid and one sealing interface, saving ~£41/vehicle in panels and ~14kg, while torsional stiffness rises ~30% from the sandwich (floor + cell array + tray). A FIPG silicone bead seals the cell array to the body. Integration is at body-in-white/general assembly rather than a standalone pack.
ideaData.materialGrade: CTB sealed floor (Al/steel) + structural adhesive cell bonding
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Merges pack closure with body floor, deleting a pack-lid panel, its stamping/sealing and a leak-test interface. Cell array bonded and FIPG-sealed to the body floor — shifts integration into body/GA flow and changes line sequencing. Sealing-bead and bond quality become vehicle-level critical operations.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Floor doubles as pack cover, deleting a panel and seal

ideaData.riskNotes: Integrating the pack into the body complicates crash sealing, NVH and especially battery service/replacement; mitigate with robust FIPG sealing, validated joint and a defined service strategy. Tolerance stack between body and cell array tightens. Repairability and recall logistics are key concerns.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD CTB (Cell-to-Body) Seal platform, 2022
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN ECE R100.03; GB 38031; crash R94/R95

title: HPDC ADC12 single-piece pack tray
description: Consolidate the welded aluminium-extrusion-and-stamping tray (38 parts) into a single high-pressure die-cast ADC12 underbody tray. Megacasting removes ~120 weld joints and their leak-test rework, cutting tray assembly labour ~50% and saving ~£60/vehicle. Integrated cooling channels and mounting bosses cast in net-shape. Requires a 6000T press. Benchmarks Zeekr/Tesla gigacast underbody and battery tray integration.

ideaData.title: HPDC ADC12 single-piece pack tray
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline tray is a welded assembly of ~38 aluminium extrusions and stampings joined by MIG/friction-stir welds with ~120 weld joints needing leak test and rework. The change consolidates it into a single high-pressure die-cast ADC12 (AlSi11Cu) underbody tray on a ~6000-tonne press (megacasting). Casting nets cooling channels, mounting bosses and cross-ribbing in net-shape, removing ~120 weld joints and their leak-test rework, cutting tray assembly labour ~50% and saving ~£60/vehicle. The single casting improves dimensional consistency. Requires giga-press capacity, large dies and post-cast trimming/CNC of interface faces.
ideaData.materialGrade: ADC12 (AlSi11Cu) megacast tray vs welded Al extrusion/stamping
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces a multi-part weld/braze tray line with one giga-press casting plus trim and CNC of datums. Removes ~120 welds and the leak-test/rework loop, cutting tray labour ~50%. Requires 6000T press, large die tooling and casting-quality (porosity/X-ray) control; reduces WIP and floor space.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One megacasting replaces 38 welded parts

ideaData.riskNotes: Megacasting porosity and repairability are key risks — a damaged casting is hard to repair vs replacing one extrusion. Mitigate with vacuum-assist HPDC, X-ray inspection and casting alloy selection. Large die capex demands volume amortisation; thermal-channel integrity leak-tested.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Zeekr/Tesla gigacast underbody and battery tray integration, 2022

title: Steel-intensive tray for cost-down base pack
description: Replace the aluminium extrusion tray with a roll-formed and stamped dual-phase steel (DP780) enclosure on volume trims. Steel BOM is ~£1.8/kg versus ~£2.9/kg for aluminium and uses existing body-shop spot-welding rather than friction-stir, saving ~£32/vehicle and capex. ~22kg mass penalty accepted on base range. Cathodic e-coat handles corrosion. Benchmarks Volvo/Geely SEA steel-hybrid pack enclosures.

ideaData.title: Steel-intensive tray for cost-down base pack
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline volume-trim tray is friction-stir-welded aluminium extrusion. The change uses a roll-formed and stamped dual-phase steel (DP780, ~780 MPa UTS) enclosure on volume trims, joined by conventional body-shop resistance spot welding instead of friction-stir. Steel BOM is ~£1.8/kg versus ~£2.9/kg for aluminium, and reusing existing spot-weld lines avoids FSW capex, saving ~£32/vehicle. A ~22kg mass penalty is accepted on base range trims. Cathodic e-coat (CED) provides corrosion protection on the steel enclosure. DP780 gives crash strength for side-pole intrusion at lower material and process cost.
ideaData.materialGrade: DP780 dual-phase steel vs 6xxx aluminium extrusion
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Shifts tray fabrication from FSW/extrusion to roll-forming, stamping and resistance spot welding on existing body-shop lines, avoiding dedicated FSW capex. Adds CED e-coat for corrosion. Heavier handling but mature, high-rate joining. Commonises tooling with body shop. Seam-sealing and adhesive application added at spot-weld flanges for IP67 integrity.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Cheaper steel BOM on existing spot-weld lines

ideaData.riskNotes: ~22kg mass penalty hurts range and efficiency; restrict to base trims. Steel corrosion risk in a wet underbody mitigated by CED and sealing. Spot-weld sealing for IP67 needs adhesive/seam sealing. Galvanic isolation from aluminium components required.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo/Geely SEA steel-hybrid pack enclosure, 2022

evidenceSources.title: Steel vs aluminium pack enclosure cost study

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN ECE R100; side-impact R95

title: FIPG silicone replacing cured-gasket pack seal
description: Switch the pack lid sealing from a molded EPDM gasket to robot-dispensed Formed-In-Place-Gasket (FIPG) silicone. FIPG removes the molded-gasket tool, eliminates groove machining on the flange, and adapts to flange tolerance, cutting IP67 leak-test failures ~35%. Material and labour net ~£3.6/pack saving. Re-openability via cut-and-reapply. Benchmarks Mercedes pack sealing line using dispensed silicone bead.

ideaData.title: FIPG silicone replacing cured-gasket pack seal
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline pack-lid sealing uses a molded EPDM gasket seated in a machined flange groove. The change uses robot-dispensed Formed-In-Place-Gasket (FIPG) silicone (RTV cure-in-place bead) on the flange. FIPG removes the molded-gasket tool and the flange-groove machining, and the dispensed bead conforms to flange tolerance, cutting IP67 leak-test failures ~35%. Net material and labour save ~£3.6/pack. The bead is dispensed on a programmable path matching the flange and cures to a compressible seal; re-openability is via cut-and-reapply at service. Integrates into the lid-close station with bead-presence vision check.
ideaData.materialGrade: RTV silicone FIPG vs molded EPDM gasket
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes molded-gasket tooling and flange-groove machining; adds a robotic silicone dispense and bead-vision station before lid close. Adapts to flange tolerance, cutting IP67 leak failures ~35% and rework. Cure dwell and bead-path control are new parameters; changeover is software-driven. Service re-sealing procedure and cure verification are added to the process plan.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Dispensed seal removes gasket tool and groove machining

ideaData.riskNotes: FIPG cure state and bead continuity drive seal integrity; voids cause IP67 leaks — mitigate with vision inspection, controlled dispense and cure monitoring. Service re-sealing must be repeatable. Validate silicone compatibility with adjacent adhesives and long-term compression set.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes pack sealing line dispensed silicone bead, 2022
ideaData.regulatoryContext: IP67/IP6K9K ingress; UN R100

title: Self-piercing rivet swap for FDS pack fasteners
description: Replace flow-drill screws joining the tray frame to the cover flange with self-piercing rivets (SPR). SPR cycle time is ~1.5s versus ~4s for FDS and needs no pre-heat or thread-forming, lifting line throughput and removing screw cost. At ~180 joints/pack the cycle and consumable delta saves ~£3.2/pack. No swarf contamination risk in the cell zone. Benchmarks Audi PPE pack frame joining strategy.

ideaData.title: Self-piercing rivet swap for FDS pack fasteners
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline joins the tray frame to the cover flange with flow-drill screws (FDS), which thread-form into the material at ~4s/joint, sometimes with pre-heat. The change uses self-piercing rivets (SPR), which cold-pierce the top sheet and flare into the bottom sheet in ~1.5s/joint with no pre-drill, pre-heat or thread-forming, lifting line throughput and removing screw cost. At ~180 joints/pack the cycle and consumable delta saves ~£3.2/pack. SPR produces no swarf, eliminating chip-contamination risk in the cell zone. Requires SPR C-frame guns, die selection per stack-up, and rivet feeding; joint strength validated for the aluminium/steel stack.
ideaData.materialGrade: Self-piercing rivet (boron-steel rivet) vs flow-drill screw
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Swaps FDS spindles for SPR C-frame guns with rivet feed and die-per-stackup setup. Cuts joint cycle from ~4s to ~1.5s, lifting throughput, and removes thread-forming/pre-heat. No swarf means no chip-extraction in the cell zone. Adds rivet logistics and die-wear management.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Faster swarf-free riveting replaces flow-drill screws

ideaData.riskNotes: SPR needs bottom-side die access and correct die for each material stack-up; mis-set rivets crack thin aluminium or under-flare. Mitigate with joint cross-section checks and force monitoring. Disassembly for service is harder than unscrewing FDS. Validate joint fatigue and sealing.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Audi PPE pack frame joining strategy, 2023

evidenceSources.title: SPR vs FDS battery-enclosure joining study
Audi PPE pack frame joint teardown

title: Stamped cold plate to roll-bond aluminium
description: Convert the brazed two-sheet stamped cooling plate to a roll-bond aluminium plate where channels are inflated from a screen-printed graphite pattern. Roll-bond removes the vacuum-braze furnace and its flux, cutting per-plate cost from ~£14 to ~£8.5 and improving channel-geometry freedom for uniform ΔT<3°C. Lighter by ~0.8kg. Benchmarks Nio roll-bond cooling plate used under blade-style modules.

ideaData.title: Stamped cold plate to roll-bond aluminium
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline cold plate is a brazed two-sheet stamped aluminium plate, joined in a vacuum-braze furnace with flux. The change is a roll-bond aluminium plate: a graphite channel pattern is screen-printed between two aluminium sheets, which are roll-bonded, then the channels are inflated with high-pressure air/oil to form the coolant galleries. Roll-bond removes the vacuum-braze furnace and flux, cutting per-plate cost from ~£14 to ~£8.5 and giving channel-geometry freedom for uniform cell ΔT <3°C. The plate is ~0.8kg lighter. Process suits the flat, large-area cold plate under blade-style modules; inflation pressure and bond integrity are the key controls.
ideaData.materialGrade: Roll-bond aluminium (e.g. AA3003/1050) vs vacuum-brazed stamped plate
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces the vacuum-braze furnace and flux process with screen-printing, roll-bonding and channel inflation. Lowers per-plate cost ~38% and enables freer channel routing for ΔT uniformity. Removes braze-leak rework; adds inflation-press and bond-peel inspection. Lighter, flatter plate. Proof-pressure testing of inflated channels added to the plate line before assembly.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Roll-bond inflation replaces vacuum brazing

ideaData.riskNotes: Roll-bond inflated channels can balloon/burst at pressure if bond is weak; mitigate with proof-pressure test, controlled inflation and channel design limits. Lower burst margin than brazed plates limits coolant pressure. Flatness for cell contact and long-term fatigue validated.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Nio roll-bond cooling plate under blade-style modules, 2022

evidenceSources.title: Nio pack roll-bond cold plate teardown

title: Immersion cooling loop replacing cold plates
description: Replace bottom cold plates plus thermal gap-filler with single-phase dielectric immersion of cells in a circulated fluorinated/ester coolant. Direct contact removes the gap-filler (~£7.6/pack at 4kg) and equalises cell temperature, cutting peak ΔT to <2°C and enabling sustained 4C fast charge without a second chiller. Pump and dielectric add cost but net BOM and warranty improve. Benchmarks Yangwang U-series immersion-cooled blade pack.

ideaData.title: Immersion cooling loop replacing cold plates
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline cooling uses bottom cold plates plus a thermally-conductive gap-filler (~4kg) between cells and plate. The change immerses cells in a circulated single-phase dielectric coolant (fluorinated or synthetic-ester fluid), giving direct contact to all cell surfaces. This removes the gap-filler (~£7.6/pack at 4kg) and equalises cell temperature, cutting peak ΔT to <2°C and enabling sustained 4C fast charge without a second chiller. A pump, dielectric fluid, seals and a sealed enclosure are added, but net BOM and warranty (longer cell life, fewer hot-spots) improve. The pack must be fully sealed and the dielectric filtered; fluid compatibility with cell cans and busbars validated.
ideaData.materialGrade: Single-phase dielectric immersion fluid (ester/fluorinated) vs gap-filler + cold plate
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes gap-filler dispense (~4kg, a slow station) but adds dielectric fill, a circulation pump, sealed enclosure and fluid-handling/leak-test. Pack must be hermetic. Changes line to a fill-and-seal flow; fluid filtration and reclaim systems added. Removes cold-plate brazing for immersion zones.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Direct immersion deletes gap-filler and a chiller stage

ideaData.riskNotes: Dielectric fluid cost, weight, sealing integrity and long-term material compatibility are major risks; a leak is safety/serviceability critical. Mitigate with proven ester fluids, hermetic sealing, leak-down testing and filtration. Added pump parasitics and fill complexity. Validate fire behavior.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Yangwang U-series immersion-cooled blade pack, 2023
ideaData.regulatoryContext: GB 38031 thermal safety; UN R100

title: Refrigerant-direct pack chiller deleting LCC loop
description: Adopt direct-refrigerant (R1234yf) cooling in the pack cold plate, deleting the secondary glycol loop, chiller heat exchanger, coolant pump, and expansion tank. Removing the intermediate loop eliminates ~7 components and ~£36/vehicle while improving cooling response by ~25% via direct evaporation. Electronic expansion valve manages superheat. Benchmarks Zeekr 800V pack with direct-cooling thermal architecture.

ideaData.title: Refrigerant-direct pack chiller deleting LCC loop
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline thermal architecture uses a secondary glycol (LCC) loop: pack cold plate cooled by coolant, which is itself cooled by a chiller heat exchanger against the AC refrigerant, plus a coolant pump and expansion tank. The change adopts direct-refrigerant (R1234yf) cooling in the pack cold plate, evaporating refrigerant directly in the plate and deleting the glycol loop, chiller heat exchanger, coolant pump and expansion tank. Removing the intermediate loop eliminates ~7 components and ~£36/vehicle while improving cooling response ~25% via direct evaporation. An electronic expansion valve (EXV) meters refrigerant and controls superheat across the plate; refrigerant-side pressure rating and distribution uniformity are the design challenges.
ideaData.materialGrade: R1234yf direct-cooled cold plate vs secondary glycol (LCC) loop
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes coolant pump, chiller HX, expansion tank and glycol fill/bleed from assembly (~7 parts). Cold plate becomes a refrigerant evaporator needing higher pressure rating and EXV control. Simplifies coolant routing but adds refrigerant-charge and brazed-joint leak verification. Higher-pressure cold-plate brazing and EXV calibration stations added to the line.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Delete secondary loop and its pump/HX/tank

ideaData.riskNotes: Direct refrigerant cooling risks uneven evaporation/dry-out across cells and cell-temperature non-uniformity; mitigate with EXV superheat control and engineered distribution. Higher plate pressure rating and refrigerant-leak detection required. Control complexity and refrigerant charge management increase.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Zeekr 800V pack direct-cooling thermal architecture, 2023

title: Gap-filler downgauge via cell-bottom cooling
description: Re-route cooling to cell base contact and cut thermally-conductive gap-filler from a 3mm full-coverage bead (4.0kg) to localized 1.5mm pads. Gap-filler is ~£9.3/kg dispensed; halving mass saves ~£19/pack and reduces dispense takt. Cell-bottom cooling via blade geometry keeps junction ΔT in spec. Lower filler mass also eases end-of-life disassembly. Benchmarks BYD blade bottom-cooling thermal layout.

ideaData.title: Gap-filler downgauge via cell-bottom cooling
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline uses a 3mm full-coverage thermally-conductive gap-filler bead (~4.0kg) between cells and cold plate. The change re-routes cooling to direct cell-base contact (blade-style bottom cooling) and reduces gap-filler to localized 1.5mm pads where contact tolerance demands it. Gap-filler dispensed costs ~£9.3/kg; halving mass saves ~£19/pack and shortens the slow dispense takt. Cell-bottom cooling via the blade geometry keeps junction ΔT within spec with less interface material. Lower filler mass also eases end-of-life disassembly and recycling. Requires tighter cell-to-plate flatness control and a revised dispense path with reduced volume.
ideaData.materialGrade: Reduced thermally-conductive gap-filler (1.5mm localized) + cell-bottom cooling
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Cuts gap-filler dispense volume ~50%, shortening the dispense station takt and material use. Requires tighter cell/plate flatness and contact-pressure control. Lower filler mass simplifies EOL disassembly. Dispense path reprogrammed to localized pads rather than full coverage. Thermal-mapping verification added to confirm junction temperatures with the reduced filler.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Halve gap-filler mass via cell-base cooling

ideaData.riskNotes: Reduced interface material risks local hot-spots if cell/plate flatness or contact pressure varies; mitigate with thermal mapping, tolerance control and verified pad placement. Validate fast-charge junction ΔT and long-term pump-out/dry-out of remaining filler.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD Blade bottom-cooling thermal layout, 2021

title: Heat-pump-shared chiller for pack thermal loop
description: Integrate pack preconditioning into the vehicle CO2/R744 heat pump rather than a dedicated PTC battery heater. Waste heat recovery warms the pack for fast charge, cutting heater energy ~40% and deleting the 4kW PTC element and its HV harness (~£24/vehicle). Improves winter charge acceptance and range. Octovalve coordinates flows. Benchmarks Tesla octovalve-shared thermal system adopted in Volvo EX90.

ideaData.title: Heat-pump-shared chiller for pack thermal loop
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline pack heating uses a dedicated 4kW PTC element with its own HV harness. The change integrates pack preconditioning into the vehicle's CO2/R744 (or R1234yf) heat pump, recovering waste heat from drive electronics and ambient to warm the pack for fast charge, cutting heater energy ~40% and deleting the PTC element and its HV harness (~£24/vehicle). An octovalve (multi-port coolant valve) coordinates flows between cabin, drive, and battery loops. This improves winter charge acceptance and range. Adds control complexity and reliance on heat-pump capacity at very low ambient; a small backup heat source may remain for extreme cold.
ideaData.materialGrade: R744/R1234yf heat-pump shared loop vs 4kW PTC battery heater
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes the PTC heater and its HV harness branch; adds an octovalve and coolant-routing to share the heat-pump loop. Shifts complexity from a discrete heater to valve/controls. Fewer HV connections at the pack; integration tested at vehicle thermal-loop level. Vehicle-level thermal-loop calibration and cold-climate validation are added before sign-off.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Heat-pump waste heat replaces PTC battery heater

ideaData.riskNotes: Heat-pump capacity falls at extreme low ambient, risking slow pack warm-up; may need a small backup heater, reducing savings. Octovalve and control software add failure modes. Mitigate with validated cold-climate calibration and redundancy. Charge-precondition timing critical.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Tesla octovalve-shared thermal system adopted in Volvo EX90, 2023

title: Wireless BMS removing slave-board harness
description: Replace wired daisy-chain cell-monitoring with 2.4GHz wireless BMS (wBMS) per module. Removes the isoSPI/CAN harness, connectors, and ~30m of wiring per pack, cutting ~£22/vehicle and 1.2kg, while freeing routing for CTP layouts. Reduces assembly faults from connector mating. Functional-safety via redundant RF channels. Benchmarks GM/ADI wBMS informing Mercedes next-gen pack electronics.

ideaData.title: Wireless BMS removing slave-board harness
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline cell monitoring is a wired daisy-chain: slave boards link via isoSPI/CAN through a harness of connectors and ~30m of wiring per pack. The change is a 2.4GHz wireless BMS (wBMS): each module's monitor board transmits cell voltages/temperatures over RF to the master, removing the isoSPI/CAN harness and connectors, cutting ~£22/vehicle and 1.2kg, and freeing routing for CTP layouts. Eliminating connector mating reduces assembly faults. Functional safety is maintained via redundant RF channels, frequency hopping and message authentication to meet ASIL-D timing/availability. Requires RF-shielding-aware pack design and antenna placement validation inside the metallic enclosure.
ideaData.materialGrade: 2.4GHz wireless BMS (wBMS) vs isoSPI/CAN wired daisy-chain
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes the cell-monitoring harness, connectors and ~30m wiring; slave boards become wireless nodes. Removes connector-mating stations and their fault modes, simplifying CTP assembly. Adds RF qualification and antenna/EMC validation inside the metal enclosure; no in-pack wiring routing. Antenna placement and shielding layout are fixed early in pack mechanical design.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Delete monitoring harness via wireless links

ideaData.riskNotes: RF reliability inside a shielded metal pack, EMC/co-existence and cyber-security are key risks for ASIL-D monitoring; mitigate with redundant channels, frequency hopping, authentication and antenna placement testing. Latency/availability must meet safety timing. Validate against interference and aging.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: GM Ultium / ADI wBMS informing Mercedes next-gen pack electronics, 2021

evidenceSources.title: Wireless BMS adoption and safety review

ideaData.regulatoryContext: ISO 26262 ASIL-D; UN R100

title: BMS slave board channel consolidation
