End-coil seat over-moulding integrated. Higher material cost offset by removed coating lines; corrosion-free part cuts warranty.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Corrosion-free composite coil deletes peening and all coating ops at lower mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: GFRP coil long-term set/creep, stone-impact and end-seat fatigue must be validated over life and temperature; rate consistency depends on tight winding-process control. Customer/field confidence in a non-steel spring and repair logistics are hurdles. High novelty; durability is the central risk.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Audi GFRP coil spring program, 2014; production-trialled

title: Welded-on disconnect dog clutch eliminating front e-axle gearbox losses
description: Integrate a low-mass dog-clutch disconnect on the front primary e-axle so the motor and gear train fully decouple during rear-drive cruise. Saves 1.1kW of churning and bearing drag, equating to 45Wh/100km. Range gain permits 1.5kWh smaller pack, a net system saving near £68/vehicle versus the £17 clutch. Benchmarks Denza front-axle disconnect.

ideaData.title: Welded-on disconnect dog clutch eliminating front e-axle gearbox losses
ideaData.technicalDescription: In a dual-motor EV the front primary e-axle is back-driven during rear-drive cruise, churning its gears in oil and spinning its bearings and rotor, costing ~1.1kW. The change integrates a low-mass face-tooth dog-clutch disconnect at the front reduction/half-shaft interface so the motor and gear train fully decouple at cruise. Eliminating churning and bearing drag saves ~45Wh/100km; the resulting range margin lets the pack shrink ~1.5kWh, a net system saving near £68/vehicle versus the ~£17 dog-clutch. Gears are 20MnCr5 case-hardened; the inverter speed-matches the rotor for shock-free re-engagement when front torque is demanded. Actuation is electromechanical with a sealed housing.
ideaData.materialGrade: 20MnCr5 case-hardened gears; dog-clutch collar
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Adds a dog-clutch collar, shift fork and electromechanical actuator into the front e-axle housing plus harness and inverter sync control. Minor housing machining; no part deletion at axle. Enables upstream battery-cell reduction. Sealed actuator integration and EOL shift calibration are new steps.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Front-axle disconnect cuts churning drag, enabling a smaller, cheaper pack at equal range.

ideaData.riskNotes: Fast, clash-free re-engagement for AWD/traction demand and at low temperature must be calibrated; actuator durability and functional-safety (torque availability) require validation. Mis-sync risks tooth damage and NVH. Proven concept at Denza; controls and cold-engagement robustness are the risks.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Denza front-axle disconnect, 2023; OEM-confirmed
ideaData.regulatoryContext: ISO 26262 functional safety for drive-torque availability

title: Cast GJS-600 lower control arm replacing forged aluminium on heavy trims
description: On heavy 2.6t SUV trims, switch the forged-aluminium lower arm to a GJS-600-3 ductile-iron casting where unsprung-mass sensitivity is lower. Near-net casting deletes forging plus five machining ops, and iron is far cheaper per kg. Saves about £6/corner despite a 1.4kg mass gain accepted on the platform. Benchmarks Hongqi heavy-SUV iron arm.

ideaData.title: Cast GJS-600 lower control arm replacing forged aluminium on heavy trims
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline lower control arm on heavy SUV trims is a forged AA6082-T6 aluminium part requiring forging plus ~five machining operations. On a ~2.6t SUV, unsprung-mass sensitivity is proportionally lower and cost-per-kg dominates, so the change casts the arm near-net in GJS-600-3 ductile iron (~600 MPa tensile, ~370 MPa yield), forming the bushing bosses and ball-joint seat directly so only bores are reamed. This deletes the forging step and five machining ops, and iron is far cheaper per kg than aluminium, saving ~£6/corner despite a 1.4kg mass gain accepted on this platform. Nodular iron's strength and fatigue meet the suspension load cases; e-coat handles corrosion.
ideaData.materialGrade: GJS-600-3 ductile iron (vs forged AA6082-T6)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Near-net iron casting replaces aluminium forging and ~five machining ops; only bushing/ball-joint bores are reamed. Far lower material cost per kg. Adds foundry sourcing and e-coat. Casting cycle and post-machining minimal; reuses standard bushing press. Mass gain accepted on heavy platform.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Cheaper near-net iron casting replaces aluminium forging where mass matters less.

ideaData.riskNotes: Casting nodularity and porosity at the ball-joint/bushing bosses must be controlled for fatigue; the 1.4kg unsprung gain needs ride/NVH confirmation on the platform. Mature material/process; foundry quality is the main risk. Reverse-lightweighting accepted only on cost-led heavy trims.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Hongqi heavy-SUV ductile-iron control arm, 2022; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R79; suspension durability per OEM load cases

title: Box-section welded subframe replacing cast aluminium on volume trim
description: For the high-volume base trim, replace the premium cast-aluminium front subframe with a 590MPa steel box-section welded design. Steel piece cost is about £29 lower than the aluminium casting despite 4kg gain, acceptable on FWD base cars. Welded build reuses existing body-shop robots, avoiding casting capex. Benchmarks BYD Dolphin steel subframe.

ideaData.title: Box-section welded subframe replacing cast aluminium on volume trim
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline premium front subframe is a cast/forged aluminium unit, lightweight but costly and capex-heavy. For high-volume FWD base trims, the change uses a stamped-and-welded steel box-section subframe in 590MPa HSLA (CR420LA/590DP), with two-shell rails MIG/spot-welded into closed boxes and welded cross-members. Steel's far lower material cost makes the piece ~£29 cheaper than the aluminium casting despite a ~4kg mass gain, acceptable on cost-led base cars. Crucially the welded build reuses existing body-shop robots and fixtures, avoiding HPDC casting capex entirely. Suspension pickups are stamped-in and reinforced; e-coat handles corrosion.
ideaData.materialGrade: 590MPa HSLA steel box-section (vs Al casting)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Stamping and robotic welding reuse existing body-shop equipment, avoiding HPDC casting machine and die capex. Adds weld content and a subframe weld cell but far lower piece and tooling cost. e-coat for corrosion. Mass gain accepted; build flexibility high for volume trim.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Cheaper welded steel reuses body-shop capex, avoiding the costly aluminium casting.

ideaData.riskNotes: Steel subframe adds ~4kg, affecting front-axle load and efficiency, acceptable only on base trims; weld-seam fatigue and NVH versus the stiff casting must be validated. Two variants (steel/aluminium) across trims adds complexity. Mature welding; NVH and mass trade are the considerations.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD Dolphin steel front subframe, 2022; teardown-confirmed

evidenceSources.title: Steel vs aluminium subframe cost study

ideaData.regulatoryContext: Front crash load paths per C-NCAP / Euro NCAP and OEM standards

title: Sealed-for-life electric park brake caliper deleting drum-in-hat
description: Replace the rear drum-in-hat park brake plus cable with an integrated electric-park-brake motor-on-caliper. Deletes the drum, shoes, cable, lever and adjuster, removing eight parts and one assembly station. Saves about £9.3/corner and 1.3kg. Self-adjusting, no cable routing. Benchmarks Volvo EPB motor-on-caliper rear corner.

ideaData.title: Sealed-for-life electric park brake caliper deleting drum-in-hat
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline rear park brake is a drum-in-hat system: a small drum cast into the disc hat with shoes, a cable, hand-lever/pedal mechanism and a manual adjuster. The change is an integrated electric park brake (EPB) with a motor-on-caliper actuator: a DC motor and spur/planetary gear drive a spindle that applies the service-brake piston for parking, controlled electronically. This deletes the drum, shoes, cable, lever and adjuster — eight parts — and the cable-routing assembly station, saving ~£9.3/corner and 1.3kg. The EPB is self-adjusting (no manual adjuster), sealed-for-life, and enables auto-hold and hill-hold functions. The caliper body is shared with the service brake; only the actuator module is added.
ideaData.materialGrade: EPB motor-on-caliper actuator (deletes drum/shoes)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Bolt-on EPB actuator module replaces drum casting, shoes, cable, lever and adjuster plus the cable-routing assembly station; deletes eight parts. Adds actuator harness and EOL EPB calibration. Reuses the service caliper body. Net lower part count and assembly time; electronic control replaces mechanical adjustment.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Motor-on-caliper EPB deletes the drum-in-hat hardware, cable and a routing station.

ideaData.riskNotes: EPB actuator must hold on grade over life and temperature with fail-safe behaviour; gearing self-lock, motor durability and electronic redundancy need validation per R13-H. Loss of mechanical backup requires a defined fallback. Mature technology at Volvo; safety-case and durability are the items.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo EPB motor-on-caliper rear corner, 2020; teardown-confirmed
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R13-H parking-brake hold requirements

title: Induction-hardened raceway hub deleting separate inner ring
description: Induction-harden the bearing raceway directly onto the hub flange of the Gen3 unit, deleting one pressed inner ring and its grinding. Integrated raceway removes a component and a press-and-stake op, saving about £2.6/corner. Hardness profile reaches 58HRC at 0.9mm depth. Benchmarks Porsche integrated-raceway hub.

ideaData.title: Induction-hardened raceway hub deleting separate inner ring
ideaData.technicalDescription: In a baseline Gen3 hub-bearing the inner raceway for one row of balls is a separate pressed-on inner ring (in 100Cr6) that is ground and then press-and-staked onto the hub. The change forms that raceway directly on the hub flange body (a C56/medium-carbon steel hub), induction-hardening the integrated raceway to ~58HRC at ~0.9mm case depth, then finish-grinding it. This deletes the separate inner ring and its press-and-stake operation, removing a component and a station, saving ~£2.6/corner. The integrated raceway also improves runout (no press stack-up) and stiffness. The second row keeps a conventional ring; sealing and grease packing are unchanged.
ideaData.materialGrade: C56 hub, induction-hardened raceway (58HRC, 0.9mm)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Induction-hardening and grinding the raceway onto the hub deletes the separate inner ring, its grinding and the press-and-stake station. Adds an induction-hardening cell and raceway grind on the hub. Improves runout by removing press stack-up; net lower part count and assembly content.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Hardening the raceway onto the hub deletes a ring and a press-and-stake station.

ideaData.riskNotes: Induction-hardening the integrated raceway must achieve uniform case depth and hardness without distortion of the flange; soft spots or distortion cause early spalling, requiring tight process control and grinding stock. Material choice must suit both flange forming and raceway hardening. Established; hardening uniformity is the risk.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Porsche integrated-raceway Gen3 hub, 2023; teardown-confirmed

title: Coaxial e-axle integrating motor, gearbox and differential housing
description: Adopt a coaxial e-axle where the rotor shaft passes through one half-shaft, integrating motor, two-stage reduction and differential into a single die-cast housing. Deletes one housing joint, two seals and the offset bracket, saving 4.5kg and about £26/axle. Shared oil sump cuts a fill operation. Benchmarks AVATR coaxial drive unit.

ideaData.title: Coaxial e-axle integrating motor, gearbox and differential housing
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline e-axle is an offset (parallel-shaft) layout: the motor sits parallel to the half-shaft axis, needing a separate gearbox housing, a joint between motor and gearbox cases, two extra shaft seals and an offset mounting bracket. The change is a coaxial e-axle: a hollow rotor shaft is concentric with the differential and one half-shaft passes through it, aligning motor, two-stage reduction and differential on one axis inside a single HPDC AlSi10MnMg housing. This deletes one housing split-joint, two seals and the offset bracket, saving 4.5kg and ~£26/axle, and a shared oil sump for motor/gearbox cooling removes a separate fill operation. Through-shaft bearing support and rotor balancing are added; the integrated case stiffens the unit.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC single housing; coaxial through-shaft
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One integrated HPDC housing replaces split motor/gearbox cases, deleting a split-joint, two seals, the offset bracket and one oil-fill op (shared sump). Adds hollow-rotor through-shaft machining and bearing support. Fewer sealing interfaces cut leak-test scope; net lower part count and assembly.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Coaxial integration deletes a housing joint, two seals, a bracket and a fill op.

ideaData.riskNotes: Routing a half-shaft through a hollow rotor complicates bearing support, sealing and rotor dynamics; concentricity and shared lubrication between motor and gears must avoid contamination and churning. Thermal management of an integrated case is harder. High integration risk; proven at AVATR.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: AVATR coaxial drive unit, 2023; teardown-confirmed

title: Forged 7075-T6 monoblock caliper replacing two-piece cast
description: Replace the bolted two-piece cast-aluminium front caliper with a forged 7075-T6 monoblock. Forging deletes the bridge bolts and the split-face machining, raising stiffness 28% for better pedal feel and pad wear. Net about £4.3/corner after forging tooling, plus 0.4kg saved. Benchmarks Porsche monoblock front caliper.

ideaData.title: Forged 7075-T6 monoblock caliper replacing two-piece cast
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline fixed front caliper is a two-piece bolted design: two cast-aluminium halves joined by bridge bolts across the split face, requiring machining of both mating faces and a bolted-joint that flexes under clamp load. The change forges a monoblock caliper from 7075-T6 (Al-Zn-Mg-Cu, ~470 MPa yield) as a single body, with the bore cylinders machined and pistons inserted. Eliminating the split deletes the bridge bolts and split-face machining, and the one-piece forged body raises caliper stiffness ~28%, improving pedal feel, pad-wear uniformity and reducing fluid-volume loss. Net ~£4.3/corner after forging tooling, with 0.4kg saved. 7075-T6's high strength enables thin, stiff sections.
ideaData.materialGrade: 7075-T6 forged aluminium monoblock caliper
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Closed-die forging plus bore/piston machining replaces two castings, split-face machining and the bridge-bolt assembly; deletes those bolts and a joining op. Adds forging die capex and T6 heat-treat. Monoblock improves stiffness and reduces leak paths. Net lower assembly content; higher forging tooling amortised over volume.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One-piece forging deletes bridge bolts and split-face machining while stiffening the caliper.

ideaData.riskNotes: 7075's stress-corrosion-cracking susceptibility needs proper T73/T6 temper and corrosion protection; forging die complexity for the closed monoblock bore access is high. Thermal performance under repeated braking and bore-seal durability require validation. Established in performance brakes; SCC and tooling are the risks.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Porsche monoblock front caliper, 2021; production-verified
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R90; brake performance per R13-H

title: Polymer composite leaf-spring trailing arm replacing steel link
description: Mold the rear trailing arm as a glass-fibre composite leaf integrating spring compliance and longitudinal location. Replaces a stamped-steel arm plus a separate bushing pair, deleting two press-fit bushings. Saves 1.7kg/corner and about £4.1/corner with corrosion-free life. Benchmarks Zeekr composite trailing arm.

ideaData.title: Polymer composite leaf-spring trailing arm replacing steel link
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline rear trailing arm is a stamped-steel link that locates the wheel longitudinally, with a separate pair of press-fit bushings and, on twist-beam/multilink layouts, a discrete compliance element. The change moulds the trailing arm as a glass-fibre composite (GF/epoxy compression-moulded) leaf whose tailored bending flexibility integrates a degree of spring compliance with longitudinal location. Co-moulded end fittings replace one press-fit bushing pair. This deletes two press-fit bushings, saves 1.7kg/corner and ~£4.1/corner, and gives corrosion-free life (no e-coat/chip protection). Fibre orientation sets the longitudinal stiffness while allowing controlled vertical/lateral compliance; bonded metal inserts handle the pivot and wheel-carrier interfaces.
ideaData.materialGrade: GF/epoxy compression-moulded composite trailing arm
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Compression-moulding with co-moulded inserts replaces steel stamping plus a bushing press operation; deletes two press-fit bushings and corrosion coating. Adds composite cure takt and NDT. Multifunction leaf reduces part count. Corrosion-free part removes e-coat/chip-protect steps.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Composite leaf integrates compliance and location, deleting bushings and coatings.

ideaData.riskNotes: Composite leaf fatigue and creep under combined bending/torsion, plus bonded-insert durability, need validation; stone-impact and damage tolerance must be proven. Integrating compliance changes rear K&C, requiring ride/handling re-tune. Established at Zeekr; bond and fatigue validation are the risks.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Zeekr composite trailing arm, 2023; teardown-confirmed

evidenceSources.title: Zeekr rear composite trailing arm teardown

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R79; rear-suspension durability per OEM standards

title: Hollow stabiliser-bar link with molded ball-stud sockets
description: Replace the solid steel drop link with a hollow tube link and injection-molded POM ball-stud sockets, deleting metal socket cups and grease boots staking. Saves 0.4kg/corner and about £1.9/corner from material and assembly. Maintenance-free sealed sockets. Benchmarks AITO stabiliser drop-link sourcing.

ideaData.title: Hollow stabiliser-bar link with molded ball-stud sockets
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline anti-roll-bar drop link is a solid steel rod with two ball-stud joints, each using a stamped-metal socket cup, a separate grease boot and a staked retainer. The change uses a hollow steel/aluminium tube body with injection-moulded POM (polyoxymethylene/acetal) ball-stud sockets moulded directly around the hardened ball studs, with integrated lip seals replacing the metal cup, separate boot and staking. POM's low friction and wear resistance give a maintenance-free, self-lubricating bearing surface for the ball studs (a link is a low-load two-force member, so a tube suffices). This deletes the metal socket cups and boot-staking operation, saving 0.4kg/corner and ~£1.9/corner from material and assembly.
ideaData.materialGrade: Hollow tube body + moulded POM ball-stud sockets
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Injection-moulding the POM sockets over the ball studs replaces stamped metal cups, separate boots and the staking operation; hollow tube cuts material. Adds an insert-moulding step; deletes grease-fill and staking stations. Sealed-for-life sockets remove service. Net lower part count and assembly content.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Moulded POM sockets and a hollow body delete metal cups, boots and staking.

ideaData.riskNotes: POM socket wear, low-temperature toughness and seal integrity over life must be validated to avoid play and rattle; moulded-socket pull-out strength under link load needs testing. Mature in suspension links; cold-temperature durability is the main item. Low overall risk.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: AITO stabiliser drop-link sourcing, 2023; benchmarked

title: Lubricated-for-life sealed propshaft U-joint replacing greaseable
description: Switch the propshaft universal joints to sealed lubricated-for-life cross-and-bearing units, deleting the grease zerk and the service relube operation. Improved seal package extends life beyond 240k km. Saves about £1.5/joint across four joints and removes a service interval. Benchmarks Li Auto sealed U-joint.

ideaData.title: Lubricated-for-life sealed propshaft U-joint replacing greaseable
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline propshaft universal joint is a greaseable cross-and-bearing (cardan) unit with a grease zerk (nipple) requiring periodic service relube. The change is a sealed lubricated-for-life U-joint: the 18CrNiMo trunnion cross runs in needle-roller bearing cups packed with high-temperature grease and retained by an upgraded multi-lip seal package that excludes water/dust and retains lubricant beyond 240k km. Deleting the grease zerk and eliminating the service relube interval removes a part and a service operation, saving ~£1.5/joint across four joints. The improved seal also reduces contamination-driven warranty. Cup retention is by circlip or staking; the cross is case-hardened.
ideaData.materialGrade: Case-hardened cross + sealed needle-bearing cups
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Upgraded seal package and factory grease-pack replace the grease-zerk feature; deletes the zerk machining and the service relube interval. Adds tighter seal-assembly control. Minimal line change at the joint supplier; removes a maintenance step from service. Lower warranty from better contamination exclusion.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Sealed-for-life U-joint deletes the grease zerk and a service relube interval.

ideaData.riskNotes: Sealed-for-life relies entirely on the seal package; any lip wear or grease degradation over 240k km causes bearing failure, so seal and grease durability must be validated across temperature/water-fording. No relube fallback in service. Mature method; seal durability is the key risk.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Li Auto sealed propshaft U-joint, 2023; teardown-confirmed

title: Plasma-transferred-arc clad valve seats deleting hardened insert subframe bush sleeves
description: On the subframe bushing interfaces, replace pressed-in hardened steel sleeves with a laser-clad wear track on the aluminium subframe bore. Cladding removes the sleeve component and its press-fit op while resisting fretting at the bush. Saves about £2.5/vehicle across four bushes and one station. Benchmarks Audi subframe bore cladding.

ideaData.title: Clad subframe bush bores deleting hardened insert sleeves
ideaData.technicalDescription: On an aluminium subframe, the bushing interface bores are protected against fretting/wear by pressed-in hardened steel sleeves — one component and a press-fit operation per bush. The change applies a laser/PTA (plasma-transferred-arc) clad wear track of a hard Fe/Ni-based alloy directly onto the aluminium subframe bore, then bores it to size. The metallurgically bonded clad layer resists the micro-slip fretting at the bushing outer surface without a separate sleeve, deleting the steel sleeve and its press-fit op across four bushes and one station, saving ~£2.5/vehicle. Cladding also avoids galvanic and press-relaxation issues of a steel-in-aluminium interface. The clad zone is machined concentric with the bushing seat.
ideaData.materialGrade: Fe/Ni laser/PTA clad track on aluminium subframe bore
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Laser/PTA cladding plus bore finishing replaces four pressed-in steel sleeves and their press-fit station. Adds a cladding cell and post-clad boring. Deletes sleeve components and press-relaxation scrap. Metallurgical bond avoids galvanic interface; net lower part count and one fewer station.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Clad wear track replaces pressed steel sleeves, deleting parts and a press station.

ideaData.riskNotes: Clad-layer adhesion and crack-freedom on aluminium must be validated against fretting and corrosion; dilution and heat-affected softening of the aluminium bore need process control. Cladding cycle time and capex are constraints. Newer process for subframes; bond and fretting durability are the risks.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Audi subframe bore cladding studies, 2022; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Laser cladding of aluminium chassis bores

title: Single planetary reduction replacing offset two-shaft on rear drive
description: Replace the offset two-shaft helical reduction with a coaxial single planetary gearset on the rear drive unit. Balanced planet loads cut bearing count from six to four and reduce housing size, saving 3.1kg and about £13/axle. Mesh efficiency improves 0.4% lowering loss-driven cooling. Benchmarks Yangwang planetary rear unit.

ideaData.title: Single planetary reduction replacing offset two-shaft on rear drive
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline rear drive uses an offset two-shaft helical reduction: motor, countershaft and output on parallel axes, needing six support bearings and a wide housing to package the offset. The change is a coaxial single planetary gearset (sun-planet-ring) where the motor drives the sun, planets on a carrier reduce speed, and the ring is grounded. The planetary's symmetric load sharing across multiple planets balances radial loads, cutting bearing count from six to four and shrinking the housing, saving 3.1kg and ~£13/axle. Splitting torque across planets also raises mesh efficiency ~0.4%, lowering heat and cooling demand. Gears are 20MnCr5 case-hardened and ground; the carrier and ring are integrated into the e-axle housing.
ideaData.materialGrade: 20MnCr5 case-hardened planetary gearset
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Coaxial planetary replaces the offset countershaft layout, deleting two bearings and shrinking the housing casting. Adds planet-carrier and ring-gear machining. Coaxial packaging simplifies the case and reduces oil volume. Net fewer bearings and smaller housing; gear-grinding content shifts to planets.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Planetary load-sharing cuts bearings and housing size while raising efficiency.

ideaData.riskNotes: Planetary gearsets can have higher gear-whine and load-sharing sensitivity to manufacturing tolerances; planet-pin bearing life and carrier stiffness need validation. Lubrication of planet bearings at high speed is challenging. Efficiency gain is modest; NVH and planet-bearing durability are the key risks.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Yangwang planetary rear drive unit, 2023; OEM-confirmed

title: Aluminium knuckle with cast-in steel bearing seat bimetal
description: Cast the aluminium upright around a steel bearing-seat insert (bimetal HPDC), deleting a pressed steel sleeve and its insertion op while giving a robust 58HRC bore for the hub bearing. Saves 1.5kg vs iron knuckle and about £3.8/corner over insert-press route. Benchmarks Luxeed bimetal knuckle.

ideaData.title: Aluminium knuckle with cast-in steel bearing seat bimetal
ideaData.technicalDescription: An aluminium upright is lighter than cast iron but its soft bore can creep/fret under the press-fit hub-bearing, so a hardened steel sleeve is normally pressed in afterward — a separate part plus an insertion operation. The change cast-encapsulates a hardened steel bearing-seat insert directly during HPDC of the AlSi10MnMg knuckle (bimetal casting): the steel ring (hardened to ~58HRC) is preheated and placed in the die, and aluminium solidifies around it forming a mechanical/metallurgical lock. This deletes the separate sleeve and its press-fit op, giving a robust hardened bore in one casting. Saves 1.5kg versus an iron knuckle and ~£3.8/corner over the insert-press route. Differential thermal expansion is managed by the interlock geometry.
ideaData.materialGrade: AlSi10MnMg HPDC + cast-in hardened steel seat (58HRC)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Bimetal HPDC encapsulates the steel bearing seat during casting, deleting the separate sleeve component and its press-fit insertion station. Adds insert preheat/placement automation in the die. Finish-machine the bore. Net lower part count and assembly; casting-cell complexity rises with insert handling.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Casting-in the bearing seat deletes a pressed sleeve and its insertion station.

ideaData.riskNotes: Bimetal bond integrity and differential thermal expansion between steel insert and aluminium can cause gaps or fretting over thermal cycles, needing interlock design and bond validation; insert placement repeatability in the die affects scrap. Casting porosity around the insert risks leak paths. High process risk.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Luxeed bimetal knuckle, 2023; teardown-confirmed
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R79; corner durability per OEM load cases

title: Integrated brake-by-wire system deleting vacuum booster and lines
description: Adopt a one-box electro-hydraulic brake-by-wire unit, deleting the vacuum booster, vacuum pump, separate ESC modulator and several brake lines. Consolidates three modules into one, saving 3.8kg and about £38/vehicle while improving regen blending. Removes a vacuum-line assembly station. Benchmarks Nio one-box brake-by-wire.

ideaData.title: Integrated brake-by-wire system deleting vacuum booster and lines
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline brake actuation uses a vacuum booster, an electric vacuum pump (needed on EVs lacking manifold vacuum), a separate ESC/ABS hydraulic modulator and the brake lines linking them. The change is a one-box electro-hydraulic brake-by-wire (EHB) unit: a single module with an electric-motor-driven ball-screw plunger generating brake pressure, integrated pressure sensing, and built-in ABS/ESC valving, with a pedal-feel simulator decoupling the pedal. Consolidating booster, vacuum pump and ESC modulator into one box deletes those three modules and several lines, saving 3.8kg and ~£38/vehicle, removing the vacuum-line assembly station, and enabling smooth regen-friction blending for efficiency. A redundant fallback (or RBU) preserves braking on fault.
ideaData.materialGrade: One-box EHB unit (ball-screw, integrated ESC valving)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One EHB module replaces booster, vacuum pump, ESC modulator and interconnecting lines, deleting the vacuum-line assembly station and three sub-assemblies. Adds EHB mounting and harness plus EOL bleed/calibration. Fewer hydraulic connections cut leak-test scope. Net lower part count and assembly content.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One-box brake-by-wire consolidates booster, vacuum pump and ESC, deleting lines and a station.

ideaData.riskNotes: Brake-by-wire is safety-critical: loss of the actuator must leave a fail-safe hydraulic or redundant path, requiring ASIL-D redundancy and homologation under R13-H; pedal-feel simulation and regen-blend tuning are demanding. Single-supplier dependence and software validation add risk. Mature at Nio/suppliers; safety-case is central.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Nio one-box brake-by-wire, 2022; teardown-confirmed
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN/ECE R13-H braking; ISO 26262 ASIL-D redundancy

title: Flow-formed half-shaft tube replacing thick-wall machined tube
description: Flow-form the half-shaft interconnecting tube to a variable wall (3.0mm ends, 2.2mm mid), replacing a constant thick-wall machined tube. Grain alignment lets mass drop 0.7kg/side at equal 3000Nm capacity, and flow-forming removes an internal turning op. Saves about £3.1/side. Benchmarks BYD flow-formed shaft tube.

ideaData.title: Flow-formed half-shaft tube replacing thick-wall machined tube
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline tubular half-shaft uses a constant thick-wall tube machined to size, carrying uniform wall even where torque demand is lower mid-span. The change flow-forms (spin-forges) the tube to a variable wall — thicker (~3.0mm) at the splined/welded ends where stress concentrates, thinner (~2.2mm) mid-span — by rolling the tube over a profiled mandrel. The cold work aligns grain and work-hardens the material, so the optimised section carries the 3000Nm torque at 0.7kg less/side. Flow-forming also produces the bore net-shape, removing an internal turning operation. Net ~£3.1/side. The shaft is in 37Cr4/equivalent with induction-hardened splines.
ideaData.materialGrade: 37Cr4 flow-formed variable-wall half-shaft tube
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Flow-forming produces the variable-wall tube net-shape, removing an internal turning op and reducing steel input. Adds a flow-forming machine. Work-hardened section meets torque at lower mass. Induction-hardening of splines retained. Net lower material and machining content per side.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Variable-wall flow-formed tube cuts mass and an internal turning op at equal torque.

ideaData.riskNotes: Flow-forming wall-thickness control and transition geometry must avoid stress risers at the thick-to-thin step; torsional fatigue at 3000Nm and the weld/spline transitions need validation. Machine capex and cycle time are constraints. Mature in driveline; wall-transition fatigue is the key item.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD flow-formed half-shaft tube, 2022; teardown-confirmed

title: Modular front corner module pre-assembled off-line
description: Pre-assemble the full front corner (knuckle, hub, brake, damper, arms, link) as a modular unit fitted to the subframe in one bolted operation on the main line. Moves 14 sequential line tasks off-line, cutting main-line takt 90s/vehicle and rework. Logistics and station savings net about £20/vehicle. Benchmarks Mercedes corner-module assembly.

ideaData.title: Modular front corner module pre-assembled off-line
ideaData.technicalDescription: In the baseline build the front corner — knuckle, hub-bearing, brake caliper/disc, damper, control arms and stabiliser link — is assembled in ~14 sequential tasks directly on the main trim line, each consuming takt and risking in-line rework. The change pre-assembles the complete corner as a modular unit off-line on a dedicated sub-line (or at a supplier), torqued and quality-gated, then fits it to the subframe/body in one bolted, error-proofed operation on the main line. This moves 14 sequential tasks off the critical path, cutting main-line takt ~90s/vehicle and reducing rework, while pre-set alignment improves quality. Logistics consolidation (one module SKU vs many parts) and station savings net ~£20/vehicle. This is a process/assembly architecture change, not a part change.
ideaData.materialGrade: N/A — corner-module assembly (mixed steel/Al parts)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Shifts 14 corner-assembly tasks off the main line to a sub-line/supplier with one bolted fitment on the main line, cutting ~90s/vehicle takt and rework. Reduces main-line stations and improves alignment via off-line quality gating. Consolidates many part SKUs into one module for line-side logistics.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Off-line modular corner cuts main-line takt, stations and rework via single bolted fitment.

ideaData.riskNotes: Module strategy needs robust off-line quality gates and traceability since defects ship complete; logistics of bulky modules and a single module supplier add supply risk. Tolerance stack at the single bolted interface must be controlled for alignment. Established at Mercedes; supply continuity and quality gating are the risks.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes front corner-module assembly, 2021; production-verified

title: Lightweight aluminium air-spring piston replacing steel rolling lobe
description: Replace the steel air-spring rolling-lobe piston with an impact-extruded aluminium piston and a deep-drawn aluminium top cap. Cuts 0.9kg/corner and deletes a phosphate-and-paint corrosion line, saving about £4.3/corner. Extrusion gives the contour in one stroke. Benchmarks BMW air-spring aluminium piston.

ideaData.title: Lightweight aluminium air-spring piston replacing steel rolling lobe
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline air-spring rolling-lobe piston (the contoured surface the rubber lobe rolls over to set the spring rate) and its top cap are deep-drawn/welded steel, needing a phosphate-and-paint corrosion line. The change impact-extrudes the piston from aluminium (6082/EN AW-6060) so the rolling contour is formed in one stroke, paired with a deep-drawn aluminium top cap. Aluminium's corrosion resistance deletes the entire phosphate-and-paint process, and the lower density cuts 0.9kg/corner (unsprung). The extruded contour is dimensionally precise for consistent spring-rate progression, saving ~£4.3/corner. The piston interfaces with the existing rolling-lobe air sleeve and damper rod.
ideaData.materialGrade: EN AW-6060/6082 impact-extruded aluminium piston
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Impact extrusion forms the piston contour in one stroke, replacing deep-draw/weld steel construction and deleting the phosphate-and-paint corrosion line. Adds extrusion tooling. Deep-drawn aluminium top cap. Lower mass eases handling; corrosion-free aluminium removes a finishing line. Net lower process content.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Extruded aluminium piston deletes the corrosion-coat line and cuts unsprung mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: Impact-extruded contour tolerance must hold to keep spring-rate progression consistent; aluminium fatigue under cyclic pressure and lobe-rolling contact needs validation. Galvanic isolation at steel-rod interfaces required. Mature method; contour accuracy and fatigue are the items. Moderate risk.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BMW air-spring aluminium piston, 2022; teardown-confirmed

title: Full air suspension system consolidating compressor and ECU one-box
description: Consolidate the air-supply unit, valve block, dryer, reservoir and air-suspension ECU into one integrated module, deleting external air lines, two brackets and a separate controller housing. Reduces fittings from 12 to 5 and a leak-test station, saving about £32/vehicle and 2.6kg. Benchmarks Li Auto integrated air-supply module.

ideaData.title: Full air suspension system consolidating compressor and ECU one-box
ideaData.technicalDescription: The baseline air-suspension supply consists of discrete components: a compressor/air-supply unit, a separate valve block, an air dryer, a reservoir and a standalone air-suspension ECU, all linked by external air lines and mounted on multiple brackets. The change integrates these into one compact module — compressor, valve block, dryer and ECU on a shared manifold with a co-located reservoir — so internal galleries replace external air lines, deleting two brackets and the separate controller housing. Fittings drop from 12 to 5 and one leak-test station is removed, saving ~£32/vehicle and 2.6kg. Fewer connections also cut field leak warranty. The module is pre-charged and leak-tested as a unit, simplifying vehicle assembly to a few connections.
ideaData.materialGrade: Integrated air-supply manifold module (Al/PA housing)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One integrated module with internal galleries replaces the compressor, valve block, dryer, reservoir, ECU housing and external lines; fittings fall from 12 to 5, deleting two brackets and one leak-test station. Module pre-tested as a unit. Vehicle assembly reduces to a few connections; fewer joints cut leak warranty.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One-box air-supply module deletes external lines, brackets, a housing and a leak-test station.

ideaData.riskNotes: Integrating compressor, ECU and dryer concentrates heat and vibration, so thermal management and ECU durability near the compressor need validation; a single module means one failure affects the whole supply, and serviceability shifts to module replacement. Mature at Li Auto; thermal/vibration integration is the key risk.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Li Auto integrated air-supply module, 2023; teardown-confirmed

evidenceSources.title: Li Auto integrated air-supply module teardown

ideaData.regulatoryContext: Headlamp leveling per UN/ECE R48 where air system controls ride height

title: Mono-material PET instrument panel substrate for recyclability
description: Replace multi-material PA6-GF/PUR/PVC IP build-up with a mono-material PET substrate plus PET-foam skin, enabling closed-loop recycling and eliminating material separation at end-of-life. Single-resin sourcing cuts compound cost £1.8/vehicle and removes the adhesion-promoter coating step. Recyclate content rises to 30% without performance loss. Validated against Volvo EX90 mono-material interior strategy targeting full IP recyclability.

ideaData.title: Mono-material PET instrument panel substrate for recyclability
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline IP is a PA6-GF30 structural carrier carrying a slush-moulded PVC skin over PUR backfoam, with a PVC-adhesion promoter primer. The redesign uses a mono-material build: an amorphous PET injection-moulded substrate, a PET-foam (expanded PET, ~80 kg/m3) energy layer, and a PET skin, all in the polyester family so the laminate recycles as a single stream. Single-resin sourcing of PET (IV ~0.80) replaces three incompatible polymers, deleting the adhesion-promoter coating cell. Recyclate content reaches 30% rPET without loss of HDT or impact. Eliminating PVC also removes plasticiser fogging and the chlorine end-of-life burden.
ideaData.materialGrade: PET / rPET (IV 0.80) + expanded-PET foam
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes the adhesion-promoter spray cell and its flash-off oven; PET substrate runs on existing injection presses with adjusted drying (dew point -40C). Skin/foam lamination uses heat bonding instead of mixed adhesive, removing the adhesive-mix station. End-of-life shredding becomes single-stream, no material separation.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Single-resin sourcing plus deleted coating step lowers compound and process cost while enabling closed-loop recycling.
costSavingPotential.calculationBasis: £1.8/veh material + coating-cell save × ~330k veh/yr inc. EoL credit

ideaData.riskNotes: PET substrate HDT and creep under summer soak (95C) must be validated versus PA6-GF; risk of skin gloss/grain drift addressed by tool texturing. rPET IV variability needs incoming-lot viscosity control. Foam-skin heat-bond peel strength validated to spec; supply of automotive-grade expanded PET is still consolidating.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo EX90 mono-material interior strategy, 2024; benchmarked in 2024 sustainability teardown

evidenceSources.title: Volvo EX90 sustainable interior materials disclosure
Mono-material polyester interiors for closed-loop recycling

ideaData.regulatoryContext: EU ELV Directive 2000/53/EC recyclability quota (95% by weight)

title: PP-LGF30 cross-car beam replacing magnesium die-casting
description: Substitute the magnesium die-cast cross-car beam with a hybrid PP-LGF30 (long-glass-fibre) injection-moulded beam with localised steel inserts for steering-column stiffness. Consolidates 14 brackets into the moulded geometry, removing 22 fasteners and 0.9kg. Avoids volatile magnesium pricing and high-pressure die-cast tooling, saving £9.7/vehicle. NVH targets met via tuned rib pattern. Benchmarked to BMW Neue Klasse polymer cockpit carrier.

ideaData.title: PP-LGF30 cross-car beam replacing magnesium die-casting
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline cross-car beam is an AM60B magnesium high-pressure die-casting (~3.5 kg) carrying the steering column, IP and HVAC. The substitute is a hybrid long-glass PP-LGF30 (30% long-glass, 11-12 mm fibre length) injection-moulded beam with over-moulded steel inserts at the steering-column mount and A-pillar tie points for torsional/bending stiffness. Long fibres give the modulus (~6.5 GPa) and creep resistance to carry column loads while 14 brackets are integrated into the moulded geometry, removing 22 fasteners and 0.9 kg. Avoiding magnesium removes exposure to volatile Mg pricing and the HPDC hot-chamber tooling. NVH first-mode and steering-column shake targets are met via a tuned cross-rib pattern and the localised steel tube.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP-LGF30 (long-glass PP) + steel inserts
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces Mg hot-chamber HPDC cell with a large-tonnage injection press and insert-loading robot. Bracket integration removes 22 fasteners and 14 weld/rivet ops; assembly becomes mould-in. Tooling cost lower than Mg die; cycle ~55-70 s with insert pre-heat. No magnesium melt-safety infrastructure required.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Polymer beam removes magnesium price exposure and integrates 14 brackets, cutting part count and fasteners.

ideaData.riskNotes: Steering-column stiffness and crash IP intrusion are the key risks; mitigated by over-moulded steel inserts and CAE-tuned ribs, plus modal test for >38 Hz first mode. Long-fibre weld-line strength and creep at 90C need validation. Insert-to-polymer adhesion under thermal cycling validated; column-mount torque retention checked.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BMW Neue Klasse polymer cockpit carrier development, 2025; benchmarked vs Mg CCB

evidenceSources.title: Polymer cross-car beams replacing magnesium HPDC
PP-LGF30 hybrid CCB structural performance data

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN-R11/R94 occupant interior intrusion and steering-column displacement

title: In-mould-grain (IMG) door card eliminating paint and grain steps
description: Move the upper door trim to in-mould-grain technology, transferring the grain texture directly during compression moulding and removing the separate spray-paint and laser-etch grain steps. Eliminates two finishing stations and reduces VOC abatement load. Net saving £3.7/part across both front doors, with improved grain consistency. Benchmarked against Mercedes EQE door-card IMG implementation.

ideaData.title: In-mould-grain (IMG) door card eliminating paint and grain steps
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline upper door trim is an injection-moulded PP-T20 carrier that is spray-painted then laser-etched to create grain. In-mould-grain (IMG) transfers a negatively-etched grain from a textured tool cavity directly during moulding/compression, so the part leaves the press grained and colour-matched. This deletes the paint booth and the laser grain-etch station. PP-T20 (20% talc) gives the dimensional stability and gloss control for crisp grain pickout. Net saving £3.7/part across both front-door uppers, with improved grain consistency (no spray DOI variance) and lower VOC abatement load because solvent-borne paint is removed.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP-T20 (20% talc-filled PP)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Eliminates two finishing stations (paint booth + laser-etch) and their VOC abatement; tool carries the etched grain (nickel-shell or laser-textured cavity). Adds tool-texture maintenance but removes paint sludge and overspray scrap. Cycle time net-neutral; first-time-right grain raises yield. Net result is fewer line operations, lower consumable spend and a more robust, repeatable trim surface straight from the press.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Removing paint and laser-etch finishing steps eliminates two stations and VOC cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Grain durability and scratch/UV ageing of un-painted PP surface must meet trim spec; mitigated by mar-resistant grade and additive package. Tool-texture wear over high volumes risks grain fade - managed by re-texture interval. Colour-match across mouldings needs masterbatch control.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes EQE door-card in-mould-grain implementation, 2022; teardown-confirmed

evidenceSources.title: Mercedes EQE interior door trim teardown

title: Foamed TPO skin replacing slush-moulded PVC for IP cover
description: Replace heavy slush-moulded PVC IP skin with a foamed TPO (thermoplastic olefin) skin produced by negative thermoforming. TPO is fully recyclable, 35% lighter, and free of plasticiser fogging, removing the fog-test rework loop. Material cost drops £3.1/vehicle and the slush tool's nickel shell maintenance disappears. Soft-touch haptics retained via 2.5mm PUR backfoam. Benchmarked to Audi Q6 e-tron TPO IP skin.

ideaData.title: Foamed TPO skin replacing slush-moulded PVC for IP cover
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline IP cover skin is slush-moulded PVC (~1.2 mm) over 2.5 mm PUR backfoam, produced on a heated nickel-electroform slush tool with powder PVC. The change is a foamed TPO (thermoplastic olefin, PP/EPDM blend) skin produced by negative (vacuum) thermoforming of a co-extruded foam/cap sheet. TPO is polyolefin-recyclable, ~35% lighter than PVC, and plasticiser-free so the fog-test rework loop is eliminated. Soft-touch haptics are retained by the 2.5 mm PUR backfoam laminated behind. Material cost falls £3.1/vehicle and the nickel slush-shell maintenance/powder handling disappear. The foamed cap layer tunes softness and grain pickout.
ideaData.materialGrade: Foamed TPO (PP/EPDM) skin + PUR backfoam
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces slush-moulding line (nickel shell heat/cool cycle, powder handling) with vacuum thermoforming of co-extruded TPO sheet. Removes fog-test rework loop. Trim/scrap of thermoform edge is regrindable. Lower energy per part; faster cycle than slush. Backfoam step retained. Net result is a lighter, recyclable skin produced with lower energy and fewer rework loops, and a shorter, more stable process chain.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: TPO sheet replaces heavier PVC and deletes slush-tool maintenance and fog rework.

ideaData.riskNotes: Thermoformed grain definition over deep IP topography and cold-temperature (-30C) skin cracking are the risks; mitigated by grade selection and draw-ratio control. Skin-to-foam adhesion and long-term low-gloss stability validated. TPO scratch/UV durability checked against PVC baseline.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Audi Q6 e-tron TPO IP skin, 2024; benchmarked in teardown

evidenceSources.title: Audi Q6 e-tron interior skin teardown
Foamed TPO IP skin material datasheet

title: Single-shot 2K injection centre console with integrated trim
description: Produce the centre console base and soft cup-holder lip in a single-shot 2K injection process, combining ABS structure and TPE soft-touch in one cycle. Removes a separate over-moulded insert, two clips, and the manual assembly station. Parts consolidated from 5 to 2, saving £3.3/vehicle and 18 seconds cycle time. Benchmarked against Porsche Macan EV console module.

ideaData.title: Single-shot 2K injection centre console with integrated trim
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline centre console uses an ABS structural base with a separately over-moulded TPE cup-holder lip insert fixed by two clips and a manual assembly station. The redesign moulds both in a single-shot 2K (two-component) injection process: rigid ABS substrate and a TPE (Shore A ~60) soft-touch lip injected sequentially in one tool with a rotary/transfer plate. This bonds the soft lip chemically to the ABS in-mould, removing the separate insert, two clips, and the manual station. Parts consolidate from 5 to 2, saving £3.3/vehicle and 18 s cycle versus the assemble-then-clip sequence. The 2K interface gives a flush, gap-free soft lip with better durability than a clipped insert.
ideaData.materialGrade: ABS + TPE (Shore A 60) 2K
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Requires a 2K injection press with rotary platen or core-back; replaces a separate over-mould tool plus manual clip station. Part count 5->2; removes two clips. Cycle -18 s net at module level. Tool more complex but one machine versus two plus assembly labour.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: 2K single-shot consolidates structure and soft lip, removing inserts, clips and a labour station.

ideaData.riskNotes: ABS/TPE interfacial bond strength under thermal cycling and the 2K tool's sequential-fill balance are key risks; mitigated by adhesion-compatible TPE grade and mould-flow tuning. Soft-lip wear and cleaning-chemical resistance validated. Higher tool spend offsets only at volume.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Porsche Macan EV centre-console module, 2024; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Porsche Macan EV console module teardown

title: Natural-fibre (flax) door trim carrier replacing wood-fibre board
description: Adopt a flax-PP natural-fibre composite carrier for the door card, compression-moulded in one step versus the wood-fibre board plus PP backing. Cuts carrier weight 25% (0.6kg/door) and material cost £2.4/vehicle, while the renewable fibre lowers CO2 footprint for ESG scoring. Mouldability allows integrated map-pocket ribs, removing two fasteners. Benchmarked to BMW i-series natural-fibre door panels.

ideaData.title: Natural-fibre (flax) door trim carrier replacing wood-fibre board
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline door-card carrier is a wood-fibre board (WFB) with a PP backing, requiring two material flows and lamination. The substitute is a flax-PP natural-fibre composite (NFC, ~50/50 flax/PP by weight) compression-moulded in one step from a needle-punched flax/PP mat that melts the PP matrix and consolidates to net shape. The flax composite reaches the flexural modulus needed for the carrier at ~25% lower mass (0.6 kg/door) and £2.4/vehicle lower material cost, while renewable flax improves the CO2/ESG score. Mouldability lets map-pocket ribs and clip bosses be formed integrally, removing two fasteners. Single-step consolidation deletes the separate PP-backing lamination.
ideaData.materialGrade: Flax-PP natural-fibre composite (~50% flax)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Single heated-press compression-moulding of flax/PP mat replaces board-cut + PP-backing lamination. Integral ribs/bosses moulded-in remove two fasteners. Edge trim regrind limited (natural fibre). Lower part mass eases handling; mat sourcing replaces WFB supply. Net result is one consolidated press operation, lower part mass and renewable fibre content, simplifying both supply and downstream assembly.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One-step flax-PP moulding cuts mass and material cost and integrates ribs to remove fasteners.

ideaData.riskNotes: Flax moisture uptake and odour/emission (VOC, formaldehyde-free) under cabin soak are risks; mitigated by dried mat and capped PP content. Batch fibre-property variation needs supplier control. Flammability (FMVSS-302 / GB 8410) and fogging validated; clip-boss pull-off to spec.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BMW i-series natural-fibre door panels, 2023; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS-302 / GB 8410 interior flammability

title: Bio-PU foam seat cushions from castor-oil polyol
description: Substitute up to 30% of petroleum polyol with castor-oil-derived bio-polyol in moulded seat-cushion bio-PU foam. Drop-in compatibility with existing foam-in-place tooling means zero capex. Stabilises foam cost against crude-linked feedstock swings, saving an estimated £1.9/seat, and supports recycled-content marketing. Comfort hardness (CLD) retained within spec. Benchmarked to Mercedes bio-based foam adoption in EQ-series seating.

ideaData.title: Bio-PU foam seat cushions from castor-oil polyol
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline seat cushions are moulded flexible PU foam from 100% petroleum-derived polyether polyol and MDI/TDI isocyanate, foamed-in-place in heated aluminium tools. The change substitutes up to 30% of the polyol with castor-oil-derived bio-polyol (ricinoleic-acid based), a drop-in reactive component compatible with existing FIP tooling and dispensing - zero capex. Bio-polyol partially decouples foam cost from crude-linked feedstock, saving an estimated £1.9/seat and stabilising price. Comfort hardness (CLD/IFD) and 25%/65% sag factor stay within spec by adjusting the index and cell-opener. Recycled/bio content supports interior sustainability marketing.
ideaData.materialGrade: Bio-PU foam (<=30% castor-oil polyol)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Drop-in feedstock change on existing foam-in-place carousel; no tooling or dispenser capex. Requires polyol blend tank and revised metering ratio plus a re-balanced catalyst/cell-opener package. Cure/demould time unchanged after recipe tuning. Net result is a recipe-only change that holds takt and quality while diversifying feedstock and adding marketable bio-content with no new equipment.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Partial bio-polyol substitution stabilises foam cost against crude swings at zero capex.

ideaData.riskNotes: Bio-polyol can shift cure kinetics, hydrolytic ageing and odour; mitigated by capped 30% level and catalyst rebalance. Wet/heat ageing CLD retention and VOC/fogging must be re-validated. Castor-oil supply and price seasonality is a secondary risk; dual-source.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes EQ-series bio-based seat foam adoption, 2023; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Mercedes bio-based foam in EQ seating

title: Vegan microfibre seat trim replacing genuine leather
description: Replace genuine leather across the full seat-trim set with a recycled-PET vegan microfibre suede offering consistent yield and no hide-defect scrap. Cut-and-sew yield improves from 78% to 94%, removing tannery lead-time and slashing material cost £15/seat-set. The synthetic eliminates the chrome-tanning environmental burden and stabilises pricing against hide markets. Durability exceeds 60k Martindale cycles with retained soft haptics. Benchmarked to Polestar/Volvo Nordico vegan microfibre interior.

ideaData.title: Vegan microfibre seat trim replacing genuine leather
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline seat trim is chrome-tanned genuine leather, cut from hides with 78% yield and inherent hide-defect scrap and lead-time. The substitute is a recycled-PET (rPET) microfibre suede (non-woven PU-impregnated microfibre, similar to Dinamica/Alcantara-class), supplied in defect-free rolls. Roll goods raise cut-and-sew yield from 78% to 94%, remove tannery lead-time and slash material cost £15/seat-set. The synthetic eliminates the chrome-tanning effluent burden and decouples cost from volatile hide markets. Durability exceeds 60k Martindale abrasion cycles with retained soft haptics and consistent colour/grain across the full trim set.
ideaData.materialGrade: Recycled-PET microfibre suede (PU-impregnated)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Roll-goods nesting on automated cutters lifts yield 78%->94% and removes hide-grading labour. No tannery lead-time; consistent width simplifies marker-making. Sewing unchanged. Eliminates hide-defect scrap and chrome-tan supply chain. Net result is higher cut yield, removal of hide-grading and tannery lead-time, and stable roll-goods cost with simpler, more predictable supply.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Defect-free roll goods lift cut yield and remove hide cost volatility.

ideaData.riskNotes: Perceived-premium acceptance and long-term soil/UV/abrasion versus leather are risks; mitigated by >60k Martindale spec and topcoat. Seam slippage and hot/cold haptics validated. rPET microfibre supply concentration is a sourcing risk; qualify second source.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo/Polestar Nordico recycled microfibre interior, 2022; verified in production

title: Integrated pillar-to-headliner one-piece moulded trim
description: Combine the A-pillar trim and headliner edge into a single low-pressure-moulded PUR/glass-mat part with an integrated airbag-deployment chute and energy-absorbing ribs. Consolidates 3 parts into 1, removing 4 clips and a separate foam ramp, saving £2.2/vehicle and reducing rattle interfaces that drive warranty squeak claims. The tear-seam is tuned for FMVSS-208 head-impact and curtain-airbag breakout. Benchmarked against Audi A6 e-tron integrated headliner trim module.

ideaData.title: Integrated pillar-to-headliner one-piece moulded trim
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline A-pillar trim, headliner edge, and a separate foam deployment ramp are three parts with four clips. The redesign low-pressure-moulds (LPM) a single PUR/glass-mat part combining the A-pillar trim and headliner edge, with an integrated curtain-airbag deployment chute and energy-absorbing ribs moulded in. Glass-mat-reinforced PUR gives the stiffness and a controlled tear-seam for break-out. Consolidating 3->1 removes 4 clips and the foam ramp, saving £2.2/vehicle and cutting the rattle interfaces that drive squeak/buzz warranty. The tear-seam is tuned for FMVSS-208 head-impact (FMH) and reliable curtain-airbag deployment at cold and hot temperature.
ideaData.materialGrade: Glass-mat reinforced PUR (LPM)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Low-pressure PUR/glass-mat moulding replaces three injection parts plus a foam ramp; integrates airbag chute and EA ribs in one tool. Removes 4 clips and a sub-assembly. Larger single tool; trim/edge finishing added. Reduces line clip-install labour. Net result is fewer line operations, a consolidated tool and reduced clip-install labour, with rattle interfaces designed out of the build.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: 3-to-1 consolidation removes clips, a foam ramp and rattle interfaces that drive warranty.

ideaData.riskNotes: Curtain-airbag break-out repeatability across -35C to +85C and FMH head-impact compliance are the dominant risks; validated by deployment and sled testing of the tuned tear-seam. PUR/glass-mat dimensional stability and squeak-free fit at integrated interfaces verified.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Audi A6 e-tron integrated headliner trim module, 2024; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Audi A6 e-tron headliner/pillar trim teardown

ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS-201/208 interior head-impact; UN-R21

title: Compression-moulded carpet with integrated NVH backing
description: Replace tufted carpet plus a separate EVA heavy-layer with a single compression-moulded needle-punch carpet incorporating a recycled-fibre absorber backing in one stack. Combining floor covering and mass-spring damping in one moulding removes the heavy-layer cut-and-place station and its fixing tape. Saves £4.3/vehicle and 1.1kg while meeting absorption-alpha and transmission-loss targets across the powertrain band. Mono-fibre construction aids end-of-life recycling. Benchmarked to Nio ET5 integrated floor carpet system.

ideaData.title: Compression-moulded carpet with integrated NVH backing
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline floor covering is tufted carpet plus a separately cut-and-placed EVA heavy-layer (mass-spring barrier) fixed with tape. The redesign compression-moulds a single needle-punch (PET) carpet incorporating a recycled-fibre decoupler/absorber backing in one stack, combining floor covering and mass-spring damping in one moulded part. This removes the heavy-layer cut-and-place station and its fixing tape, saving £4.3/vehicle and 1.1 kg, while meeting absorption-coefficient (alpha) and transmission-loss targets across the powertrain/road band by tuning fibre density gradient. Mono-fibre PET construction aids end-of-life recycling versus the bitumen/EVA mixed barrier.
ideaData.materialGrade: Needle-punch PET carpet + recycled-fibre absorber
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Single heated compression-moulding forms carpet and absorber backing together, deleting the heavy-layer cut/place station and tape fixing. Edge skiving and cut-outs in one tool. Mono-fibre offcuts regrindable. Lower mass eases install; one part number replaces two. Net result is a single consolidated floor part, removed barrier station and lower mass, simplifying both the assembly sequence and recycling stream.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Combining carpet and damping in one moulding removes a barrier station, tape and mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: Fibre-decoupler may give less low-frequency transmission loss than dense EVA; mitigated by graded density and local mass patches at hot spots. Moulded-in cut-out tolerance and stack delamination under heat validated. Acoustic sign-off vs baseline alpha/TL required.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Nio ET5 integrated floor carpet/absorber system, 2023; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Moulded needle-punch carpet with integrated absorber

title: Foam-in-place armrest pad eliminating cut-foam and adhesive
description: Switch the centre-console armrest from a die-cut PUR slab bonded to the carrier to a foam-in-place process injecting PUR directly into the closed-trim cavity. Removes the foam-cutting operation, spray adhesive, and manual wrap, saving £1.6/part and improving haptic uniformity. Cycle automated on a single carousel. Benchmarked to Li Auto L7 armrest foam-in-place line.

ideaData.title: Foam-in-place armrest pad eliminating cut-foam and adhesive
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline armrest pad is a die-cut flexible PUR slab spray-adhesive-bonded to the carrier and hand-wrapped with cover. The change is foam-in-place (FIP): liquid PUR is injected directly into the closed trim cavity (cover pre-loaded over the carrier) and rises to fill, bonding skin to substrate in one cure. This removes the foam-cutting operation, the spray-adhesive cell, and the manual wrap, saving £1.6/part and giving uniform haptics with no glue read-through. The process is automated on a single carousel with mould-close clamping. Foam density/hardness is tuned by shot weight and index for the target softness.
ideaData.materialGrade: Foam-in-place flexible PUR
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: FIP carousel with mix-head and closed-mould clamping replaces foam die-cut, adhesive spray and manual wrap. One automated cell; cover pre-loaded as in-mould skin. Removes adhesive VOC and slab scrap. Cycle gated by PUR cure; shot-weight control sets quality. Net result is one automated cure cell replacing three manual operations, with removed adhesive VOC, less scrap and more uniform haptics.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Foam-in-place deletes foam-cut, adhesive and manual wrap into one automated cure.

ideaData.riskNotes: Foam fill/voids and cover bleed-through at seams are risks; mitigated by vented mould and controlled shot. Cover pre-stress/wrinkle and FIP adhesion to substrate validated. PUR isocyanate handling/ventilation required; cure-time sets throughput.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Li Auto L7 armrest foam-in-place line, 2023; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Li Auto L7 console armrest teardown

title: Common HVAC vent register platform across model range
description: Standardise the centre and side HVAC vent register on a single carry-over barrel-and-blade module across three platforms, varying only the visible bezel. Cuts active tool count from 9 to 3 and consolidates spend, lowering piece cost £2/vehicle through volume aggregation. Common air-direction kinematics simplify validation. Benchmarked against BYD e-platform 3.0 shared register architecture.

ideaData.title: Common HVAC vent register platform across model range
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline uses unique centre and side HVAC vent registers per platform - nine active tools. The change standardises a single carry-over barrel-and-blade register module (POM blades, PP-T20 barrel, common air-direction kinematics) across three platforms, varying only the visible bezel skin to suit each interior. Active tool count drops from 9 to 3, aggregating volume to lower piece cost £2/vehicle. The common kinematics (blade pitch, detent torque, shut-off damper) are validated once, simplifying airflow and squeak/rattle sign-off. POM gives low-friction, consistent blade feel; the bezel decouples styling from the functional core.
ideaData.materialGrade: POM blades / PP-T20 barrel (common core)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates 9 register tools to 3 shared cores plus bezel-only variants. Volume aggregation amortises tooling and cuts piece price. One validation set for kinematics and airflow. Logistics carries fewer part numbers; assembly identical across lines. Net result is far fewer active tools, aggregated volume, one validation set and reduced part numbers across the plant network and service.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Shared register core across three platforms aggregates volume and cuts tooling.

ideaData.riskNotes: Package/airflow fit across three IP geometries and styling acceptance of a common core are risks; mitigated by adaptable bezel and validated air-direction envelope. Detent-feel consistency and whistle/squeak across variants checked. Carry-over locks design freedom; manage via bezel-only differentiation.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD e-Platform 3.0 shared HVAC register architecture, 2023; benchmarked

title: Slim integrated display bezel replacing chrome-trimmed frame
description: Replace the discrete chrome-galvanised display frame with a black-panel cover-glass bonded directly to a single moulded bezel via optical bonding, achieving an edge-to-edge integrated display. Removes the galvanic chrome process and a separate trim ring, saving £3.9/vehicle and eliminating chrome-line environmental cost. Improves perceived quality. Benchmarked to Zeekr 001 integrated display surround.

ideaData.title: Slim integrated display bezel replacing chrome-trimmed frame
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline display surround is a chrome-galvanised (electroplated ABS) frame plus a separate trim ring around the cluster/centre display. The change bonds a black-panel cover glass directly onto a single moulded PC/ABS bezel via optical bonding (OCA/LOCA), achieving an edge-to-edge integrated black-panel display. This removes the galvanic chrome line and the separate trim ring, saving £3.9/vehicle and deleting the chrome-plating environmental and Cr(VI) compliance cost. Optical bonding also removes the air gap, cutting reflection and parallax for higher perceived quality. The moulded bezel carries mounting bosses, eliminating ring fasteners.
ideaData.materialGrade: PC/ABS bezel + optically-bonded cover glass
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes the chrome electroplating line (with Cr(VI) abatement) and a trim-ring tool. Adds an optical-bonding cell (OCA lamination, autoclave de-bubble). Bezel mounting bosses remove ring fasteners. Cleanroom bonding raises capex but consolidates two parts to one assembly. Net result is deleted chrome chemistry, a consolidated bezel assembly and improved optical quality, trading added bonding capex for lower process burden.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Optical-bonded black-panel removes chrome line and a trim ring for an integrated display.

ideaData.riskNotes: Optical-bond yield (bubbles, delamination under thermal cycling) and cover-glass breakage in head-impact zones are risks; mitigated by qualified OCA, rework process and FMH-compliant glass/laminate. Sunload/black-panel readability validated. Chrome deletion may face premium-perception pushback.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Zeekr 001 integrated display surround, 2022; benchmarked in teardown
ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS-201 IP head-impact; REACH Cr(VI) restriction on chrome plating

title: Edge-lit ambient lighting via single moulded light-guide
description: Replace the door ambient strip's discrete LED-per-segment harness with a single end-fed PMMA light-guide edge-lit by two RGB LEDs, achieving uniform glow. Reduces LED count from 14 to 2 per door and shortens the wiring harness, saving £2.6/vehicle. Single connector eases assembly. Light uniformity within 15% across the run. Benchmarked to Xpeng G6 light-guide ambient system.

ideaData.title: Edge-lit ambient lighting via single moulded light-guide
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline door ambient strip uses a harness of discrete LED-per-segment (14 LEDs/door) for an even glow. The change is a single end-fed PMMA light-guide edge-lit by two RGB LEDs at one end, using engineered out-coupling features (laser-etched or micro-prism extraction) to distribute light uniformly along the run. LED count drops 14->2 per door and the multi-tap wiring becomes a short single feed, saving £2.6/vehicle. A single connector eases door-build. Uniformity is held within 15% along the guide by grading the extraction-feature density toward the far (LED-distal) end.
ideaData.materialGrade: Optical-grade PMMA light-guide + RGB LED
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces 14-LED PCB strip and multi-tap harness with a moulded PMMA guide and a 2-LED feed PCB. Single connector per door cuts assembly time and harness cost. Guide is injection-moulded with optical surface; extraction features tooled or laser-formed. Net result is far fewer LEDs and connectors, a shorter harness and a single drop-in optical part, simplifying door build and logistics.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Edge-lit light-guide replaces 14 LEDs with 2 and shortens the harness.

ideaData.riskNotes: End-to-end brightness uniformity and colour-mixing along a long guide are risks; mitigated by graded extraction density and RGB calibration. PMMA UV-yellowing and hot-spot near the LED managed by additive and diffuser. Optical-surface scratch in handling controlled.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Xpeng G6 light-guide ambient lighting, 2023; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Xpeng G6 door ambient lighting teardown

title: One-piece moulded seat-back panel replacing carpet-wrapped board
description: Substitute the carpet-wrapped seat-back board with a single textured-grain injection-moulded PP seat-back panel, removing the carpet lamination and edge-wrap operations. Integrated snap features eliminate 6 clips per row. Saves £2.3/seat and improves scuff resistance for the cargo-load surface. Grain matches lower trim. Benchmarked to Tesla-style moulded seat-back adopted by AITO M7.

ideaData.title: One-piece moulded seat-back panel replacing carpet-wrapped board
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline seat-back (rear of front seat) is a board wrapped with carpet, requiring carpet lamination and edge-wrap, fixed with 6 clips/row. The change is a single textured-grain injection-moulded PP-T20 seat-back panel with integrated snap features, removing the carpet lamination and edge-wrap operations and the 6 clips. The moulded panel saves £2.3/seat and improves scuff/abrasion resistance for the cargo-load surface (folded-seat load floor). Grain is matched to lower trim for appearance. Integral snaps and locating ribs allow tool-less assembly. The single moulding also removes a textile supply stream and its lamination scrap, simplifying the bill of materials.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP-T20 (textured-grain moulded)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Single injection-moulding with in-tool grain replaces board + carpet lamination + edge-wrap and 6 clips. Integral snaps enable tool-less fit. Removes carpet supply and laminator station. Scuff-resistant surface needs no cover. One part replaces a laminated sub-assembly. Net result is one moulded part replacing a laminated sub-assembly, removed clips and a more durable surface, simplifying the trim station.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Moulded panel removes carpet lamination, edge-wrap and 6 clips.

ideaData.riskNotes: Perceived quality (hard plastic vs carpet) and impact/scuff durability of the load surface are risks; mitigated by grain choice and reinforcing ribs. Snap retention and rattle under cargo load validated; cold-impact (-30C) brittleness checked on PP grade.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: AITO M7 moulded seat-back panel, 2023; benchmarked

title: Full cockpit module commonisation across LHD/RHD
description: Re-architect the full IP/cockpit module to a symmetric cross-car-beam and ducting layout enabling a single LHD/RHD carry-over substrate with mirrored cluster cut-outs only. Halves IP tooling investment and consolidates logistics, lowering amortised cost £8.3/vehicle. Common HVAC core and wiring break-points reduce variant validation. Benchmarked to Hongqi shared cockpit platform strategy.

ideaData.title: Full cockpit module commonisation across LHD/RHD
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline LHD and RHD cockpits use separate IP substrates, cross-car beams and HVAC ducting - duplicate tooling and logistics. The re-architecture creates a symmetric cross-car-beam and ducting layout so a single carry-over IP substrate serves both LHD and RHD, with only mirrored cluster/steering cut-outs differing. This halves IP tooling investment, consolidates logistics, and lowers amortised cost £8.3/vehicle. A common HVAC core and defined wiring break-points reduce variant validation. The symmetric CCB (PP-LGF or Mg) is designed with mirror-able mount geometry; the visible cluster aperture is the only handed feature, filled by a handed insert.
ideaData.materialGrade: Symmetric IP substrate (PP-T20 / PP-LGF carrier)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One IP/CCB tool set serves LHD+RHD, halving major tooling and freeing press capacity. Handed cluster aperture handled by insert, not a new substrate. Common HVAC core and harness break-points cut validation builds and reduce part numbers across the plant network.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Symmetric architecture lets one substrate serve LHD+RHD, halving IP tooling.

ideaData.riskNotes: Symmetry constrains styling and steering-column/pedal package; risk of compromise to both markets. Mitigated by early symmetric DMU and CAE. Airbag/knee-bolster certification needed for both hands; HVAC airflow symmetry validated. Cut-over locks design for programme life.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Hongqi shared cockpit platform strategy, 2023; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN-R94/R12 occupant protection (both hand variants)

title: Recliner mechanism commonisation to single supplier platform
description: Consolidate three recliner variants onto one continuous-disc recliner platform with a common torsion-bar and pinion interface across all front seats. Volume aggregation drops the recliner piece cost £3.2/seat and removes two separate validation programs plus their durability test fleets. The common backrest-pivot geometry simplifies the seat-frame weld jig and spares logistics. Latch strength and backset retention meet ECE-R17. Benchmarked to Denza seat recliner standardisation.

ideaData.title: Recliner mechanism commonisation to single supplier platform
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline front seats use three recliner variants. The change consolidates all front seats onto one continuous-disc recliner platform with a common torsion-bar and pinion interface. Continuous-disc (taumel) recliners give infinite back-angle adjust and high latch strength; standardising the disc, pawl and pinion to one design across all front seats aggregates volume to drop piece cost £3.2/seat and removes two separate validation programmes and their durability fleets. The common backrest-pivot geometry simplifies the seat-frame weld jig and spares logistics. Latch strength and backset retention meet ECE-R17 across the common platform.
ideaData.materialGrade: Continuous-disc recliner (boron/spring steel pawl)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One recliner platform across front seats aggregates volume and uses one weld-jig pivot geometry. Removes two validation programmes and durability fleets. Common spares reduce service SKUs. Seat-frame interface standardised, simplifying the body-in-frame weld fixtures.
