Net result is aggregated recliner volume, one weld-jig geometry, fewer validation programmes and reduced service SKUs across all front seats.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Single recliner platform aggregates volume and removes two validation programmes.

ideaData.riskNotes: One recliner must cover the highest load case of all applications, risking over-spec for lighter seats. Mitigated by load-case envelope analysis. ECE-R17 latch strength/backset and abuse-cycle durability re-validated for the common design; supplier single-source risk dual-sourced.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Denza seat recliner standardisation, 2023; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: ECE-R17 seat/head-restraint strength

title: Tailored-blank steel seat frame replacing multi-gauge weldment
description: Replace the multi-piece, multi-gauge seat-back frame weldment with a laser-welded tailored-blank stamping that places sheet thickness only where load paths demand it. The approach reduces 9 stamped parts to 3, removing 12 spot welds and 0.8kg, and saving £6.1/seat in material and weld-cell time. Crash performance and head-restraint backset for ECE-R17 are retained through the graded gauge transition. Benchmarked to Mercedes EQS lightweight seat-frame architecture.

ideaData.title: Tailored-blank steel seat frame replacing multi-gauge weldment
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline seat-back frame is a multi-piece, multi-gauge weldment of 9 stamped HSLA parts with 12 spot welds. The change is a laser-welded tailored-blank (LWB) stamping that joins different sheet thicknesses (e.g. CR340LA/HC340LA where load demands, thinner elsewhere) before forming, placing material only where load paths need it. This reduces 9 parts to 3, removes 12 spot welds and 0.8 kg, saving £6.1/seat in material and weld-cell time. Crash performance and head-restraint backset for ECE-R17 are retained through the graded-gauge transition at the upper recliner/belt-load region. The tailored blank also improves stiffness-to-mass for seat-back bending.
ideaData.materialGrade: Laser-welded tailored blank (CR340LA HSLA)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Laser tailor-welding of blanks pre-stamping cuts 9 stamped parts to 3 and removes 12 spot welds and a weld station. Stamping draws the graded blank in one tool per part. Lower weldment complexity simplifies the seat-back jig; 0.8 kg removed eases handling.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Tailored blank places gauge only where needed, cutting parts, welds and mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: Weld-line formability (thickness-step cracking) and fatigue at the laser seam under seat-back bending are risks; mitigated by step-ratio limits and seam placement off peak strain. ECE-R17 rearward strength and head-restraint backset re-validated. Tailor-blank supply and gauge-pairing logistics managed.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes EQS lightweight seat-frame architecture, 2021; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: ECE-R17 seat/head-restraint strength

title: Snap-fit B-pillar trim eliminating screw fasteners
description: Redesign the B-pillar upper trim attachment from 4 screws plus belt-guide bezel to integrated metal-spring snap clips and a captive seatbelt-guide feature. Removes 4 fasteners and one bezel per side, cutting £1.8/vehicle and 14 seconds line time. Clip retention validated to 90N pull-off and buzz-squeak. Benchmarked to Volvo EX30 fastener-reduced pillar trim.

ideaData.title: Snap-fit B-pillar trim eliminating screw fasteners
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline B-pillar upper trim is fixed with 4 screws plus a separate belt-guide bezel per side. The redesign attaches the trim via integrated metal-spring (doghouse) snap clips and a captive seatbelt-guide feature moulded into the trim, removing 4 fasteners and the bezel per side. This cuts £1.8/vehicle and 14 s line time. Metal-spring clips give higher, more consistent retention than plastic snaps and survive the airbag/seatbelt environment. Clip retention is validated to 90 N pull-off with buzz-squeak (BSR) sign-off at the trim-to-pillar interface. The captive belt-guide removes a second part and its alignment step.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP-T20 trim + metal-spring snap clips
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Metal-spring clips and a moulded captive belt-guide replace 4 screws and a bezel per side. Removes driver tooling at the station and 14 s/veh. Clip bosses moulded into trim; metal clips pre-inserted. Fewer part numbers; tool-less install raises right-first-time. Net result is a tool-less, fastener-reduced trim with a captive belt-guide, cutting station time and raising right-first-time assembly quality.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Snap clips plus captive belt-guide remove 4 fasteners and a bezel per side.

ideaData.riskNotes: Clip retention near the curtain-airbag/belt path and serviceability (removal without breakage) are risks; mitigated by 90 N pull-off spec and service-tool slots. BSR and rattle at the snap interface validated; pillar sheet-metal tolerance stack checked for clip engagement.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo EX30 fastener-reduced pillar trim, 2023; benchmarked

title: Open-pore real-wood veneer via direct-press lamination
description: Produce open-pore wood decor trim by direct-pressing the veneer onto the carrier with a single matt PUR seal coat, removing the conventional multi-coat high-gloss lacquer-and-sand process entirely. This eliminates 5 paint and sand steps plus the flatting line, saving £5.4/vehicle while delivering the on-trend natural matt finish customers prefer. VOC emissions and energy per part both fall sharply. Benchmarked to Audi natural open-pore wood inlays.

ideaData.title: Open-pore real-wood veneer via direct-press lamination
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline wood decor is a real-wood veneer on a carrier finished with a multi-coat high-gloss PUR lacquer plus intermediate sanding/flatting - five paint/sand steps. The change direct-presses the veneer onto the carrier and applies a single matt PUR seal coat, delivering an open-pore natural finish without the gloss build-and-flat line. This removes 5 paint/sand steps and the flatting line, saving £5.4/vehicle while giving the on-trend matt open-pore look. VOC emissions and energy per part fall sharply because solvent lacquer volume and oven dwell drop. The veneer is stabilised on an aluminium/fleece backer for crack resistance before press lamination.
ideaData.materialGrade: Real-wood veneer + matt PUR seal (open-pore)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Direct-press lamination plus one matt seal coat replaces 5-coat lacquer-and-sand and the flatting line, deleting multiple paint booths, sanders and oven dwell. Lower VOC abatement and energy. Veneer pre-stabilised on backer. Single seal-coat cell raises throughput. Net result is far fewer finishing operations, lower VOC and energy per part and a single seal-coat cell delivering the on-trend matt look.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Open-pore direct-press deletes the multi-coat gloss lacquer and flatting line.

ideaData.riskNotes: Open-pore surface stain/cleanability and veneer micro-crack at low temperature are risks; mitigated by sealed pores and backer stabilisation. Matt-finish uniformity and UV greying validated. Real-wood grain/colour variation needs visual grading; reduced lacquer lowers scratch protection - checked to spec.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Audi natural open-pore wood inlays, 2022; verified in production trim

title: Mono-material PET full door-trim module for recycling
description: Re-engineer the complete door-trim module to a mono-material PET system: PET carrier, PET-felt backing, and PET textile cover, all weldable and recyclable as one stream. Eliminates material separation and the mixed-adhesive bonding, cutting £5.9/door at end-of-life processing and simplifying sourcing. Acoustic targets met by needled PET felt. Benchmarked to Volvo EX90 mono-material door panel.

ideaData.title: Mono-material PET full door-trim module for recycling
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline door-trim module mixes a PP/wood-fibre carrier, PU/foam backing, mixed-adhesive bonding and a fabric/PVC cover - multiple incompatible streams. The redesign is a mono-material PET system: a PET (or PET-GF) carrier, needled PET-felt backing for acoustics, and a PET textile cover, all weldable/recyclable as one polyester stream. Eliminating material separation and mixed-adhesive bonding cuts £5.9/door at end-of-life processing and simplifies sourcing to one family. Acoustic absorption targets are met by the needled PET felt density. Components are joined by ultrasonic/thermal welding rather than adhesive, keeping the stream pure.
ideaData.materialGrade: Mono-material PET (carrier/felt/textile)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: PET carrier, PET-felt and PET cover are thermal/ultrasonic-welded, deleting the mixed-adhesive bonding cell. Single-resin sourcing simplifies supply. End-of-life shred is single-stream. Felt acoustic layer integrated; cover laminated by heat. Tooling for PET carrier on existing presses with adjusted drying. Net result is single-resin sourcing, welded rather than bonded joints and a fully single-stream end-of-life path, simplifying both supply and recycling.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Mono-PET door module removes adhesive bonding and enables single-stream recycling.

ideaData.riskNotes: PET carrier stiffness/creep under door-mounted loads and weld-joint strength vs adhesive are risks; mitigated by PET-GF carrier and validated weld schedules. Cover-to-felt bond and BSR validated. rPET supply and colour consistency managed; cost benefit largely at EoL, so plant credit model must hold.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo EX90 mono-material door panel, 2024; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: EU ELV Directive 2000/53/EC recyclability quota

title: Knit-to-shape 3D seat cover reducing cut-and-sew scrap
description: Adopt 3D flat-knit-to-shape seat covers that knit the cushion and bolster contour in one piece, eliminating panel cutting, sewing seams, and offcut scrap. Material utilisation rises to 96% and assembly labour drops 40%, saving £6.9/seat. Variable knit density tunes breathability and bolster firmness. Benchmarked to Mercedes knitted seat-cover concept in EQ interiors.

ideaData.title: Knit-to-shape 3D seat cover reducing cut-and-sew scrap
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline seat covers are cut-and-sew: flat panels are cut from rolls (with offcut scrap) and sewn at multiple seams. The change is 3D flat-knit-to-shape (e.g. Stoll/Shima seamless knitting) that knits the cushion and bolster contour as one 3D piece, eliminating panel cutting, most sewing seams and offcut scrap. Material utilisation rises to ~96% and assembly labour drops ~40%, saving £6.9/seat. Variable knit density (tuck/float structures) tunes breathability and bolster firmness zonally in one piece. Yarn (rPET) gives consistent colour; the seamless surface improves comfort and reduces seam-failure warranty.
ideaData.materialGrade: 3D flat-knit rPET yarn (knit-to-shape)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Computerised flat-knit machines produce contoured covers near-net, deleting cutting tables, marker-making and most sewing stations. Utilisation ~96% vs ~75% cut-and-sew. Labour -40%. Zonal density knitted in one programme. Removes offcut scrap and seam stock. Net result is near-net contoured covers with far higher utilisation, less labour and removed offcut scrap, with zonal properties knitted in one programme.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Knit-to-shape removes cutting, seams and offcut scrap with ~96% utilisation.

ideaData.riskNotes: Knit cover dimensional stability over foam and abrasion/snag durability are risks; mitigated by knit-structure tuning and topcoat. Fit/tension on contoured foam and stretch-recovery validated; flammability (FMVSS-302) and soiling checked. Knit-machine capacity/throughput vs sew lines must scale.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes knitted seat-cover concept (EQ interiors), 2022; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS-302 interior flammability

title: Polypropylene-foam headliner replacing fibreglass core
description: Replace the glass-fibre/PU headliner substrate with a recyclable PP-foam core laminated to a PET knit face, removing irritant glass-fibre handling on the line and enabling mono-stream recycling at end-of-life. The full headliner is compression-moulded in one step, saving £2.9/vehicle and 0.5kg while improving sag resistance under high summer cabin temperatures. Acoustic absorption is retained via the open-cell foam. Benchmarked to BYD Seal PP-foam headliner module.

ideaData.title: Polypropylene-foam headliner replacing fibreglass core
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline headliner substrate is a glass-fibre/PU (urethane) sandwich requiring irritant glass-fibre handling. The change is a recyclable PP-foam core (expanded/closed-cell PP) laminated to a PET knit face, removing glass-fibre from the line and enabling mono-(poly)olefin/polyester recycling at end-of-life. The full headliner is compression-moulded in one heated step, saving £2.9/vehicle and 0.5 kg while improving sag resistance under high summer cabin soak (PP-foam holds modulus better than PU at temperature). Acoustic absorption is retained through the open-cell foam structure and PET face. No isocyanate or glass-fibre exposure improves line ergonomics.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP-foam core + PET knit face
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Single-step compression-moulding of PP-foam/PET laminate replaces glass-fibre/PU build-up, removing glass-fibre handling, dust extraction and isocyanate. Trim/cut-outs in tool. Lower mass and better hot-sag. Mono-stream offcuts regrindable. Improves line ergonomics and HSE. Net result is one consolidated moulding, removed glass-fibre and isocyanate handling, lower mass and a mono-stream part with better line ergonomics.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: PP-foam headliner removes glass-fibre/PU, cuts mass and enables recycling.

ideaData.riskNotes: Hot-sag/creep at +95C roof soak and acoustic absorption vs glass/PU are the risks; mitigated by foam-density selection and PET-face tuning. Headliner stiffness for handling/sunroof cut-outs validated; FMVSS-201 head-impact and FMVSS-302 flammability checked on the new stack.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD Seal PP-foam headliner module, 2022; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS-201 head-impact; FMVSS-302 flammability

title: Integrated grab-handle and coat-hook in headliner moulding
description: Mould the rear coat-hook boss and the grab-handle damper bracket directly into the headliner substrate, removing two separate stamped-steel brackets and four screws from the build. The living-hinge damped grab handle then clips in without tools at the trim station. Parts are consolidated from 6 to 2, saving £1.5/vehicle and cutting line time. Pull-load capability to 200N and damping feel are validated. Benchmarked to Li Auto L9 integrated headliner features.

ideaData.title: Integrated grab-handle and coat-hook in headliner moulding
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline rear coat-hook and grab-handle use two stamped-steel brackets fixed by four screws to the body, with the trim around them. The change moulds the coat-hook boss and the grab-handle damper bracket directly into the headliner substrate (PP/PET), removing both steel brackets and four screws. The living-hinge damped grab handle then clips tool-lessly into the moulded boss at the trim station. Parts consolidate 6->2, saving £1.5/vehicle and cutting line time. Pull-load capability to 200 N and rotary-damper feel are validated; the moulded boss is reinforced with ribs and a metal load-spreader washer only where the 200 N grab load demands.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP/PET headliner substrate (moulded bosses)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Moulds hook boss and handle bracket into the headliner, deleting 2 steel brackets and 4 screws. Grab handle snaps in tool-lessly. Parts 6->2; removes a body-side fastening operation. Local ribs/washer carry load. Fewer part numbers in logistics. Net result is two steel brackets and four screws deleted, a tool-less snap handle and fewer part numbers, simplifying both build and logistics.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Moulded-in bosses remove two steel brackets and four screws.

ideaData.riskNotes: Grab-handle 200 N pull-load and fatigue on a moulded plastic boss are risks; mitigated by ribbing and a steel load-spreader. Headliner substrate creep at the boss under sustained load and at high temperature validated. Damper-feel consistency and rattle checked.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Li Auto L9 integrated headliner features, 2022; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Li Auto L9 headliner integration teardown

title: Foamed-core steering wheel rim replacing cast-magnesium armature wrap
description: Switch the steering-wheel rim from leather-wrapped over PU-foamed magnesium to an in-mould foamed TPE rim over a steel-tube armature, removing the hand-wrapping and stitching labour. Eliminates leather cut-and-sew and saves £4.5/wheel while maintaining grip haptics. Integrated heating film co-moulded. Benchmarked to Xpeng P7 moulded steering-wheel rim.

ideaData.title: Foamed-core steering wheel rim replacing cast-magnesium armature wrap
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline steering-wheel rim is leather hand-wrapped and stitched over a PU-foamed cast-magnesium armature. The change uses an in-mould-foamed TPE rim over a steel-tube armature, removing the hand-wrapping and stitching labour and the leather cut-and-sew. TPE is injected/foamed onto the steel-tube skeleton in a tool, giving a consistent grippy surface; a heating film is co-moulded for heated-wheel function. This saves £4.5/wheel while retaining grip haptics. The steel-tube armature meets stiffness and the airbag/horn package; eliminating leather decouples cost from hide markets and removes the most labour-intensive trim step.
ideaData.materialGrade: In-mould-foamed TPE rim + steel-tube armature
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: TPE foam-mould over steel-tube armature replaces Mg-cast armature, leather cut-and-sew and hand-wrap/stitch. Heating film co-moulded. Single moulding cell replaces a labour wrap line. Consistent surface raises yield; steel tube is lower-cost than cast Mg armature. Net result is one moulding cell replacing a labour-intensive wrap line, with co-moulded heating and a lower-cost armature raising yield.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Moulded TPE rim removes leather wrap/stitch labour and cast-Mg armature.

ideaData.riskNotes: Perceived premium vs leather and TPE grip durability/soiling are risks; mitigated by surface texture and topcoat. Steel-tube modal stiffness, airbag deployment clearance and heated-film durability validated. TPE UV/abrasion and warm/cold haptics checked.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Xpeng P7 moulded steering-wheel rim, 2022; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS-203/204 steering-control rearward displacement

title: Single-piece floor console storage tray replacing modular bins
description: Consolidate the modular cup-holder insert, phone-tray liner, and coin bin into a single soft-touch over-moulded storage tray. Removes two separate inserts and their locating clips, saving £1.4/vehicle and simplifying assembly to one drop-in part. Anti-slip TPE base co-moulded. Benchmarked to Zeekr 007 console storage integration.

ideaData.title: Single-piece floor console storage tray replacing modular bins
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline console storage is a modular cup-holder insert, a phone-tray liner and a coin bin - three parts plus locating clips. The change consolidates them into a single soft-touch over-moulded storage tray (rigid PP base with a co-moulded TPE anti-slip liner). This removes two separate inserts and their locating clips, saving £1.4/vehicle and reducing assembly to one drop-in part. The TPE base is co-moulded so the anti-slip surface needs no separate mat. Integrated cup-holder ribs and a phone pocket are formed in the single tool. Fewer interfaces also reduce rattle.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP base + co-moulded TPE (2K tray)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: 2K over-moulding makes one tray with cup-holders, phone pocket and anti-slip TPE base, deleting 2 inserts, a separate mat and locating clips. One drop-in part on the line. Removes part numbers; fewer interfaces cut rattle. 2K tool replaces three single tools.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One over-moulded tray replaces three inserts plus clips and a mat.

ideaData.riskNotes: TPE anti-slip wear/staining and 2K bond durability are risks; mitigated by grade choice and adhesion-compatible TPE. Drop-in fit tolerance and rattle validated; cup-holder retention force checked. Larger 2K tool capex offsets only at volume; styling locked to one tray.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Zeekr 007 console storage integration, 2023; benchmarked

title: Low-pressure-moulded IP substrate with integrated ducts
description: Produce the full IP substrate by low-pressure moulding with integrated demister and centre-vent ducts formed in the part, eliminating separately moulded-and-welded ABS ducts. Removes 4 duct parts, 6 vibration welds, and a sealing-foam tape, saving £6.5/vehicle. Lower clamp tonnage cuts press energy. Benchmarked to Audi Q6 e-tron integrated-duct IP.

ideaData.title: Low-pressure-moulded IP substrate with integrated ducts
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline IP substrate has separately injection-moulded ABS demister and centre-vent ducts vibration-welded on, with sealing-foam tape. The change low-pressure-moulds the full IP substrate (PP-LGF or GMT) with the demister and centre-vent ducts formed integrally in the part, eliminating the separate ducts and welds. This removes 4 duct parts, 6 vibration welds, and the sealing-foam tape, saving £6.5/vehicle. Low-pressure moulding's lower clamp tonnage cuts press energy versus high-pressure injection. Integral ducts also seal better (no weld-leak path), aiding HVAC efficiency and demist performance. Glass reinforcement carries the substrate stiffness for cluster and airbag mounts.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP-LGF / GMT (low-pressure moulded)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Low-pressure moulding forms IP substrate with integral ducts, deleting 4 duct mouldings, 6 vibration welds and foam tape. Lower clamp tonnage cuts press energy and tool cost vs HP injection. One large tool; ducts net-shape. Removes the duct-weld station and its leak rework.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Integral-duct low-pressure moulding removes 4 ducts, 6 welds and sealing tape.

ideaData.riskNotes: Integral-duct draw/wall-thickness control and substrate stiffness for airbag/cluster mounts are risks; mitigated by glass-mat reinforcement and CAE. Duct sealing/airflow and demist validated. Low-pressure surface may need a cover skin; large-tool dimensional stability checked.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Audi Q6 e-tron integrated-duct IP, 2024; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Audi Q6 e-tron IP substrate teardown

ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS-201 IP head-impact

title: Common door-pull bracket across front and rear doors
description: Standardise the metal door-pull reinforcement bracket to one symmetric part used in all four doors, replacing four unique handed brackets. Volume quadruples on a single tool, dropping piece cost £1.9/vehicle and removing three part numbers from logistics. Pull-load to 400N validated common. Benchmarked to BYD Dolphin door-pull commonisation.

ideaData.title: Common door-pull bracket across front and rear doors
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline uses four unique handed metal door-pull reinforcement brackets (one per door). The change standardises one symmetric bracket used in all four doors, replacing the handed designs. Volume quadruples on a single tool, dropping piece cost £1.9/vehicle and removing three part numbers from logistics. The symmetric bracket is designed so its mounting and pull-load geometry are common to all door inner panels; pull-load to 400 N is validated common. A symmetric stamping (HSLA steel) with mirror-able mounting holes carries the door-pull/closing load equally for front and rear, LH and RH.
ideaData.materialGrade: HSLA steel (symmetric stamped bracket)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One symmetric stamping tool replaces four handed tools; volume x4 amortises tooling and lowers piece cost. Removes three part numbers from logistics and assembly pick. Common bracket fits all door inner panels via mirror-able geometry. Single validation for 400 N pull.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One symmetric bracket across four doors quadruples volume and cuts tooling.

ideaData.riskNotes: Symmetric geometry must satisfy the worst-case (front-door) pull and packaging in all doors, risking slight over-design for rears. Mitigated by load-envelope design. 400 N pull and door-module attachment tolerance validated; weld/screw interface common across panels.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD Dolphin door-pull bracket commonisation, 2022; benchmarked

title: Recycled-PET felt C-pillar trim replacing moulded ABS
description: Replace the moulded ABS C-pillar trim with a compression-moulded recycled-PET felt panel offering integrated acoustic absorption and a premium textile finish. Removes the paint/grain step and adds sound absorption, saving £2.5/vehicle and 0.3kg. 100% recycled fibre supports ESG content targets. Benchmarked to Polestar/Volvo PET-felt pillar trims.

ideaData.title: Recycled-PET felt C-pillar trim replacing moulded ABS
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline C-pillar trim is moulded ABS, painted/grained, with no acoustic function. The change is a compression-moulded recycled-PET (rPET) felt panel that provides integrated acoustic absorption and a premium textile finish in one part. This removes the paint/grain step and adds sound absorption, saving £2.5/vehicle and 0.3 kg. The needled rPET felt is moulded to net shape with clip features; its porous structure absorbs mid/high-frequency cabin noise (useful in quiet EV cabins). 100% recycled fibre supports ESG recycled-content targets and the textile face needs no painting. Mono-fibre construction aids recycling.
ideaData.materialGrade: 100% recycled-PET needled felt (moulded)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Compression-moulds rPET felt to net shape with clip features, deleting the ABS injection tool plus paint/grain line. Adds acoustic absorption with no extra part. Lighter by 0.3 kg. Felt offcuts regrindable; one mono-fibre part. Removes VOC paint process. Net result is one mono-fibre part adding acoustic absorption while deleting the paint line, cutting mass and supporting recycled-content targets.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: rPET-felt pillar adds absorption and removes paint while cutting cost and mass.

ideaData.riskNotes: Felt surface durability (snag, soiling) and clip pull-off from a felt panel are risks; mitigated by densified clip zones and topcoat. Flammability (FMVSS-302) and fogging validated; head-impact (FMVSS-201) for the softer panel checked - generally favourable. Colour consistency of rPET managed.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo/Polestar PET-felt pillar trims, 2022; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Moulded rPET felt trim acoustic data

ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS-201 head-impact; FMVSS-302 flammability

title: Heat-staked vent register assembly removing screws
description: Replace screwed bezel-to-barrel assembly of the HVAC vent register with ultrasonic heat-staking, removing 3 self-tapping screws per register. Cuts fastener cost and 9 seconds of assembly per unit, saving £1.2/vehicle across four registers. Joint strength exceeds screw retention with no thread-strip risk. Benchmarked to AITO M9 heat-staked register module.

ideaData.title: Heat-staked vent register assembly removing screws
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline HVAC vent registers screw the bezel to the barrel with 3 self-tapping screws per register. The change ultrasonically heat-stakes the bezel to the barrel, melting moulded posts to form retained heads, removing the screws. This cuts fastener cost and ~9 s assembly per unit, saving £1.2/vehicle across four registers. Heat-staked joints exceed screw retention with no thread-strip risk and no loose-fastener BSR. The PP-T20/POM register parts are designed with staking posts; an ultrasonic/thermal stake head replaces the driver. Joint integrity is validated for pull-off and the register's temperature range.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP-T20 / POM register (heat-staked)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Ultrasonic/thermal heat-staking replaces 3 self-tapping screws per register, removing the driver operation (~9 s) and screw cost. Staking posts moulded into parts. One staking head per cell; no thread-strip rework. Joint stronger and BSR-quiet versus screws. Net result is removed screws and driver time per register, a stronger BSR-quiet joint and no thread-strip rework across all four registers.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Heat-staking removes 3 screws/register and 9 s assembly across four registers.

ideaData.riskNotes: Stake-head strength consistency and serviceability (non-removable joint) are risks; mitigated by validated stake parameters and design-for-replacement at module level. Long-term creep/relaxation of staked posts under thermal cycling validated; weld flash controlled to avoid BSR.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: AITO M9 heat-staked vent register module, 2023; benchmarked

title: Full seat-structure commonisation between front rows
description: Architect the complete front seat structure—frame, recliner, rails, height-adjuster—as a single carry-over module for driver and passenger, varying only switch-pack trim. Halves structure validation and doubles weld-line volume, cutting £7.1/seat. Common rail pitch simplifies floor-pan interface. Crash to ECE-R17 validated once. Benchmarked to Maextro S800 shared seat-structure approach.

ideaData.title: Full seat-structure commonisation between front rows
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline driver and passenger front seats use different structures. The change architects the complete front seat structure - frame, recliner, rails, height-adjuster - as a single carry-over module for both, varying only switch-pack/trim. This halves structure validation and doubles weld-line volume, cutting £7.1/seat. The common rail pitch standardises the floor-pan interface, simplifying body integration. Crash to ECE-R17 (and frontal/rear sled) is validated once for the common structure. The shared frame is designed to the higher of the two load cases (e.g. driver with belt-load), with passenger application inheriting the validated structure.
ideaData.materialGrade: Common front-seat structure (HSLA frame, common recliner)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One seat-structure module for both fronts doubles weld-line volume, halves validation and uses one set of weld jigs. Common rail pitch standardises floor-pan mounts. Single ECE-R17 crash sign-off. Service and spares SKUs cut. Switch-pack/trim are the only variants. Net result is doubled structure volume, one set of weld jigs, a single crash sign-off and reduced spares, with only switch-pack trim varying.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One front-seat structure for both seats doubles volume and halves validation.

ideaData.riskNotes: Designing to the worst-case load (driver) over-specs the passenger seat slightly; mitigated by load-envelope optimisation. ECE-R17, whiplash (head-restraint backset) and frontal crash re-validated for the common structure. Asymmetric features (e.g. driver-only memory) handled by add-on modules.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Maextro S800 shared seat-structure approach, 2024; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: ECE-R17 seat strength; UN-R94/R95 occupant protection

title: Magnetic-attach floor mats replacing grommet anchors
description: Replace twist-lock grommet floor-mat anchors moulded into the carpet with low-profile magnetic pads bonded to the floor pan, removing two grommet inserts per footwell and the carpet-pierce operation. Saves £1.1/vehicle and improves mat retention against pedal interference. Benchmarked to Nio ES6 magnetic floor-mat retention.

ideaData.title: Magnetic-attach floor mats replacing grommet anchors
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline floor mats use twist-lock grommet anchors moulded into the carpet (two grommet inserts per footwell) plus a carpet-pierce operation. The change replaces these with low-profile magnetic pads bonded to the floor pan that mate with steel discs in the mat, removing the grommet inserts and the carpet-pierce. This saves £1.1/vehicle and improves retention against pedal interference (a recall-sensitive issue). The magnets are sized for shear/peel retention exceeding mat slip loads; bonding to the floor pan (or under-carpet) removes carpet penetration and its sealing concern. Mat alignment is self-locating to the magnet pattern.
ideaData.materialGrade: Bonded magnet pads + steel mat discs
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Magnetic pads bonded to floor pan replace grommet anchors and the carpet-pierce/grommet-set operation. Two inserts per footwell removed. Mat self-locates magnetically; faster install. No carpet penetration improves carpet integrity and sealing. Steel discs added to mat. Net result is removed grommet inserts and the carpet-pierce step, faster self-locating install and improved carpet integrity and sealing.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Magnetic retention removes grommet inserts and the carpet-pierce step.

ideaData.riskNotes: Mat-shift/pedal-entrapment retention is safety-critical (FMVSS pedal-interference scrutiny); mitigated by magnet sizing to exceed slip loads and a positive locating feature. Magnet long-term adhesion and corrosion validated; demagnetisation at temperature checked. Aftermarket mat compatibility considered.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Nio ES6 magnetic floor-mat retention, 2022; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: NHTSA floor-mat/pedal-entrapment guidance

title: Co-moulded ambient light into door-trim carrier
description: Integrate the ambient light-guide directly into the door-trim carrier via 2K co-moulding of clear PMMA into the structural PP, removing the separate clipped-in light bar and its bracket. Parts consolidated 4 to 1, saving £2.7/door and eliminating a fit-gap interface. Single LED feed at the carrier edge. Benchmarked to Mercedes co-moulded ambient door trim.

ideaData.title: Co-moulded ambient light into door-trim carrier
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline ambient lighting is a separate clipped-in light bar with its own bracket inside the door trim. The change 2K co-moulds a clear PMMA light-guide directly into the structural PP door-trim carrier, integrating the ambient line into the carrier and removing the separate light bar and bracket. Parts consolidate 4->1, saving £2.7/door and eliminating a fit-gap interface that can show light-leak or misalignment. A single LED feed at the carrier edge lights the co-moulded guide. The 2K bond between PMMA and PP must be managed (compatibiliser or mechanical key) since the polymers differ; out-coupling features are tooled into the guide.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP carrier + co-moulded PMMA light-guide (2K)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: 2K injection co-moulds the PMMA guide into the PP carrier, deleting the separate light-bar, bracket and clips (4->1). Single LED feed at carrier edge. Removes a fit-gap/light-leak interface. 2K tool with PMMA+PP; bond via mechanical key or tie-layer. Assembly drops a sub-part.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Co-moulding the light-guide into the carrier removes the light bar, bracket and clips.

ideaData.riskNotes: PMMA-to-PP 2K adhesion (dissimilar polymers) and light-leak at the bond line are the key risks; mitigated by mechanical interlock geometry and tuned out-coupling. PMMA thermal expansion vs PP and stress-cracking validated; uniform brightness and colour over the run checked.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes co-moulded ambient door trim, 2023; benchmarked

title: Blow-moulded hollow cross-car beam in PA-GF
description: Replace the steel tubular cross-car beam with a gas-assist/blow-moulded hollow PA6-GF35 beam carrying integrated air ducts and harness channels. Consolidates the beam, two ducts, and eight clips, removing 1.4kg and 16 fasteners, saving £8.7/vehicle. Steering-column stiffness met via local rib and steel insert. Benchmarked to BMW polymer cross-car beam development.

ideaData.title: Blow-moulded hollow cross-car beam in PA-GF
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline cross-car beam is a steel tubular weldment with two separately moulded HVAC ducts and eight clips. The change is a gas-assist/blow-moulded hollow PA6-GF35 (35% glass) beam carrying integrated air ducts and harness channels in the hollow section. Consolidating the beam, two ducts and eight clips removes 1.4 kg and 16 fasteners, saving £8.7/vehicle. PA6-GF35 gives the modulus/heat resistance for the structural beam; steering-column stiffness is met via a local rib pattern and a steel insert at the column mount. The hollow blow/gas-assist section delivers a stiff, light closed profile that doubles as ductwork - a major part-count and mass reduction.
ideaData.materialGrade: PA6-GF35 (gas-assist/blow-moulded hollow)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Gas-assist/blow-moulding forms a hollow PA6-GF35 beam with integral ducts and harness channels, replacing a steel-tube weldment plus two ducts and eight clips (-16 fasteners, -1.4 kg). Steel insert over-moulded at column mount. Removes welding and duct-assembly stations; longer cycle for hollow process.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Hollow PA6-GF35 beam integrates ducts and channels, cutting 16 fasteners and 1.4 kg.

ideaData.riskNotes: Steering-column shake/first-mode stiffness and crash IP intrusion on a polymer beam are the dominant risks; mitigated by steel insert, ribbing and modal/CAE validation (>38 Hz). PA6 moisture-conditioned stiffness, weld-line strength and hollow-section wall control validated. Long development reflects high difficulty.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BMW polymer cross-car beam development, 2024; benchmarked vs steel CCB

evidenceSources.title: Blow-moulded structural cross-car beam with integral ducts

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN-R11/R94 interior intrusion; steering-column displacement

title: Single-material PP seat-cushion carrier replacing wire-and-foam
description: Replace the steel-wire seat-cushion suspension plus separate foam pad with a moulded PP-LGF carrier and bonded thin foam, integrating bolster geometry. Removes the wire-frame bending operation and clip-on pad, saving £3.1/seat and 0.5kg. Comfort tuned via carrier flex zones. Benchmarked to Li Auto L8 integrated cushion carrier.

ideaData.title: Single-material PP seat-cushion carrier replacing wire-and-foam
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline seat cushion uses a steel-wire suspension (zig-zag/bent wire frame) plus a separate moulded foam pad. The change replaces the wire suspension with a moulded PP-LGF (long-glass) carrier and a bonded thin foam, integrating bolster geometry into the carrier. This removes the wire-frame bending operation and the clip-on pad, saving £3.1/seat and 0.5 kg. Comfort is tuned via flex zones designed into the PP-LGF carrier (controlled local compliance) plus the thin foam top layer. The structural carrier also locates trim and harness features. Long glass gives the fatigue strength for sustained seating loads.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP-LGF cushion carrier + bonded thin foam
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Injection-moulds a PP-LGF cushion carrier with integral bolsters and flex zones, deleting the wire-bend cell and clip-on foam pad. Thin foam bonded in one step. -0.5 kg; fewer parts. Carrier also carries trim/harness features. Tool replaces wire-forming equipment. Net result is one moulded carrier replacing a wire frame and clip-on pad, lower mass and integrated bolster/trim features in a single part.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Moulded carrier replaces wire suspension and separate pad, integrating bolsters.

ideaData.riskNotes: Seating comfort/durability (sag factor, sit-down feel) and long-term carrier fatigue under cyclic load are risks; mitigated by tuned flex zones and LGF fatigue validation. Squeak/creak from a rigid carrier vs sprung wire checked; cold-impact and foam-bond durability validated.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Li Auto L8 integrated cushion carrier, 2023; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Li Auto L8 seat cushion teardown

ideaData.regulatoryContext: ECE-R17 seat strength

title: Direct-print decor on IP replacing applied trim appliqué
description: Apply the IP decor pattern by digital direct-print plus UV clearcoat onto the moulded substrate, removing the separately moulded-and-clipped appliqué panel and its 6 clips. Eliminates a part and its fit-gap, saving £3.3/vehicle, and enables low-cost decor variants without new tools. Benchmarked to Xpeng G9 direct-print IP decor.

ideaData.title: Direct-print decor on IP replacing applied trim appliqué
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline IP decor is a separately moulded-and-clipped applique panel (6 clips) carrying the trim pattern. The change applies the decor pattern by digital direct-print (single-pass inkjet) plus a UV clearcoat onto the moulded IP substrate, removing the applique panel and its 6 clips. Eliminating a part and its fit-gap saves £3.3/vehicle and enables low-cost decor variants without new tools - the print file changes, not the tool. The UV clearcoat protects the print for abrasion/UV. Direct-print on the contoured substrate uses a 5-axis/conformal print head; the substrate surface is primed for ink adhesion.
ideaData.materialGrade: Moulded IP substrate + UV direct-print decor
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Digital direct-print + UV clearcoat onto the IP substrate deletes the applique tool, the panel and 6 clips. Decor variants change by print file (no new tool). Removes a fit-gap. Adds a conformal print/UV cure cell and primer step. Eliminates applique assembly.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Direct-print removes the applique part, clips and per-variant tooling.

ideaData.riskNotes: Print abrasion/UV durability, ink adhesion to the substrate and registration on a 3D contour are risks; mitigated by primer, UV clearcoat and conformal print path. Perceived quality vs a real applique (depth/feel) checked; throughput of single-pass print must meet line rate.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Xpeng G9 direct-print IP decor, 2022; benchmarked

title: Common cup-holder cartridge across console and door
description: Standardise a single cup-holder cartridge with spring-loaded retainer used in both the centre console and rear-door bin, replacing two unique designs. Doubles tool volume and removes a part number, saving £1.4/vehicle. Common retainer kinematics validated once. Benchmarked to AVATR 11 modular cup-holder cartridge.

ideaData.title: Common cup-holder cartridge across console and door
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline uses two unique cup-holder designs - one for the centre console and one for the rear-door bin. The change standardises a single cup-holder cartridge with a spring-loaded retainer used in both locations, replacing the two designs. This doubles tool volume and removes a part number, saving £1.4/vehicle. The cartridge is a self-contained module (POM/PP housing, spring-loaded retainer arm) that drops into either host with a common mounting interface. Common retainer kinematics (grip force, range) are validated once. A cartridge approach decouples the holder function from the host trim styling.
ideaData.materialGrade: POM/PP cup-holder cartridge (common module)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One cartridge tool serves console and rear-door bins, doubling volume and removing a part number. Drop-in module with common mounting. One validation for retainer kinematics. Host trims carry a common pocket interface. Simplifies service spares and assembly pick. Net result is one cartridge tool serving two hosts, doubled volume, a removed part number and one validation, simplifying spares and assembly.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: One cup-holder cartridge across console and door doubles volume and removes a part number.

ideaData.riskNotes: A common cartridge must fit two host packages and styling, risking compromise; mitigated by a defined interface and host-side pocket. Retainer grip force across cup sizes and rattle validated; spring durability and cold-temperature feel checked. Single-source cartridge supply dual-sourced.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: AVATR 11 modular cup-holder cartridge, 2023; benchmarked

title: Thermoformed mono-PET full IP topper for recyclability
description: Build the full upper-IP module as a thermoformed mono-PET skin over a PET-foam layer on a PET-GF substrate, fully recyclable as one stream and free of mixed adhesives. Removes the slush skin and PUR backfoam line, saving £5.8/vehicle and enabling 40% recyclate. Soft haptics via foam gauge. Benchmarked to Volvo mono-material IP strategy.

ideaData.title: Thermoformed mono-PET full IP topper for recyclability
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline upper-IP module is a slush PVC skin over PUR backfoam on a PA/PP substrate - mixed polymers and adhesives. The change builds the full upper-IP as a thermoformed mono-PET skin over a PET-foam layer on a PET-GF substrate, fully recyclable as one polyester stream and free of mixed adhesives. This removes the slush-skin and PUR-backfoam lines, saving £5.8/vehicle and enabling up to 40% rPET recyclate. Soft haptics are delivered by the PET-foam gauge behind the thermoformed skin. PET-GF gives substrate stiffness for cluster/airbag mounts; the layers are heat-bonded, keeping the stream pure for closed-loop recycling.
ideaData.materialGrade: Mono-PET (PET skin / PET-foam / PET-GF substrate)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Thermoformed PET skin + PET-foam + PET-GF substrate, heat-bonded, replaces slush-PVC and PUR-backfoam lines. Single-stream, 40% rPET. Removes powder-slush and isocyanate foam cells. Substrate on injection presses with -40C drying. Mono-stream offcuts regrindable; airbag chute integrated. Net result is removed slush and backfoam lines, a single-stream recyclable module with high recyclate and an integrated airbag chute in one build.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Mono-PET upper-IP removes slush and backfoam lines and enables 40% recyclate.

ideaData.riskNotes: PET substrate HDT/creep at +95C, airbag-chute tear performance and skin haptics vs PVC are risks; mitigated by PET-GF, tuned tear-seam and foam gauge. Thermoform grain over deep topography and cold-crack validated. rPET supply/IV control and FMVSS-201/208 compliance verified.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo mono-material IP strategy, 2024; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS-201/208 IP head-impact / passenger airbag; EU ELV recyclability

title: Single-shot IMG vent register bezel removing paint
description: Produce the vent-register bezel via in-mould-grain single-shot moulding, delivering the grained finish directly and removing the spray-paint and grain-etch steps. Eliminates two finishing operations per bezel, saving £1.8/vehicle across four registers and cutting VOC load. Benchmarked to Audi IMG interior trim adoption.

ideaData.title: Single-shot IMG vent register bezel removing paint
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline vent-register bezel is moulded then spray-painted and grain-etched. The change produces the bezel by in-mould-grain (IMG) single-shot moulding, delivering the grained, colour-matched finish directly from a textured tool cavity and removing the spray-paint and grain-etch steps. This eliminates two finishing operations per bezel, saving £1.8/vehicle across four registers and cutting VOC load. The PP-T20/ASA bezel leaves the press finished; a mar-resistant grade and additive package protect the un-painted surface. Tool texture is maintained on a re-texture interval. Colour is set by masterbatch for match across registers.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP-T20 / ASA (in-mould grain)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: IMG single-shot moulding gives a grained finished bezel, deleting spray-paint and grain-etch (two ops/bezel) and their VOC abatement. Tool carries etched grain. Colour by masterbatch. Removes paint scrap; tool-texture maintenance added. Net cycle neutral; first-time-right finish. Net result is removed paint and etch operations per bezel, lower VOC and a finished part straight from the press across all four registers.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: In-mould grain removes paint and etch finishing on the register bezel.

ideaData.riskNotes: Un-painted surface scratch/UV durability and grain consistency over tool life are risks; mitigated by mar-resistant ASA/PP grade and re-texture schedule. Colour-match across mouldings via masterbatch control; gloss/DOI acceptance vs painted bezel validated.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Audi in-mould-grain interior trim adoption, 2022; benchmarked

title: Integrated armrest-and-switchpack door module
description: Consolidate the door armrest, switch-pack carrier, and pull-handle bezel into a single co-moulded sub-module pre-assembled off-line, reducing main-line door-build from 9 parts to 4. Removes 5 clips and shortens line time 22 seconds, saving £3/door. Single connector to the door harness. Benchmarked to Luxeed S7 integrated door module.

ideaData.title: Integrated armrest-and-switchpack door module
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline door build assembles the armrest, switch-pack carrier and pull-handle bezel as separate parts on the main line (9 parts). The change consolidates them into a single co-moulded sub-module pre-assembled off-line, reducing main-line door build from 9 parts to 4. This removes 5 clips and shortens line time 22 s, saving £3/door. The sub-module integrates the armrest soft-touch, the switch-pack carrier with its connector, and the pull-handle bezel into one assembly with a single connector to the door harness. Off-line pre-assembly improves quality and removes complexity from the constrained main line; one electrical connector simplifies door-harness mating.
ideaData.materialGrade: Co-moulded PP/TPE door sub-module
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Off-line pre-assembled sub-module (armrest + switch carrier + pull bezel) cuts main-line door build 9->4 parts, removes 5 clips and 22 s. Single harness connector. Moves complexity off the constrained main line, raising quality and line rate. One module part number replaces several.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Pre-assembled door sub-module cuts main-line parts 9 to 4 and removes 5 clips.

ideaData.riskNotes: Module fit/tolerance stack to the door panel and single-connector pin reliability are risks; mitigated by datum strategy and connector validation. BSR at module interfaces and switch-pack durability checked. Off-line sub-assembly logistics and module handling/damage managed.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Luxeed S7 integrated door module, 2023; benchmarked

title: Lightweight EPP dash insulator replacing EVA heavy-layer
description: Replace the EVA heavy-layer mass-spring dash insulator with a moulded EPP plus micro-perforated absorber tuned for the 800–4000Hz powertrain band, exploiting the lower noise floor of EV cabins. Cuts dash-insulator mass 45% (1.8kg) and material cost £4.4/vehicle while meeting articulation-index targets. Benchmarked to BYD Seal lightweight dash insulation.

ideaData.title: Lightweight EPP dash insulator replacing EVA heavy-layer
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline dash insulator is an EVA heavy-layer mass-spring system (dense EVA barrier over decoupler foam) tuned for ICE noise. The change is a moulded EPP (expanded polypropylene) plus a micro-perforated absorber tuned for the 800-4000 Hz powertrain band, exploiting the lower noise floor of EV cabins (no engine combustion noise). Removing the dense EVA cuts dash-insulator mass 45% (1.8 kg) and material cost £4.4/vehicle while meeting articulation-index (speech intelligibility) targets. EPP gives a light, mouldable decoupler; the micro-perforated layer adds tuned absorption where EV tonal/road noise dominates, replacing broadband mass with targeted absorption.
ideaData.materialGrade: Moulded EPP + micro-perforated absorber
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Moulds EPP to the firewall contour with a micro-perforated absorber, replacing the cut/placed EVA heavy-layer. -1.8 kg dash mass. EPP steam-chest moulding; absorber laminated. Removes dense-barrier handling. Lighter part eases install; tuned for EV band rather than broadband mass. Net result is a lighter moulded insulator tuned for the EV band, removed dense-barrier handling and easier install at no acoustic-target loss.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: EPP + micro-perf absorber replaces dense EVA, cutting 45% mass at lower material cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: Lower mass risks reduced low-frequency transmission loss vs heavy-layer; mitigated by exploiting EV's lower noise floor and adding local mass at hot spots. Articulation-index and pass-through sealing validated; EPP creep/sealing at the firewall and high-temp performance checked.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD Seal lightweight dash insulation, 2022; benchmarked

title: Sprayable LASD floor damping replacing bitumen pads
description: Replace 14 die-cut bitumen damping pads with robot-applied liquid-applied sound deadener (LASD) sprayed to optimised thickness maps on the floor pan. Removes manual pad placement and heat-melt fixing, saving £5.2/vehicle and 1.6kg while improving coverage at structural anti-nodes. Benchmarked to Mercedes LASD floor-damping line.

ideaData.title: Sprayable LASD floor damping replacing bitumen pads
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline floor damping is 14 die-cut bitumen/asphaltic damping pads heat-melt-fixed to the floor pan by hand. The change is robot-applied liquid-applied sound deadener (LASD) - a water-based acrylic/butyl deadener sprayed to optimised thickness maps on the floor pan, curing in the e-coat/paint oven. This removes manual pad placement and heat-melt fixing, saving £5.2/vehicle and 1.6 kg while improving coverage at structural anti-nodes (spray follows the optimal damping map, not pad shapes). LASD constrained-layer-free damping is tuned by thickness; robot application gives repeatable mass and placement, lowering scrap and improving NVH loss-factor where it matters.
ideaData.materialGrade: Liquid-applied sound deadener (water-based acrylic LASD)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Robot spray of LASD to thickness maps replaces 14 die-cut bitumen pads and the manual heat-melt fix. Cures in existing paint oven. -1.6 kg via optimised placement. Removes pad die-cut tooling and manual labour; adds spray robots and material-feed. Coverage at anti-nodes improved.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Robot LASD replaces 14 manual bitumen pads, cutting labour and 1.6 kg via optimised maps.

ideaData.riskNotes: Spray thickness/edge control and adhesion over e-coat are risks; mitigated by robot path calibration and surface-prep spec. Damping loss-factor vs bitumen validated at anti-nodes; cure in paint oven and overspray/masking managed. Line integration into paint shop is the main implementation hurdle.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Mercedes LASD floor-damping line, 2021; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: LASD robotic floor damping in BIW paint shop

title: Recycled-cotton-fibre body-cavity filler replacing PU foam
description: Replace expanding PU acoustic foam pillar fillers with pre-formed recycled-cotton-fibre baffles clipped into the cavity, removing the foam-expansion oven dwell and overspray cleanup. Saves £2.2/vehicle and uses recycled-content fibre for ESG scoring. Cavity sealing for wind-noise validated. Benchmarked to Volvo recycled-fibre cavity baffles.

ideaData.title: Recycled-cotton-fibre body-cavity filler replacing PU foam
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline pillar cavity sealing uses expanding PU acoustic foam (heat-expanded baffles cured in an oven dwell, with overspray cleanup). The change uses pre-formed recycled-cotton-fibre baffles (shoddy/recycled-cotton nonwoven) clipped into the cavity, removing the foam-expansion oven dwell and overspray cleanup. This saves £2.2/vehicle and uses recycled-content fibre for ESG scoring. The fibre baffle seals the cavity for wind-noise/air-leak control (acoustic and water management) without the chemistry of expanding PU. Pre-formed parts clip to body studs/holes; cavity sealing for wind-noise and HVAC pass-by is validated by the baffle's compressed fit.
ideaData.materialGrade: Recycled-cotton-fibre nonwoven baffle
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Pre-formed recycled-cotton baffles clip into cavities, deleting the PU foam-expansion oven dwell and overspray cleanup. No reactive chemistry; clips to body. Removes oven energy and masking. Recycled-content fibre. Install becomes a clip-in operation; fewer HSE controls than PU. Net result is a clip-in part replacing reactive foam chemistry, removed oven energy and overspray cleanup, with recycled-content fibre and simpler HSE.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Pre-formed recycled-cotton baffles remove the foam-expansion oven and overspray cleanup.

ideaData.riskNotes: Cavity-fill/sealing repeatability vs expanding foam (which fills voids) and water-management are risks; mitigated by compressed-fit baffle sizing and seal validation. Wind-noise and water-leak tests required; fibre moisture/mould resistance and flammability (FMVSS-302) checked. Baffle install access in tight cavities.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo recycled-fibre cavity baffles, 2023; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS-302 flammability

title: Two-component moulded wheel-arch liner with integrated absorber
description: Combine the structural PP wheel-arch liner and the separately fixed felt absorber into one 2K-moulded liner with a needled-PET absorber layer fused in the tool. Removes the felt cut-and-clip operation and 6 fasteners per arch, saving £3.7/vehicle and improving tyre-cavity noise absorption. Benchmarked to Nio ET7 integrated arch-liner absorber.

ideaData.title: Two-component moulded wheel-arch liner with integrated absorber
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline wheel-arch acoustics use a structural PP wheel-arch liner plus a separately fixed felt absorber (clipped on, 6 fasteners/arch). The change combines them into one 2K-moulded liner with a needled-PET absorber layer fused in the tool, so the structural liner and absorber are one part. This removes the felt cut-and-clip operation and 6 fasteners per arch, saving £3.7/vehicle and improving tyre-cavity (tyre/road) noise absorption through a well-coupled absorber. The PP structural shell resists stone-impact/water while the fused PET felt absorbs mid/high-frequency tyre noise - a meaningful contributor in quiet EV cabins. One part replaces two plus fasteners.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP liner + fused needled-PET absorber (2K)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: 2K/over-moulds a needled-PET absorber to the PP arch-liner shell in one tool, deleting the felt cut-and-clip step and 6 fasteners/arch. One part replaces two. Absorber well-coupled for tyre-cavity noise. Removes a sub-assembly station; tool more complex but consolidates. Net result is one consolidated arch part replacing a liner plus clipped felt, removed fasteners and better-coupled tyre-cavity noise absorption.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: 2K liner fuses the absorber, removing the felt clip-on step and 6 fasteners/arch.

ideaData.riskNotes: Absorber-to-shell bond under stone-impact, water and road salt is the key risk; mitigated by fused-felt process and drainage design. Wet-felt acoustic degradation and clogging validated; absorption vs separate felt confirmed. Tool complexity and arch-package fit checked.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Nio ET7 integrated arch-liner absorber, 2022; benchmarked

title: Micro-perforated panel absorber replacing fibrous wadding
description: Replace bulky fibrous-wadding absorbers behind trim with thin micro-perforated panel (MPP) resonant absorbers tuned to dominant EV tonal frequencies. The 2mm MPP saves 30mm packaging and £2.6/vehicle in fibre while targeting absorption precisely. Recyclable single-material PP. Benchmarked to Zeekr MPP cabin absorbers.

ideaData.title: Micro-perforated panel absorber replacing fibrous wadding
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline trim-cavity absorbers are bulky fibrous wadding (PET/glass wadding ~30 mm) placed behind trim for broadband absorption. The change is a thin micro-perforated panel (MPP) resonant absorber - a 2 mm PP panel with sub-millimetre perforations backed by an air cavity, tuned to dominant EV tonal frequencies (inverter whine, motor orders, tyre tones). The MPP saves 30 mm packaging and £2.6/vehicle in fibre while targeting absorption precisely at the problem frequencies rather than broadband. It is a recyclable single-material PP part (fibre-free), which also aids end-of-life. MPP absorption is set by hole diameter, open-area ratio and cavity depth.
ideaData.materialGrade: Micro-perforated panel (PP, single-material)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Thin 2 mm perforated PP panel replaces 30 mm fibrous wadding, freeing packaging and removing fibre handling. Perforations laser/needle-formed; tuned by hole pitch and cavity depth. Single-material PP recyclable. Lighter, thinner part; mounts to trim with an air gap. No fibre dust on line.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: MPP resonant absorber replaces bulky wadding, saving 30 mm package and fibre cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: MPP is narrow-band by nature, so mistuning to shifting EV tonal frequencies risks lost performance; mitigated by multi-cavity/graded perforation tuning. Hole-blockage by dust/contamination and cavity-depth control validated. Broadband road-noise still needs some absorption - hybrid where required.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Zeekr micro-perforated cabin absorbers, 2023; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Micro-perforated panel absorbers for EV cabins

title: Common floor-damping pad set across wheelbase variants
description: Standardise the floor-pan damping pad kit to one common set across short- and long-wheelbase variants by tuning a shared anti-node map, replacing two unique pad layouts. Consolidates die-cut tooling and removes part numbers, saving £2/vehicle. Damping loss-factor validated common. Benchmarked to Li Auto floor-pad commonisation.

ideaData.title: Common floor-damping pad set across wheelbase variants
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline short- and long-wheelbase variants use two unique floor-pan damping-pad layouts. The change standardises the floor-pan damping pad kit to one common set across both variants by tuning a shared anti-node map that works for both floor structures, replacing the two layouts. This consolidates die-cut tooling and removes part numbers, saving £2/vehicle. The common pad set is placed at anti-nodes common to both wheelbases (or covering both with a single superset), with damping loss-factor validated common. The constrained-layer bitumen/butyl pads are die-cut from one tooling set, simplifying logistics and reducing variant complexity.
ideaData.materialGrade: Constrained-layer damping pads (common set)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: One die-cut pad set serves both wheelbases, consolidating cut tooling and removing part numbers. Common anti-node map; one logistics kit. Same place-and-melt install. Reduces variant complexity at the body line. Volume aggregation on the shared pad tools. Net result is one die-cut pad set across wheelbases, consolidated tooling, fewer part numbers and reduced body-line variant complexity.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Common pad set across wheelbases consolidates die-cut tooling and part numbers.

ideaData.riskNotes: A shared map may be sub-optimal for one wheelbase's modes, risking slight NVH compromise or added mass; mitigated by superset placement at common anti-nodes. Damping loss-factor validated on both bodies; pad fit at structural seams checked. Lock-in of layout for programme life.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Li Auto floor-damping commonisation, 2023; benchmarked

title: Full floor acoustic package consolidation to single moulding
description: Consolidate the carpet, decoupler foam, and heavy-layer of the full floor acoustic package into a single moulded recycled-PET fibre stack with graded density, eliminating two separate layers and their assembly. Removes 1.3kg and £5.8/vehicle while meeting reverberation-time targets. Mono-material aids recycling. Benchmarked to BYD integrated floor acoustic module.

ideaData.title: Full floor acoustic package consolidation to single moulding
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline full-floor acoustic package is three layers: carpet, decoupler foam, and a heavy-layer mass barrier - separately assembled. The change consolidates all three into a single moulded recycled-PET fibre stack with graded density (a soft decoupler zone and a denser surface zone moulded in one process), eliminating two separate layers and their assembly. This removes 1.3 kg and £5.8/vehicle while meeting reverberation-time/absorption targets through the graded density. Mono-material PET aids recycling versus the mixed bitumen/EVA/foam baseline. The graded-density fibre stack provides both decoupling and absorption in one moulded part, replacing the classic mass-spring layered build.
ideaData.materialGrade: Graded-density recycled-PET fibre stack (mono-material)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Moulds a graded-density rPET fibre stack combining carpet, decoupler and barrier function, deleting two layers and their assembly. -1.3 kg; one part replaces three. Mono-fibre offcuts regrindable. Removes heavy-layer place and tape. Tuned by density gradient in the mould. Net result is one graded-density part replacing three floor layers, removed heavy-layer place and tape, lower mass and a mono-stream recyclable build.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Graded-density rPET stack replaces three floor layers, cutting assembly and 1.3 kg.

ideaData.riskNotes: Fibre stack provides less low-frequency mass-law TL than a dense heavy-layer; mitigated by local densification and graded structure, accepting EV's lower noise floor. Reverberation-time and road-noise validated; moulded cut-out tolerance and delamination under heat checked. Acoustic sign-off vs baseline required.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD integrated floor acoustic module, 2023; benchmarked

title: Clip-in tunable Helmholtz absorber for body cavities
description: Add lightweight clip-in Helmholtz resonator absorbers tuned to body-cavity boom modes, replacing mass-loaded foam dampers. The moulded PP resonator targets the 120–180Hz drumming band with minimal mass, saving £1.9/vehicle and 0.4kg versus broadband foam. Benchmarked to AVATR Helmholtz cavity tuning.

ideaData.title: Clip-in tunable Helmholtz absorber for body cavities
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline body-cavity boom is controlled by mass-loaded foam dampers (broadband mass added to cavities). The change adds lightweight clip-in Helmholtz resonator absorbers tuned to specific body-cavity boom modes (e.g. 120-180 Hz drumming), replacing the broadband foam. A moulded PP Helmholtz resonator (a cavity with a tuned neck) targets the boom band with minimal mass, saving £1.9/vehicle and 0.4 kg versus broadband foam. The resonator's neck length and cavity volume are tuned to the cavity's modal frequency, giving high attenuation at the problem band for very little mass - a targeted alternative to mass-loading the structure broadband.
ideaData.materialGrade: Moulded PP Helmholtz resonator
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Clip-in moulded PP Helmholtz resonators replace mass-loaded foam dampers in cavities. Tuned by neck/volume to the boom mode; very low mass (-0.4 kg). Injection-moulded, clips to body studs. Removes foam-damper material and placement. Resonator tuning set in design. Net result is low-mass clip-in resonators replacing broadband foam, removed damper material and tuned boom attenuation set entirely in design.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Tuned Helmholtz resonators target boom modes at far lower mass than broadband foam.

ideaData.riskNotes: Narrow-band tuning risks missing the mode if vehicle build/load shifts the frequency; mitigated by multi-resonator or slightly broadened necks and modal validation. Resonator clip retention, BSR and cavity packaging validated. Targets boom only - broadband noise still managed elsewhere.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: AVATR Helmholtz cavity tuning, 2023; benchmarked

title: Robot-applied dash-insulator decoupler foam-in-place
description: Replace the moulded decoupler foam layer of the dash insulator with robot-dispensed foam-in-place applied directly to the firewall contour, eliminating the separate moulded part and its fit gaps. Improves sealing against pass-through leakage and saves £2.8/vehicle in scrap and inventory. Benchmarked to Maextro foam-in-place dash decoupler.

ideaData.title: Robot-applied dash-insulator decoupler foam-in-place
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline dash-insulator decoupler is a separately moulded foam layer fitted to the firewall (with fit gaps and pass-through leaks). The change is robot-dispensed foam-in-place (FIP): liquid PUR decoupler foam is applied directly to the firewall contour and rises to fill, eliminating the separate moulded part and its fit gaps. This improves sealing against pass-through (harness/pedal grommet) leakage and saves £2.8/vehicle in scrap and inventory. Robot dispensing follows the exact firewall geometry, giving conformal contact and consistent decoupling. Removing the moulded part removes its tool, inventory and the gaps that leak airborne noise into the cabin.
ideaData.materialGrade: Foam-in-place PUR decoupler
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Robot dispenses PUR decoupler foam directly onto the firewall, deleting the moulded decoupler part, its tool and inventory. Conformal fill seals pass-throughs. Adds a FIP robot cell and cure; removes part fit/place. Improves sealing and reduces scrap. Isocyanate handling required.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Foam-in-place decoupler removes the moulded part and seals pass-through leaks.

ideaData.riskNotes: Foam thickness/coverage control on a vertical firewall and overspray onto adjacent parts are risks; mitigated by robot path and masking. Pass-through sealing and acoustic decoupling validated; PUR cure, adhesion and HSE (isocyanate) managed. Repair/rework of in-place foam is harder than a part swap.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Maextro foam-in-place dash decoupler, 2024; benchmarked

title: Mono-material PET dash-insulator absorber for recycling
description: Replace the mixed PU-foam-plus-PVC heavy-layer dash absorber with a mono-material recycled-PET fibre absorber, recyclable as one stream and free of PVC. Eliminates material separation and the plasticiser-fogging risk, saving £2.4/vehicle while meeting transmission-loss targets via graded fibre density. Benchmarked to Hongqi mono-material NVH absorbers.

ideaData.title: Mono-material PET dash-insulator absorber for recycling
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline dash absorber is a mixed PU-foam decoupler plus a PVC heavy-layer barrier - incompatible materials with plasticiser-fogging risk. The change is a mono-material recycled-PET (rPET) fibre absorber, recyclable as one polyester stream and free of PVC. It eliminates material separation and the plasticiser-fogging risk while meeting transmission-loss targets via graded fibre density (a denser barrier-like zone plus a lofted decoupler zone in one needled stack). This saves £2.4/vehicle. The graded-density rPET fibre provides both absorption and partial barrier function, replacing the PU+PVC build with a single recyclable fibre part and removing the PVC chemistry.
ideaData.materialGrade: Mono-material recycled-PET fibre (graded density)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Needled/moulded graded-density rPET fibre absorber replaces PU foam + PVC heavy-layer, deleting material separation and PVC. One mono-fibre part; offcuts regrindable. Removes plasticiser-fogging risk. Tuned by density gradient. Drop-in to the dash insulator stack; simpler sourcing. Net result is one mono-fibre part replacing a PU-plus-PVC build, removed material separation and fogging risk, with simpler single-family sourcing.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Mono-PET fibre absorber replaces PU+PVC, removing separation and fogging risk.

ideaData.riskNotes: Fibre stack gives less mass-law TL than a PVC heavy-layer; mitigated by graded density and local mass where needed, leveraging EV's lower noise floor. Transmission-loss and fogging validated; flammability (FMVSS-302) and moisture/odour checked. rPET supply/colour consistency managed.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Hongqi mono-material NVH absorbers, 2023; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS-302 flammability

title: Mould-in-colour PP-TD20 front bumper fascia delete paint shop
description: Replace painted ASA-capped fascia with mould-in-colour PP-TD20 (20% talc) in a self-coloured grain, deleting two-coat clear-coat priming. Eliminates ~£9.3/vehicle paint, masking and rework plus 0.9kg VOC abatement load, and removes one offline buffing station. UV-stabilised pigment masterbatch holds ΔE<1.2 over 3000h QUV. Cuts bumper cost ~9% across 220k units. Benchmarks BYD's mould-in-colour bumper strategy on Dolphin and Atto 3.

ideaData.title: Mould-in-colour PP-TD20 front bumper fascia delete paint shop
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline is an ASA-capped PP-EPDM fascia receiving a two-coat (primer + colour/clear) waterborne paint cycle. The change moulds the fascia from PP-TD20 (20% talc-filled polypropylene, ~1.6 GPa flex modulus) compounded with a UV-stabilised pigment masterbatch in a self-coloured fine grain, eliminating paint entirely. Colour is achieved in-tool via a textured cavity; HALS/UV package holds DeltaE<1.2 over 3000h QUV. Deleting the two-coat line removes clear-coat consumables, masking, oven energy and one offline buffing station, plus the VOC abatement load. Tight melt-temperature and pack-pressure control prevents flow-line streaking on Class-A grained surfaces.
ideaData.materialGrade: PP-TD20 (20% talc-filled polypropylene), MIC grade
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes both paint coats, masking, buffing and VOC abatement for the fascia; one offline buffing station removed. Injection cycle gains a grained Class-A cavity and tighter melt/pack control to avoid flow lines. No paint flash-off ovens; reduced rework loop. Pigment masterbatch dosing added at the press.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Deleting the two-coat paint operation removes per-vehicle paint, masking, rework and abatement cost.

ideaData.riskNotes: MIC grained surfaces show flow lines and weld-line read-through if pack pressure or melt temp drift; mitigate with sequential-valve gating and DOE on grain depth. Long-term gloss/colour retention validated by 3000h QUV and Florida exposure. Scratch visibility higher than painted; mar-resistant masterbatch and deeper grain mitigate.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD Dolphin / Atto 3 mould-in-colour bumper, 2022; confirmed in teardown
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN ECE R42 low-speed bumper; VOC limits per EU Directive 2004/42/EC (paint delete reduces compliance load)

title: Thermoplastic SMC-free composite tailgate vs stamped steel
description: Convert two-piece stamped-steel liftgate to a moulded LFT-PP inner with PC/PBT outer, consolidating 6 stampings and 14 welds into 2 mouldings bonded by PUR adhesive. Saves 5.8kg per vehicle and removes spot-weld and e-coat steps, cutting ~£15/vehicle. Integrated spoiler and wiring bosses delete 4 brackets. Eliminates corrosion sealer. Benchmarks Volvo's composite tailgate on the EX30 platform.

ideaData.title: Thermoplastic SMC-free composite tailgate vs stamped steel
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline is a two-piece stamped-steel liftgate (inner + outer) joined by ~14 spot welds, hemmed, e-coated and sealed against corrosion. The change moulds an LFT-PP (long-glass-fibre PP, ~40% glass, ~6 GPa modulus) structural inner and a PC/PBT outer skin bonded with a two-component PUR adhesive, consolidating 6 stampings and 14 welds into 2 mouldings. Glass fibre carries gas-strut and hinge loads; integral bosses mould-in the spoiler and wiring routing, deleting four brackets. Removing steel deletes spot-weld, hemming, e-coat masking and corrosion sealer, and cuts 5.8 kg. Bond-line geometry and adhesive cure are dimensionally controlled in a fixture to hold gap/flush.
ideaData.materialGrade: LFT-PP-GF40 inner / PC+PBT outer, PUR-bonded
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces 6 stampings + 14 spot welds with 2 mouldings bonded in a cure fixture; deletes hemming, e-coat masking and corrosion sealer for the liftgate. Adds adhesive dispense and fixture cure time. Eliminates dedicated liftgate stamping dies and weld cell; spoiler/wiring bosses moulded-in delete 4 brackets.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Part and weld consolidation plus paint/corrosion-process deletion drives BOM and process cost down.

ideaData.riskNotes: Thermoplastic liftgate dimensional stability over -40 to +90C and bond-line creep under strut load are key risks; validate with hot-creep, slam-durability (>=100k cycles) and adhesive lap-shear ageing. Class-A on PC/PBT outer needs careful sink/warp control. Online vs offline paint compatibility verified.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo EX30 composite tailgate, 2023; benchmarked program
ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN ECE R26 external projections; slam durability per OEM spec

title: PVD-coated bright trim replacing hexavalent chrome plating
description: Switch window-line and grille bright trim from electroplated chrome to PVD-sputtered tin/SST on PC/ABS, removing hexavalent-chrome line, effluent treatment and REACH authorisation cost. PVD cuts process cost ~£3.6/part and energy 35% while passing 480h CASS corrosion. Dark-satin and gun-metal finishes need no extra tooling. Benchmarks Audi's chrome-delete satin trim strategy on Q6 e-tron.

ideaData.title: PVD-coated bright trim replacing hexavalent chrome plating
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline bright trim is PC/ABS electroplated with a Cu/Ni/Cr stack using hexavalent-chrome baths requiring REACH authorisation, effluent treatment and a dedicated plating line. The change uses PVD (physical vapour deposition) sputtering of tin or stainless-steel-look (SST) layers over a base-coat on PC/ABS, delivering bright, dark-satin or gun-metal finishes. PVD deletes the hex-chrome line, Cr(VI) effluent treatment and REACH compliance cost, cuts process energy ~35%, and passes 480h CASS corrosion. A UV base-coat and top-coat sandwich the thin metallised PVD layer for adhesion and abrasion. Same injection tool serves all finishes; only chamber recipe changes.
ideaData.materialGrade: PC/ABS substrate, PVD Sn/SST metallisation (Cr(VI)-free)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Removes the electroplating line, Cr(VI) effluent treatment and REACH authorisation overhead; adds PVD batch chambers plus UV base/top-coat. ~35% lower process energy. Finish variants are recipe changes, not new tooling. Lower scrap from plating throwing-power issues on complex geometry.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: PVD replaces costly, regulated hex-chrome plating with lower-energy dry metallisation.

ideaData.riskNotes: PVD adhesion and abrasion depend on base/top-coat cure; validate cross-hatch, CASS 480h, thermal-shock and humidity.
