The change replaces it with a flat laminated ribbon spine carrying power and LIN to zonal taps, deleting tape wrap, conduit and ~30% of connector cavities while enabling automated lay-flat assembly. The flat spine is a planar laminate of conductors that robots can place and tap into at zonal nodes, versus hand-routing a 3D bundle. Cuts ~£25/veh and ~6 kg. Standardised tap points reduce connector variety; the laminate's defined geometry improves repeatability and enables full assembly automation, a major labour saver versus manual harness build.
ideaData.materialGrade: Flat laminated ribbon spine (Cu), zonal taps
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces the branched round harness with a flat laminated spine; deletes tape wrap, conduit and ~30% of connector cavities (~6 kg). Enables automated lay-flat/robotic assembly versus manual routing, a major labour reduction. Standard zonal taps cut connector variety. New flat-harness manufacturing line.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: A flat ribbon spine deletes wrap/conduit, cuts connectors and enables automated assembly.

ideaData.riskNotes: Flat-spine flexibility for 3D body routing and crash/abrasion protection without conduit must be validated; thermal de-rating of laminated conductors checked. Major manufacturing change with automation NRE and supplier-capability risk. Repairability and field service of a laminate differ from round harness.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Luxeed R7 flat-harness spine, 2023; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: ISO 6722 conductor/insulation; ISO 16750 abrasion/environmental without conduit

title: Integrated coolant module octovalve replacing discrete valves
description: Consolidate eight three-way valves, two pumps and manifold hoses into one moulded octovalve coolant module with integrated pump and sensors, deleting ~14 hose joints, 9 clamps and a wiring branch. Saves ~£29/vehicle and 1.8kg, cutting leak paths and assembly time 90s. Benchmarks Tesla-style superbottle realised in Zeekr's integrated thermal module.

ideaData.title: Integrated coolant module octovalve replacing discrete valves
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline thermal plumbing uses eight discrete three-way coolant valves, two pumps and a maze of manifold hoses with many joints. The change consolidates them into one moulded octovalve coolant module with integrated pump(s) and sensors, deleting ~14 hose joints, 9 clamps and a wiring branch. A single rotary multi-port valve body routes coolant between battery, cabin, drive-unit and radiator circuits under one actuator/controller, mirroring the Tesla superbottle/Octovalve concept. Saves ~£29/veh and ~1.8 kg, cutting leak paths and assembly time ~90 s. Integrated sensors and one connector replace distributed valves; the moulded PA-GF manifold replaces hand-routed hoses, reducing joints and warranty leak risk.
ideaData.materialGrade: Moulded octovalve coolant module (PA-GF) with integrated pump
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates 8 valves + 2 pumps + manifold hoses into one octovalve module; deletes ~14 hose joints, 9 clamps and a wiring branch. A single connector and actuator replace many. ~90 s line time and ~1.8 kg removed; far fewer leak paths cut EOL leak-test failures. A moulded PA-GF manifold replaces hose routing.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: An integrated octovalve module replaces eight valves, pumps and hoses, cutting joints and leaks.

ideaData.riskNotes: A single multi-port valve concentrates function, so internal leakage between ports, actuator reliability and sensor accuracy must be validated; failure isolates more circuits. Moulded-manifold pressure/burst and thermal-cycle creep tested. Supplier capability and dual-source for a complex integrated module.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Tesla-style superbottle realised in Zeekr integrated thermal module, 2023; benchmarked

title: PTC cabin heater delete via heat-pump-only thermal loop
description: Remove the 7kW high-voltage PTC heater by extending heat-pump operation to -25C with vapour-injection and waste-heat recovery, deleting the PTC element, its HV connector and a coolant branch. Saves ~£22/vehicle and reclaims range. COP above 1.8 maintained. Benchmarks BYD's PTC-delete heat-pump strategy on Seal.

ideaData.title: PTC cabin heater delete via heat-pump-only thermal loop
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline cabin heating uses a 7 kW high-voltage PTC heater plus a heat pump for mild conditions. The change deletes the PTC by extending heat-pump operation down to -25C using vapour-injection (economised) scroll compression and waste-heat recovery from the drive unit and battery, removing the PTC element, its HV connector and a coolant branch. Saves ~£22/veh and reclaims winter range (heat-pump COP >1.8 maintained vs PTC's COP~1). The vapour-injection compressor and an enhanced low-temp evaporator extend the operating envelope; recovered drive/battery waste heat supplements capacity. With the heat pump covering the full envelope, the resistive PTC becomes redundant.
ideaData.materialGrade: Vapour-injection heat pump (R1234yf), waste-heat recovery; PTC deleted
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes the 7 kW HV PTC element, its HV connector and a coolant branch. Adds vapour-injection compressor capability and waste-heat plumbing/valves. Fewer HV components simplify the high-voltage assembly and reduce one HV interlock point. Net mass and HV-harness reduction.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Extending the heat pump to -25C deletes the resistive PTC heater and its HV branch.

ideaData.riskNotes: Cold-soak heat-up time and capacity at -25C must meet defrost/demist (FMVSS 103 / ECE) and comfort targets without PTC backup, so validate worst-case and consider a small reserve. Vapour-injection compressor reliability and refrigerant migration at low temp verified. Customer perception of slower warm-up managed.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: BYD Seal PTC-delete heat-pump strategy, 2022; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: FMVSS 103 / UN ECE defrost-demist performance must be met without PTC backup

title: R290 propane refrigerant replacing R1234yf charge
description: Switch the heat-pump refrigerant from R1234yf to low-cost R290 propane, cutting per-charge refrigerant cost ~£6/vehicle and improving low-ambient COP. Hermetic secondary-loop architecture contains flammability per safety standard. Lower GWP eases compliance. Benchmarks Volvo's propane heat-pump development on SPA2.

ideaData.title: R290 propane refrigerant replacing R1234yf charge
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline heat-pump refrigerant is R1234yf (a low-GWP HFO, but costly and supply-constrained). The change switches to R290 (propane), a low-cost natural refrigerant with excellent low-ambient thermodynamic performance, cutting per-charge refrigerant cost ~£6/veh and improving low-temperature COP. Because R290 is A3 (flammable), a hermetic secondary-loop architecture confines the refrigerant to an under-hood module away from the cabin, with charge minimisation and leak detection per the relevant safety standard. Lower GWP (~3) eases regulatory compliance versus HFOs. The secondary loop transfers heat to/from cabin and battery via coolant, isolating the flammable charge.
ideaData.materialGrade: R290 (propane) refrigerant, hermetic secondary-loop
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Switches the refrigerant charge to R290 with a sealed secondary-loop module; charge minimised and contained under-hood. Adds leak detection and a hermetic module but lowers refrigerant cost per fill. Secondary-loop heat exchangers replace direct cabin refrigerant lines. Service handling changes for flammable refrigerant.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: R290 propane replaces costly R1234yf charge, cutting refrigerant cost and improving low-temp COP.

ideaData.riskNotes: R290 flammability (A3) demands charge minimisation, leak detection, ignition-source control and a hermetic secondary loop, so validate to IEC 60335/EC 706 and ECE safety. The secondary loop adds a heat-exchange penalty (lower system COP vs direct). Service-network training and regulations for flammable refrigerant required.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Volvo SPA2 propane heat-pump development, 2023; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: SAE J639 / EU F-gas (MAC Directive 2006/40/EC) and flammable-refrigerant safety (charge limits, leak detection)

title: Single-fluid loop unifying battery and cabin coolant circuits
description: Merge separate battery glycol and cabin chiller loops into one shared coolant circuit with a chiller plate interface, deleting one pump, one expansion tank and ~3m of hose. Saves ~£19/vehicle and 1.4kg, simplifying fill and bleed to a single service point. Benchmarks Tesla-style unified loop adopted in Li Auto's thermal design on L7.

ideaData.title: Single-fluid loop unifying battery and cabin coolant circuits
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline thermal has separate battery glycol and cabin chiller coolant loops, each with its own pump and expansion tank. The change merges them into one shared coolant circuit interfaced through a chiller plate, deleting one pump, one expansion tank and ~3 m of hose. Valve sequencing routes the single coolant fluid between battery and cabin chiller duties as needed. Saves ~£19/veh and ~1.4 kg, and reduces fill/bleed to a single service point. Unifying the loop (Tesla-style) lets one pump and reservoir serve both, with the chiller-plate interface managing heat exchange to the refrigerant side. Fewer components and a single fill simplify both build and service.
ideaData.materialGrade: Unified single-fluid coolant loop, chiller-plate interface
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Merges two coolant loops into one; deletes one pump, one expansion tank and ~3 m hose (~1.4 kg). A single fill/bleed point cuts EOL fill time and service complexity. Valve sequencing replaces a second loop. Fewer hoses and joints reduce leak paths and assembly time.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Unifying battery and cabin coolant into one loop deletes a pump, tank and hoses.

ideaData.riskNotes: The shared loop must meet both battery and cabin flow/temperature targets simultaneously, so validate worst-case heat-reject/heat-up coincidence and valve sequencing. The single pump is a single point of failure for battery cooling; a redundancy/limp strategy is defined. Cross-contamination of thermal duties managed by control.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Tesla-style unified loop adopted in Li Auto L7 thermal design, 2022; benchmarked

title: Electric coolant pump downsizing via flow-optimised plumbing
description: Replace two 25W coolant pumps with one 30W pump after reducing hose bends and restrictions, cutting pump count, one LIN node and 20W parasitic draw. Saves ~£5.1/vehicle and a connector. Lower head loss meets battery flow target. Benchmarks Porsche's optimised single-pump battery loop on Taycan facelift.

ideaData.title: Electric coolant pump downsizing via flow-optimised plumbing
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline battery loop uses two 25 W electric coolant pumps. The change reduces hose bends and flow restrictions (flow-optimised plumbing) so a single 30 W pump meets the battery flow target, cutting pump count, one LIN node and ~20 W parasitic draw. Lower head loss from straighter routing means one slightly larger pump replaces two smaller ones at lower total power. Saves ~£5.1/veh and a connector. The plumbing redesign (fewer elbows, larger radii, smoother ports) drops system pressure drop so the duty point shifts to one pump. Fewer pumps and one LIN node simplify control and reduce parasitic energy, modestly improving range.
ideaData.materialGrade: Single 30 W electric coolant pump (flow-optimised loop)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces two 25 W pumps with one 30 W pump after reducing hose bends/restrictions; deletes one pump, one LIN node and a connector. ~20 W less parasitic draw. Fewer components ease assembly; revised hose routing for lower head loss. One pump variant simplifies service.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Flow-optimised plumbing lets one pump replace two, cutting parasitic power and a node.

ideaData.riskNotes: The single pump must hold battery flow/temperature under worst-case fast-charge heat reject, so validate the duty point and add a limp/derate strategy as it is now a single point of failure. Revised-plumbing pressure-drop verified across temperature (cold glycol viscosity). NVH of the larger pump checked.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Porsche Taycan facelift optimised single-pump battery loop, 2024; benchmarked

title: Plastic-tank aluminium radiator to all-aluminium brazed core
description: Replace mechanically-crimped plastic-tank/aluminium radiator with a fully brazed all-aluminium core, deleting the nylon tank, gasket and crimp operation while cutting a long-term seep failure mode. Saves ~£2.9/vehicle and 240g and recovers material at end of life. Benchmarks Audi's brazed low-temp radiator on e-tron.

ideaData.title: Plastic-tank aluminium radiator to all-aluminium brazed core
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline radiator is an aluminium core with mechanically-crimped nylon plastic tanks sealed by an elastomer gasket. The change is a fully brazed all-aluminium core (CAB-brazed tanks integral to the core), deleting the nylon tank, gasket and the crimp operation while removing a long-term gasket-seep failure mode. Controlled-atmosphere brazing fuses tanks and core into one monolithic aluminium part. Saves ~£2.9/veh and ~240 g and improves end-of-life material recovery (single-material aluminium). Eliminating the crimp/gasket joint removes a recurring coolant-seep warranty driver; brazed joints are more robust to thermal cycling and pressure than crimped plastic tanks.
ideaData.materialGrade: All-aluminium CAB-brazed radiator core (AA3xxx/4xxx clad)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces the crimped plastic-tank/gasket assembly with a fully brazed all-aluminium core; deletes the crimp operation, nylon tank and gasket. ~240 g lighter, single-material for recycling. A brazing-furnace process replaces mechanical assembly; removes a seep warranty mode. Joint robustness to thermal cycling improved.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: A fully brazed aluminium core deletes the plastic tank, gasket, crimp step and a seep failure mode.

ideaData.riskNotes: Brazed all-aluminium tanks are stiffer (less compliant) than plastic, so validate thermal-fatigue and pressure-cycle life and connection-port stress. Brazing flux residue and corrosion (SWAAT) controlled. Higher braze-process cost offset by deleted crimp/gasket; furnace capacity verified.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Audi e-tron brazed low-temp radiator, 2022; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: ELV single-material recyclability; coolant-system pressure per OEM/SAE J1244

title: Chiller and EXV integration into coolant valve block
description: Integrate the battery chiller plate-heat-exchanger and its electronic expansion valve directly into the coolant valve block, deleting two refrigerant joints, a bracket and a mounting hose. Saves ~£11/vehicle and a refrigerant leak path, trimming assembly 40s. Benchmarks Denza's integrated chiller block on N7.

ideaData.title: Chiller and EXV integration into coolant valve block
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline battery cooling uses a separate plate-heat-exchanger chiller and a discrete electronic expansion valve (EXV) connected by refrigerant lines, brackets and hoses. The change integrates the chiller plate-heat-exchanger and its EXV directly into the coolant valve block, deleting two refrigerant joints, a bracket and a mounting hose. Co-locating the chiller and EXV in the valve block removes refrigerant connections (a leak path) and shortens refrigerant routing. Saves ~£11/veh and trims assembly ~40 s. The integrated block combines coolant valving, the chiller interface and refrigerant metering in one machined/moulded body, reducing joints, leak risk and part count in the thermal sub-assembly.
ideaData.materialGrade: Integrated coolant valve block with chiller PHE + EXV
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Integrates the chiller PHE and EXV into the coolant valve block; deletes 2 refrigerant joints, a bracket and a mounting hose. ~40 s assembly saved and a refrigerant leak path removed. One integrated body replaces several mounted components; fewer brazed/flared joints to leak-test.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Integrating the chiller and EXV into the valve block deletes refrigerant joints and a leak path.

ideaData.riskNotes: Integrating refrigerant (high-pressure) and coolant in one block needs careful sealing and thermal isolation, so validate burst, internal leak and EXV control accuracy. Serviceability reduces (the block is replaced as a unit). Supplier integration capability and dual-source considered.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Denza N7 integrated chiller block, 2023; benchmarked

title: Three-way coolant valve actuator from BLDC to stepper LIN
description: Replace BLDC-driven coolant diverter valves with cost-down stepper-actuated LIN valves carrying integral position feedback, deleting the discrete driver IC and a sensor per valve. Saves ~£2.6/valve across four valves and standardises to one LIN node type. Benchmarks Hongqi's LIN coolant-valve standardisation on EHS7.

ideaData.title: Three-way coolant valve actuator from BLDC to stepper LIN
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline three-way coolant diverter valves use BLDC actuators, each needing a discrete driver IC and a position sensor. The change replaces them with cost-down stepper-actuated LIN valves carrying integral position feedback, deleting the discrete driver IC and sensor per valve and standardising to one LIN node type. The stepper holds position open-loop with feedback for fault detection, and LIN provides command/diagnostics on two wires. Saves ~£2.6/valve across four valves. Standardising on one LIN valve type cuts part variety and harness complexity. The simpler stepper drive is adequate for the slow positioning duty of coolant diverter valves, removing BLDC drive electronics.
ideaData.materialGrade: Stepper-actuated LIN coolant valve (integral feedback)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Replaces the BLDC valves' discrete driver IC and sensor with integrated stepper+LIN valves; one node type across four valves. Deletes per-valve driver silicon and sensor. Two-wire LIN simplifies harness. The commonised valve part reduces SKUs and EOL calibration.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Stepper LIN valves delete BLDC driver ICs/sensors and standardise to one node type.

ideaData.riskNotes: Stepper open-loop positioning must tolerate coolant viscosity/temperature and avoid step loss, so validate ice-break/stall torque and position accuracy with feedback. LIN node count and bus timing on the thermal segment verified. Single valve-type fit across all four positions confirmed.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Hongqi EHS7 LIN coolant-valve standardisation, 2022; benchmarked

title: Full thermal loop integration into front cooling super-module
description: Consolidate the complete front thermal loop, radiators, condenser, compressor mounts, valve block and pumps, into one pre-charged cooling super-module fitted as a single unit, deleting ~24 hose connections and shifting plumbing labour off-line. Saves ~£32/vehicle and 130s line time plus leak-test simplification. Benchmarks AVATR's integrated front thermal module on 12.

ideaData.title: Full thermal loop integration into front cooling super-module
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline front thermal hardware (radiators, condenser, compressor mounts, valve block, pumps and their hoses) is assembled piecemeal on the main line with ~24 hose connections. The change consolidates the complete front thermal loop into one pre-charged cooling super-module fitted as a single unit, deleting ~24 hose connections from the line and shifting plumbing labour off-line to the supplier. Saves ~£32/veh and ~130 s line time plus leak-test simplification (the module is leak-tested once, off-line). The super-module arrives charged and tested, fitting via a few quick-connects, versus building the loop hose-by-hose at the station. This is the front-end thermal analogue of modular assembly.
ideaData.materialGrade: Pre-charged front cooling super-module (integrated loop)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Consolidates radiators, condenser, compressor mounts, valve block and pumps into one pre-charged module; deletes ~24 line-side hose connections and ~130 s line time. Plumbing/charge/leak-test moves off-line to the supplier. The module fits via quick-connects; one off-line leak-test replaces many in-line joints.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: A pre-charged front thermal super-module removes ~24 line connections and shifts labour off-line.

ideaData.riskNotes: Pre-charged module logistics (refrigerant transport, charge retention, handling mass) and a few critical quick-connects becoming the only line joints raise per-joint criticality, so validate quick-connect reliability and module shipping. Supplier capability, dual-source and module dimensional fit to BIW are key.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: AVATR 12 integrated front thermal module, 2023; benchmarked
ideaData.regulatoryContext: SAE J639 refrigerant handling for pre-charged module transport and service

title: Heat-pump compressor sharing across cabin and battery duty
description: Use one variable-speed scroll compressor time-shared between cabin AC and battery chilling via valve sequencing instead of dual compressors, deleting one compressor, its HV inverter and mount. Saves ~£36/vehicle and 4kg while cutting one HV connector. Software arbitrates priority. Benchmarks Maextro's single-compressor thermal architecture on S800.

ideaData.title: Heat-pump compressor sharing across cabin and battery duty
ideaData.technicalDescription: Baseline uses two compressors, one for cabin AC and one for battery chilling. The change uses a single variable-speed scroll compressor time-shared between cabin AC and battery chilling via refrigerant valve sequencing instead of dual compressors, deleting one compressor, its HV inverter and mount. Saves ~£36/veh and ~4 kg while cutting one HV connector. Software arbitrates priority (cabin comfort vs battery thermal/fast-charge) and the variable-speed compressor modulates capacity across both duties. A multi-port valve directs refrigerant to the cabin evaporator or battery chiller as demanded. One larger, modulating compressor covers the combined load envelope, removing duplicate HV hardware and its packaging.
ideaData.materialGrade: Single variable-speed HV scroll compressor (shared duty)
ideaData.manufacturingImpact: Deletes one compressor, its HV inverter, mount and an HV connector (~4 kg). Adds valve sequencing and control to share one variable-speed compressor across cabin/battery. Simpler HV assembly with one inverter; software arbitrates duty. Fewer HV interlock points and refrigerant lines.

costSavingPotential.qualitative: Time-sharing one variable-speed compressor across cabin and battery deletes a second compressor and inverter.

ideaData.riskNotes: The single compressor must meet coincident peak (hot-ambient cabin pull-down during fast-charge battery cooling), so validate worst-case capacity and arbitration so neither duty starves; it is a single point of failure for both. Valve-sequencing transients, oil return and NVH at variable speed verified.
ideaData.benchmarkReference: Maextro S800 single-compressor thermal architecture, 2024; benchmarked

evidenceSources.title: Single vs dual compressor EV thermal

ideaData.regulatoryContext: UN ECE R100 HV safety for compressor/inverter; SAE J639 refrigerant system
